devotionals.scripture: And at the end of your life you groan, when your flesh and body are consumed... — Proverbs 5:11
devotionals.title: Regret in the End
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, help me to live today with my final day in mind.

devotionals.reflection: The most painful regret is knowing you had the truth but chose to ignore it. A hard heart is a dangerous place to live.
devotionals.question: Is there a specific piece of godly advice I am currently "hating" or ignoring?
devotionals.scripture: How I hated discipline, and my heart despised reproof! — Proverbs 5:12
devotionals.title: The Danger of "I Knew Better"
devotionals.prayer: Lord, soften my heart. Don't let my pride keep me from the correction I need to grow.

devotionals.question: How can I cultivate a deeper sense of gratitude for the blessings already in my "house"?
devotionals.reflection: Contentment is found in appreciating what God has already given us. Whether in marriage or career, look for joy in your own "well" rather than someone else's.
devotionals.scripture: Drink water from your own cistern, flowing water from your own well. — Proverbs 5:15
devotionals.title: Finding Joy at Home
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for Your specific provision for me. Help me to be fully present and satisfied in my own life.

devotionals.question: Who is a person in my life I have begun to take for granted?
devotionals.reflection: We often pine for the "new" or the "next." Wisdom calls us to find fresh joy in long-term commitments and the people who have walked with us.
devotionals.scripture: Let your fountain be blessed, and rejoice in the wife of your youth... — Proverbs 5:18
devotionals.title: Rejoice in Your Season
devotionals.prayer: Lord, bless my relationships. Refresh my love for those You have placed by my side.

devotionals.reflection: We might hide our choices from others, but God sees every step. He doesn't just see the action; He "ponders" the motive behind it.
devotionals.question: Does the reality of God's constant presence bring me comfort or conviction today?
devotionals.scripture: For a man's ways are before the eyes of the Lord, and he ponders all his paths. — Proverbs 5:21
devotionals.title: Fully Known
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for seeing me. Keep me honest in the secret places of my heart.

devotionals.question: Is there a "habit" in my life that has started to feel like a cord tightening around me?
devotionals.reflection: Sin promises freedom but delivers "cords." What starts as a choice eventually becomes a habit that is difficult to break without God's help.
devotionals.scripture: The iniquities of the wicked ensnare him, and he is held fast in the cords of his sin. — Proverbs 5:22
devotionals.title: Ensnared by Sin
devotionals.prayer: Lord, break the chains of my sin. Set me free to walk in the liberty of Your Spirit.

devotionals.question: Have I made any financial commitments that are robbing me of peace or freedom?
devotionals.reflection: Wisdom extends to our finances. Be careful about making promises or taking on debts that bind your future and stress your spirit.
devotionals.scripture: My son, if you have put up security for your neighbor... you are snared in the words of your mouth... — Proverbs 6:1-2
devotionals.title: The Debt Trap
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me financial wisdom. Help me to be a person of my word and a wise steward of my means.

devotionals.reflection: If you've made a mistake or a bad deal, don't let pride keep you there. Humble yourself and do what is necessary to make it right immediately.
devotionals.question: Is there a difficult conversation I’ve been avoiding out of pride?
devotionals.scripture: Go, humble yourself, and plead urgently with your neighbor. — Proverbs 6:3
devotionals.title: Humble Persistence
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me the humility to admit when I've messed up and the courage to fix it.

devotionals.reflection: Nature is a classroom. Even the tiny ant knows the value of hard work and preparation without needing a "boss" to watch over her.
devotionals.question: Am I self-motivated to do my best, or do I only work hard when someone is watching?
devotionals.scripture: Go to the ant, O sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise. — Proverbs 6:6
devotionals.title: The Lesson of the Ant
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me diligent. Help me to work with excellence, as if I am working directly for You.

devotionals.reflection: Wisdom looks ahead. Don't wait for the crisis to arrive before you start building your spiritual and practical reserves.
devotionals.question: What can I do today to prepare for the "winter" seasons of life?
devotionals.scripture: She prepares her bread in summer and gathers her food in harvest. — Proverbs 6:8
devotionals.title: Preparing in Summer
devotionals.prayer: God, give me foresight. Help me to use my time of "plenty" to prepare for the times of "need."

devotionals.question: Where am I letting "a little" laziness keep me from God's best?
devotionals.reflection: Ruin rarely happens all at once; it happens in "littles." A little procrastination here, a little compromise there—it adds up.
devotionals.scripture: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to rest... — Proverbs 6:10
devotionals.title: The Danger of "A Little"
devotionals.prayer: Father, wake me up. Help me to stay alert and active in the work You’ve called me to.

devotionals.reflection: Neglect eventually catches up with us. Wisdom acts with urgency because it knows that the "robber" of consequence is always on the way.
devotionals.question: Am I being passive about an area of my life that requires urgent attention?
devotionals.scripture: ...and poverty will come upon you like a robber, and want like an armed man. — Proverbs 6:11
devotionals.title: Poverty Like a Robber
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me the energy and focus to tackle the tasks I've been neglecting.

devotionals.question: Is my "non-verbal" communication as honest as my words?
devotionals.reflection: Our character is signaled by our communication. Deceptive body language and "winks" often hide a heart of mischief.
devotionals.scripture: A worthless person, a wicked man, goes about with crooked speech... — Proverbs 6:12
devotionals.title: The Worthless Person
devotionals.prayer: God, let my life be transparent and my character be consistent from the inside out.

devotionals.question: Am I a person who brings people together, or do I tend to stir up drama?
devotionals.reflection: One of the most dangerous things we can do is "sow discord"—stirring up trouble between people. Wisdom seeks to be a bridge-builder.
devotionals.scripture: ...who with perverted heart devises evil, continually sowing discord. — Proverbs 6:14
devotionals.title: Sowing Discord
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, make me a peacemaker today. Let my words heal rather than divide.

devotionals.question: Do I take the things that "abominate" God seriously, or do I brush them off?
devotionals.reflection: We must love what God loves, but we must also hate what God hates. Knowing His heart helps us align our own.
devotionals.scripture: There are six things that the Lord hates, seven that are an abomination to him... — Proverbs 6:16
devotionals.title: Things God Hates
devotionals.prayer: Lord, align my heart with Yours. Teach me to value what You value and shun what You hate.

devotionals.question: Where is "haughty" pride creeping into my view of others?
devotionals.reflection: Pride ("haughty eyes") is listed first among the things God hates. It is the root of almost every other sin.
devotionals.scripture: Haughty eyes, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood... — Proverbs 6:17
devotionals.title: Haughty Eyes
devotionals.prayer: Father, clothe me in humility. Remind me that everything I have is a gift from You.

devotionals.reflection: Wisdom is slow and thoughtful; sin is often impulsive and "hasty." When we rush into decisions, we often rush into trouble.
devotionals.question: Am I rushing into a decision or a reaction without praying first?
devotionals.scripture: ...feet that make haste to run to evil... — Proverbs 6:18
devotionals.title: Swift to Evil
devotionals.prayer: Lord, slow me down. Help me to be "quick to hear" but "slow to speak" and slow to act.

devotionals.reflection: God values truth because He IS Truth. A lie is not just a "mistake"; it is a direct contradiction of God’s nature.
devotionals.question: Have I been "breathing out" half-truths or exaggerations lately?
devotionals.scripture: ...a false witness who breathes out lies... — Proverbs 6:19
devotionals.title: A False Witness
devotionals.prayer: Spirit of Truth, guide me into all truth today. Let my "yes" be "yes" and my "no" be "no."

devotionals.question: What scripture can I "wear" today to remind me of God's presence?
devotionals.reflection: We should keep God's commands as close as our favorite jewelry. They shouldn't just be in our heads, but "tied" to our everyday life.
devotionals.scripture: Bind them on your heart always; tie them around your neck. — Proverbs 6:21
devotionals.title: Binding the Word
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let Your Word be the most visible part of my life and character.

devotionals.reflection: Rules aren't meant to restrict us; they are meant to light the way so we don't fall. God's commands are for our safety.
devotionals.question: Do I view God's commands as a "burden" or as a "lamp" for my benefit?
devotionals.scripture: For the commandment is a lamp and the teaching a light... — Proverbs 6:23
devotionals.title: The Lamp of Command
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the light of Your Law. It keeps me from wandering into the dark.

devotionals.question: Am I playing with "fire" in some area of my life and expecting to stay safe?
devotionals.reflection: You cannot dance with sin and expect not to get burned. The laws of spiritual consequences are as real as the laws of physics.
devotionals.scripture: Can a man walk on hot coals and his feet not be scorched? — Proverbs 6:28
devotionals.title: Walking on Coals
devotionals.prayer: Lord, protect me from the deception that I am the "exception" to Your rules.

devotionals.question: Am I honoring my "self" by staying faithful to God's standards?
devotionals.reflection: Sin is ultimately self-destructive. Choosing God's way is the highest form of "self-care" because it preserves your soul and your future.
devotionals.scripture: He who commits adultery lacks sense; he who does it destroys himself. — Proverbs 6:32
devotionals.title: Protecting Your Soul
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, help me to value my soul and my body as temples of Your Holy Spirit.

devotionals.reflection: The "apple of your eye" is the pupil—the most sensitive and protected part. We should protect God's truth with that same level of care.
devotionals.question: How "protected" is my time with God and His Word in my daily schedule?
devotionals.scripture: Keep my commandments and live; keep my teaching as the apple of your eye. — Proverbs 6:2
devotionals.title: Guarding the Pupil
devotionals.prayer: Father, help me to guard Your truth above everything else. Make it the center of my focus today.

devotionals.question: What verse have I meditated on long enough for it to be "written" on my heart?
devotionals.reflection: Knowledge in a book is helpful, but knowledge in the heart is transformative. We must internalize God’s truth until it becomes our first instinct.
devotionals.scripture: Bind them on your fingers; write them on the tablet of your heart. — Proverbs 7:3
devotionals.title: Writing on the Heart
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let Your Word sink deep. Move it from my head to my heart so I may not sin against You.

devotionals.reflection: Wisdom isn't a dry set of rules; it’s a relationship. Treat it with the same love and loyalty you would give to a cherished family member.
devotionals.question: Do I treat God’s wisdom like a stranger I visit occasionally, or like a close friend?
devotionals.scripture: Say to wisdom, 'You are my sister,' and call insight your intimate friend. — Proverbs 7:4
devotionals.title: Wisdom as Kin
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, be my closest companion today. Let Your insight be my constant guide.

devotionals.question: Am I paying attention to the "warning signs" in the lives of those around me?
devotionals.reflection: Sometimes the best way to learn is by watching the mistakes of others. Wisdom observes the world and notices the patterns that lead to ruin.
devotionals.scripture: For at the window of my house... I have perceived among the youths, a young man lacking sense. — Proverbs 7:6-7
devotionals.title: The Window of Observation
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me an observant eye. Help me learn from the choices of others so I don't have to learn the hard way.

devotionals.question: When am I most vulnerable to temptation, and how am I preparing for those "twilight" moments?
devotionals.reflection: Temptation often waits for the "twilight"—those moments when we are tired, lonely, or think no one is watching. Darkness is its favorite camouflage.
devotionals.scripture: ...passing along the street near her corner... in the twilight, in the evening, at the time of night and darkness. — Proverbs 7:8-9
devotionals.title: The Twilight Hour
devotionals.prayer: Lord, be my light in the darkness. Keep me alert even when I am tired or alone.

devotionals.reflection: Outer appearance often masks inner intent. A "wily" heart uses charm and flattery to hide a destructive agenda.
devotionals.question: Am I being dazzled by someone’s outward charm while ignoring their inward character?
devotionals.scripture: And behold, the woman meets him, dressed as a prostitute, wily of heart. — Proverbs 7:10
devotionals.title: The Crafty Heart
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, give me Your eyes to see past the surface and discern the true intent of those I encounter.

devotionals.question: Am I using "good deeds" or church attendance to justify a secret sin I’m holding onto?
devotionals.reflection: This is the height of deception: using "religion" to justify sin. Performing religious rituals doesn't give us a license to disobey God's moral laws.
devotionals.scripture: I had to offer sacrifices, and today I have paid my vows. — Proverbs 7:14
devotionals.title: The Peace Offering
devotionals.prayer: Search me, O God, and know my heart. Let my worship be sincere and my obedience complete.

devotionals.reflection: Words have power. "Smooth talk" can make a wrong path look right. We must weigh every word against the weight of Scripture.
devotionals.question: Whose words am I listening to more than God's Word?
devotionals.scripture: With much seductive speech she persuades him; with her smooth talk she compels him. — Proverbs 7:21
devotionals.title: The Lure of Flattery
devotionals.prayer: Lord, filter the voices I hear today. Let Your Truth be the loudest voice in my life.

devotionals.question: What choice am I making right now that feels harmless but might be leading to "slaughter"?
devotionals.reflection: Sin numbs our sense of danger. We walk into traps as if we are going to a party, unaware of the "arrow" waiting for our heart.
devotionals.scripture: All at once he follows her, as an ox goes to the slaughter... — Proverbs 7:22
devotionals.title: Like an Ox to Slaughter
devotionals.prayer: Father, open my eyes. Don't let me be led blindly into a trap of my own making.

devotionals.reflection: Compromise is a downward slope. It doesn't just lead to a "mistake"; it leads to a spiritual dead end. The stakes are eternal.
devotionals.question: Do I realize that my small compromises are actually steps on a very dangerous road?
devotionals.scripture: Her house is the way to Sheol, going down to the chambers of death. — Proverbs 7:27
devotionals.title: The Chambers of Death
devotionals.prayer: Lord, turn my feet around. Lead me away from death and back onto the path of life.

devotionals.reflection: Wisdom isn't a secret society; it’s a public invitation. God puts His truth in "high places" so everyone has the opportunity to hear.
devotionals.question: Have I been acting like God's will is a mystery, or am I looking at the "high places" where He has spoken?
devotionals.scripture: On the heights beside the way... she takes her stand. — Proverbs 8:2
devotionals.title: Wisdom’s High Places
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for making Your wisdom accessible. Give me the courage to stand for Your truth in public.

devotionals.reflection: God's wisdom is "noble." It elevates us and brings dignity to our lives. It is the opposite of the "cheap" thrills the world offers.
devotionals.question: Does my speech today reflect the "nobility" of a child of God?
devotionals.scripture: Hear, for I will speak noble things, and from my lips will come what is right. — Proverbs 8:6
devotionals.title: Noble Things
devotionals.prayer: Father, let my words be right and my thoughts be noble. May I reflect Your character today.

devotionals.reflection: To love the truth, we must learn to "detest" the lie. Integrity means our lips are physically uncomfortable with deception.
devotionals.question: Do I have a "distaste" for dishonesty, or have I become comfortable with it?
devotionals.scripture: For my mouth will utter truth; wickedness is an abomination to my lips. — Proverbs 8:7
devotionals.title: Detesting Wickedness
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are the Truth. Make me so much like You that I cannot stomach a lie.

devotionals.question: If I lost all my "rubies" but kept my wisdom, would I still feel rich?
devotionals.reflection: We often measure success by what we can "buy." God measures success by what we "become" through His wisdom.
devotionals.scripture: For wisdom is better than jewels, and all that you may desire cannot compare with her. — Proverbs 8:11
devotionals.title: Wisdom vs. Rubies
devotionals.prayer: Lord, help me to value my spiritual growth more than my financial portfolio.

devotionals.question: Where do I need more "prudence" or practical strategy in my life right now?
devotionals.reflection: Wisdom isn't just "holy"; it's practical. It gives us "prudence"—the ability to manage our affairs with skill and foresight.
devotionals.scripture: I, wisdom, dwell with prudence, and I find knowledge and discretion. — Proverbs 8:12
devotionals.title: Prudence and Knowledge
devotionals.prayer: Lord, grant me the discretion I need to manage my time, my money, and my words today.

devotionals.reflection: We cannot fear God and be full of ourselves at the same time. Pride is the direct enemy of the "fear of the Lord."
devotionals.question: Is there any area of my life where I am "arrogant" about my own abilities?
devotionals.scripture: The fear of the Lord is hatred of evil. Pride and arrogance... I hate. — Proverbs 8:13
devotionals.title: Hating Pride
devotionals.prayer: Father, I repent of my pride. Replace my arrogance with a holy awe of who You are.

devotionals.question: Who am I turning to for "sound wisdom" before I make my next big move?
devotionals.reflection: Real strength isn't muscle; it’s insight. A person with godly counsel can accomplish more than a person with just raw power.
devotionals.scripture: I have counsel and sound wisdom; I have insight; I have strength. — Proverbs 8:14
devotionals.title: The Power of Counsel
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I lean on Your counsel. Give me the strength that comes from Your insight.

devotionals.reflection: Any leader who is truly just is drawing from God's wisdom, whether they realize it or not. God is the source of all true justice.
devotionals.question: Am I praying for those in authority to seek God’s "just decrees"?
devotionals.scripture: By me kings reign, and rulers decree what is just. — Proverbs 8:15
devotionals.title: Justice in Rule
devotionals.prayer: Sovereign Lord, guide the leaders of my nation and my community with Your wisdom.

devotionals.reflection: Wisdom is responsive. If you pursue it with a "diligent" heart, it promises to be found. It’s not playing hard to get.
devotionals.question: How "diligently" have I searched for God’s perspective this week?
devotionals.scripture: I love those who love me, and those who seek me diligently find me. — Proverbs 8:17
devotionals.title: Loving Those Who Love Me
devotionals.prayer: Father, I seek You with all my heart. Thank You for the promise that I will find You.

devotionals.reflection: There is "temporary" wealth and "enduring" wealth. Only the wealth gained through righteousness lasts beyond this life.
devotionals.question: Am I building a "temporary" kingdom or an "enduring" one?
devotionals.scripture: Riches and honor are with me, enduring wealth and righteousness. — Proverbs 8:18
devotionals.title: Enduring Wealth
devotionals.prayer: Lord, help me to invest in things that have eternal value.

devotionals.question: What "fruit" of wisdom (peace, joy, patience) am I enjoying today that money couldn't buy?
devotionals.reflection: Gold can buy a bed, but wisdom buys sleep. Gold can buy a house, but wisdom builds a home. The "fruit" is always better than the "asset."
devotionals.scripture: My fruit is better than gold, even fine gold... — Proverbs 8:19
devotionals.title: Fruit Better Than Gold
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, let the fruit of Your Spirit be the most valuable thing in my life.

devotionals.question: Is my daily "walk" aligned with the paths of justice and righteousness?
devotionals.reflection: Wisdom isn't a destination; it's a "walk." It’s a daily movement in a specific direction: toward what is right.
devotionals.scripture: I walk in the way of righteousness, in the paths of justice... — Proverbs 8:20
devotionals.title: Walking in Justice
devotionals.prayer: Lord, keep my feet in Your ways. Don't let me stray from the path of doing what is right.

devotionals.question: Do I trust ancient Scripture more than I trust the "trending" advice of today?
devotionals.reflection: God’s wisdom is older than the earth itself. It is not a modern "trend"; it is an ancient, timeless foundation.
devotionals.scripture: Before the mountains had been shaped, before the hills, I was brought forth. — Proverbs 8:25
devotionals.title: Before the Mountains
devotionals.prayer: Eternal God, thank You for Your timeless Truth. Help me to build my life on the foundation that never shifts.

devotionals.question: Am I working "beside" God today, or am I trying to build things on my own?
devotionals.reflection: Wisdom was God's "master workman" in creation. When we use wisdom, we are using the very tool God used to build the world.
devotionals.scripture: Then I was beside him, like a master workman, and I was daily his delight... — Proverbs 8:30
devotionals.title: The Master Workman
devotionals.prayer: Father, let me be Your "workman" today. I want to build my life and my work according to Your designs.

devotionals.question: Do I find "delight" in God's creation and His people, as He does?
devotionals.reflection: Wisdom is not somber or boring; it is "rejoicing" and "delighting." God finds joy in His world and in us!
devotionals.scripture: ...rejoicing in his inhabited world and delighting in the children of man. — Proverbs 8:31
devotionals.title: Rejoicing in His World
devotionals.prayer: Lord, fill me with Your joy. Let me delight in the life You’ve given me today.

devotionals.reflection: We should start every morning "watching" for wisdom, like someone waiting for a king to open his gates and speak.
devotionals.question: Am I "watching at the gates" each morning, or am I rushing past them into my day?
devotionals.scripture: Blessed is the one who listens to me, watching daily at my gates... — Proverbs 8:34
devotionals.title: Watching at the Gates
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I am waiting for You. Speak to me through Your Word before I start my work.

devotionals.question: Am I seeking "things" from God, or am I seeking the Wisdom that brings His favor?
devotionals.reflection: Wisdom is the doorway to God’s favor. It isn't just about avoiding "bad" things; it's about walking into the "abundant" life Christ promised.
devotionals.scripture: For whoever finds me finds life and obtains favor from the Lord. — Proverbs 8:35
devotionals.title: Finding Life
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I seek You first today. Let Your life flow through every decision I make.

devotionals.question: What are the "pillars" my life is currently resting on? Are they sturdy or shaky?
devotionals.reflection: A house is only as strong as its support. God’s wisdom offers a structural integrity to our lives that the world cannot provide.
devotionals.scripture: Wisdom has built her house; she has hewn her seven pillars. — Proverbs 9:1
devotionals.title: The Seven Pillars
devotionals.prayer: Father, build my life on Your pillars of truth. Let my foundation be unshakable.

devotionals.reflection: God has prepared a banquet of insight for us. We don't have to starve for direction; we just have to pull up a chair.
devotionals.question: Am I "feeding" on the Word of God daily, or am I snacking on the world's opinions?
devotionals.scripture: Come, eat of my bread and drink of the wine I have mixed. — Proverbs 9:5
devotionals.title: The Feast of Truth
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are the Bread of Life. Feed my soul with Your wisdom today.

devotionals.reflection: Growth requires leaving something behind. We cannot walk toward wisdom while clinging to our old, "simple" habits.
devotionals.question: What "simple" or immature habit am I refusing to leave behind?
devotionals.scripture: Leave your simple ways, and live, and walk in the way of insight. — Proverbs 9:6
devotionals.title: Leaving Immature Ways
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me the strength to walk away from my past and into Your insight.

devotionals.reflection: Our reaction to criticism is the ultimate "litmus test" for our character. The wise man sees correction as a gift.
devotionals.question: When someone corrects me, is my first instinct to defend myself or to listen?
devotionals.scripture: Do not reprove a scoffer, or he will hate you; reprove a wise man, and he will love you. — Proverbs 9:8
devotionals.title: The Scoffer's Response
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me a "wise man" who loves correction and seeks to grow through it.

devotionals.question: Am I living in a way that honors the body and life God has given me?
devotionals.reflection: While only God knows our final day, wisdom protects us from the "shortened" life that comes from stress, sin, and recklessness.
devotionals.scripture: For by me your days will be multiplied, and years will be added to your life. — Proverbs 9:11
devotionals.title: Wisdom Adds Days
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the gift of time. Help me to use every day wisely for Your glory.

devotionals.question: What am I doing "in secret" that I wouldn't want the world—or God—to see?
devotionals.reflection: Sin always tries to make "secrecy" look exciting. It whispers that the "stolen" thing is better, but it's a lie that leads to the grave.
devotionals.scripture: Stolen water is sweet, and bread eaten in secret is pleasant. — Proverbs 9:17
devotionals.title: The Stolen Water
devotionals.prayer: Lord, bring everything into the light. I want the "bread" that comes from Your hand, not from theft.

devotionals.reflection: Our choices don't just affect us; they affect those who love us. Living wisely is a way to honor our parents and our heritage.
devotionals.question: How do my current choices reflect on my family and my God?
devotionals.scripture: A wise son makes a glad father, but a foolish son is a sorrow to his mother. — Proverbs 10:1
devotionals.title: A Father's Joy
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let my life bring joy to those who raised me and glory to my Father in Heaven.

devotionals.question: Am I more concerned with my "profit" or my "integrity"?
devotionals.reflection: Money earned dishonestly has a "curse" attached to it. It can't buy your way out of the ultimate consequences.
devotionals.scripture: Treasures gained by wickedness do not profit, but righteousness delivers from death. — Proverbs 10:2
devotionals.title: Treasures of Wickedness
devotionals.prayer: Father, I choose righteousness over easy gain. Deliver me from the lure of the "quick buck."

devotionals.question: Where have I been "slack" in my responsibilities lately?
devotionals.reflection: God blesses the "hand" that moves. He provides the opportunity, but we must provide the effort and the diligence.
devotionals.scripture: A slack hand causes poverty, but the hand of the diligent makes rich. — Proverbs 10:4
devotionals.title: The Diligent Hand
devotionals.prayer: Lord, energize my hands for the work You've given me. Let me work with all my heart today.

devotionals.reflection: A righteous life attracts God's favor like a magnet. It creates a "crown" of blessings that others can clearly see.
devotionals.question: Does my "reputation" suggest a life that is open and righteous, or one that is hiding something?
devotionals.scripture: Blessings are on the head of the righteous, but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence. — Proverbs 10:6
devotionals.title: Blessings on the Head
devotionals.prayer: Father, let Your blessings rest on me as I seek to walk in Your ways.

devotionals.reflection: What will people say at your funeral? A wise life leaves a "scent" of blessing that lingers long after you are gone.
devotionals.question: What kind of "memory" am I creating for people today?
devotionals.scripture: The memory of the righteous is a blessing, but the name of the wicked will rot. — Proverbs 10:7
devotionals.title: The Memory of the Just
devotionals.prayer: Lord, help me to live in a way that leaves a legacy of grace and truth.

devotionals.question: Am I talking so much that I'm missing what God is trying to tell me?
devotionals.reflection: Wisdom listens more than it talks. If your mouth is always moving ("babbling"), you aren't hearing the instructions that save.
devotionals.scripture: The wise of heart will receive commandments, but a babbling fool will come to ruin. — Proverbs 10:8
devotionals.title: The Wise Heart
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, quiet my spirit. Help me to be "quick to hear" and "slow to speak."

devotionals.reflection: There is a "rest" that comes with integrity. You don't have to remember your lies or worry about being "caught."
devotionals.question: Is there anything in my life right now that I am afraid will be "found out"?
devotionals.scripture: Whoever walks in integrity walks securely, but he who makes his ways crooked will be found out. — Proverbs 10:9
devotionals.title: Integrity's Security
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I choose the secure path of integrity. Keep my heart pure and my ways straight.

devotionals.question: Did my words today "water" someone's soul or "poison" it?
devotionals.reflection: Our words should be "refreshing" to others. Like a fountain, they should bring life, hope, and healing to those who hear them.
devotionals.scripture: The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life... — Proverbs 10:11
devotionals.title: The Fountain of Life
devotionals.prayer: Father, let my mouth be a source of life. Use my words to encourage and build up.

devotionals.question: Who do I need to "cover" with love today instead of pointing out their flaws?
devotionals.reflection: We have a choice: we can stir the pot of conflict, or we can use the "blanket" of love to cover an offense and move on.
devotionals.scripture: Hatred stirs up strife, but love covers all offenses. — Proverbs 10:12
devotionals.title: Love Covers Offenses
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for covering my sins. Help me to extend that same grace to others.

devotionals.reflection: Think of wisdom as a "savings account." You "lay it up" now so that you have what you need when the crisis hits.
devotionals.question: What "knowledge" am I storing up today for my future?
devotionals.scripture: The wise lay up knowledge, but the mouth of a fool brings ruin near. — Proverbs 10:14
devotionals.title: Storing Up Knowledge
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, teach me Your ways so I am prepared for whatever tomorrow brings.

devotionals.reflection: It’s not just about what you earn, but where it leads. A wise person uses their "wage" to build life for themselves and others.
devotionals.question: How am I using my "paycheck" to promote life and godliness?
devotionals.scripture: The wage of the righteous leads to life, the gain of the wicked to sin. — Proverbs 10:16
devotionals.title: The Labor of the Righteous
devotionals.prayer: Lord, everything I earn is Yours. Direct me in how to use it for Your Kingdom.

devotionals.question: Do I know when to "stop talking" in a heated or awkward situation?
devotionals.reflection: The more we talk, the more likely we are to say something we regret. Silence is often the most "prudent" choice we can make.
devotionals.scripture: When words are many, transgression is not lacking, but whoever restrains his lips is prudent. — Proverbs 10:19
devotionals.title: Many Words, Much Sin
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me the gift of restraint. Help me to keep my mouth shut when it’s better to remain silent.

devotionals.reflection: A wise person’s words are "valuable." They aren't "cheap talk"; they are weighted with truth and kindness.
devotionals.question: Are my words today "choice silver" or "cheap scrap"?
devotionals.scripture: The tongue of the righteous is choice silver; the heart of the wicked is of little worth. — Proverbs 10:20
devotionals.title: Choice Silver
devotionals.prayer: Father, refine my tongue. Let my speech be valuable to those who listen.

devotionals.reflection: Worldly wealth often comes with "sorrow"—stress, broken families, or guilt. God’s blessing brings a "pure" kind of richness.
devotionals.question: Am I chasing a "wealth" that is bringing me sorrow instead of peace?
devotionals.scripture: The blessing of the Lord makes rich, and he adds no sorrow with it. — Proverbs 10:22
devotionals.title: Wealth Without Sorrow
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I desire Your blessing above all. Give me the "richness" that comes with a quiet soul.

devotionals.question: What is the deepest "desire" of my heart, and is it aligned with God's will?
devotionals.reflection: The wicked are driven by "dread," but the righteous are pulled by "desire." God loves to fulfill the holy longings of His children.
devotionals.scripture: What the wicked dreads will come upon him, but the desire of the righteous will be granted. — Proverbs 10:24
devotionals.title: The Desire of the Righteous
devotionals.prayer: Father, grant me the desires that align with Your heart. I trust You with my hopes.

devotionals.reflection: Storms ("tempests") reveal our foundation. If we are built on Christ's wisdom, the storm can shake us, but it cannot "un-establish" us.
devotionals.question: What "storm" am I currently facing, and am I trusting in my eternal foundation?
devotionals.scripture: When the tempest passes, the wicked is no more, but the righteous is established forever. — Proverbs 10:25
devotionals.title: The Eternal Foundation
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for being my Rock. When the winds blow, I am secure in You.

devotionals.reflection: Irritating! That is how a lazy or unreliable person feels to a boss or a friend. Wisdom makes us "reliable" and "refreshing."
devotionals.question: Am I a "smoke to the eyes" for my coworkers, or am I someone they can count on?
devotionals.scripture: Like vinegar to the teeth and smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to those who send him. — Proverbs 10:26
devotionals.title: Smoke to the Eyes
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me a person of my word. Help me to follow through on my commitments today.

devotionals.question: Do I see God's Word as a "stronghold" that protects me or as a "fence" that limits me?
devotionals.reflection: God's "way" (His laws) is either your castle or your cage. To the blameless, it is a safe "stronghold" that protects them from the world.
devotionals.scripture: The way of the Lord is a stronghold to the blameless, but destruction to evildoers. — Proverbs 10:29
devotionals.title: The Way as a Stronghold
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the safety of Your Word. I run into Your stronghold today.

devotionals.question: Am I cutting any "small" corners in my work or finances that God sees as a "false balance"?
devotionals.reflection: God cares about the "small" details of our integrity—the scales of our business and the fairness of our dealings. He delights in a person who is 100% honest.
devotionals.scripture: A false balance is an abomination to the Lord, but a just weight is his delight. — Proverbs 11:1
devotionals.title: The False Balance
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I want to be Your delight. Make me a person of absolute honesty in every transaction today.

devotionals.question: Where is pride currently blinding me to the wisdom I actually need?
devotionals.reflection: Pride always precedes a fall. Wisdom is found in the "low places"—among those who admit they don't have all the answers.
devotionals.scripture: When pride comes, then comes disgrace, but with the humble is wisdom. — Proverbs 11:2
devotionals.title: Humility Brings Wisdom
devotionals.prayer: Father, clothe me in humility. I recognize that all my insight comes from You, not from myself.

devotionals.question: Is my "integrity" the primary factor in my decision-making process today?
devotionals.reflection: Integrity isn't just a trait; it’s a "GPS." When you commit to doing what is right, the next step usually becomes clear automatically.
devotionals.scripture: The integrity of the upright guides them, but the crookedness of the treacherous destroys them. — Proverbs 11:3
devotionals.title: The Guide of Integrity
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let my commitment to Your standards be the compass that points my way.

devotionals.question: What "lust" or desire is currently trying to take me captive?
devotionals.reflection: Doing the right thing is its own form of rescue. It keeps us from being "kidnapped" by our own runaway desires.
devotionals.scripture: The righteousness of the upright delivers them, but the treacherous are taken captive by their lust. — Proverbs 11:6
devotionals.title: Delivered by Righteousness
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are my righteousness. Deliver me from the distractions that try to pull me away.

devotionals.question: Am I using my words to build a bridge or to "destroy" someone's reputation?
devotionals.reflection: Words can be weapons of mass destruction. Wisdom teaches us to use "knowledge" to protect ourselves and others from toxic talk.
devotionals.scripture: With his mouth the godless man would destroy his neighbor, but by knowledge the righteous are delivered. — Proverbs 11:9
devotionals.title: The Mouth as a Weapon
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, guard my tongue and my ears today. Let me only speak what is helpful.

devotionals.reflection: One righteous person can lift the "atmosphere" of an entire office, family, or city. Your character has a ripple effect.
devotionals.question: How is my "uprightness" bringing a blessing to the environment where I work and live?
devotionals.scripture: By the blessing of the upright a city is exalted, but by the mouth of the wicked it is overthrown. — Proverbs 11:11
devotionals.title: City-Wide Influence
devotionals.prayer: Lord, use me to lift up my community. Let my presence be a blessing to those around me.

devotionals.question: Do I feel the need to point out everyone else's flaws, or can I be "silent" in grace?
devotionals.reflection: It takes a "man of understanding" to keep his mouth shut when he sees someone else's failure. Belittling others only proves our own lack of wisdom.
devotionals.scripture: Whoever belittles his neighbor lacks sense, but a man of understanding remains silent. — Proverbs 11:12
devotionals.title: The Silent Neighbor
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me the wisdom to be silent when I am tempted to criticize.

devotionals.reflection: A trustworthy person is a safe harbor. They don't trade on "secrets" to gain social standing. They protect the confidences of others.
devotionals.question: Can people trust me with their "secrets," or am I a "slander collector"?
devotionals.scripture: Whoever goes about slandering reveals secrets, but he who is trustworthy in spirit keeps a thing covered. — Proverbs 11:13
devotionals.title: Trustworthy in Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me a "trustworthy spirit." Help me to honor the privacy and dignity of my friends.

devotionals.question: Who are the "counselors" I have invited to speak into my current situation?
devotionals.reflection: Isolation is the enemy of success. We are "safe" when we surround ourselves with multiple godly voices who can see our blind spots.
devotionals.scripture: Where there is no guidance, a people falls, but in an abundance of counselors there is safety. — Proverbs 11:14
devotionals.title: Safety in Many Counselors
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the people in my life who offer wise advice. Help me to listen.

devotionals.reflection: Kindness isn't just for others; it creates a healthy inner environment for "yourself." Bitterness is a poison we drink hoping someone else dies.
devotionals.question: Am I being "cruel" to my own soul by holding onto anger or unforgiveness?
devotionals.scripture: A man who is kind benefits himself, but a cruel man hurts himself. — Proverbs 11:17
devotionals.title: Kind to Oneself
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let Your kindness flow through me. Heal me of the cruelty I’ve held toward myself or others.

devotionals.question: What am I "sowing" today? Am I willing to wait for a "sure" reward?
devotionals.reflection: Wickedness promises a payout that never arrives. Righteousness is like a seed—it may take time, but the "reward" is guaranteed by God.
devotionals.scripture: The wicked earns deceptive wages, but one who sows righteousness gets a sure reward. — Proverbs 11:18
devotionals.title: Sowing Righteousness
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, help me to keep planting seeds of truth and love, even when I don't see the harvest yet.

devotionals.question: Am I spending more time on my "outer ring" than on my inner "discretion"?
devotionals.reflection: Physical beauty without "discretion" or character is a waste of a precious gift. True beauty is found in the wisdom of our choices.
devotionals.scripture: Like a gold ring in a pig's snout is a beautiful woman without discretion. — Proverbs 11:22
devotionals.title: The Golden Ring in a Pig
devotionals.prayer: Lord, refine my character. Let my inner beauty be what people notice most.

devotionals.question: Who can I "water" with encouragement or resources today?
devotionals.reflection: The economy of God is the opposite of the world. We gain by giving away. When you "water" others, God makes sure you don't run dry.
devotionals.scripture: Whoever brings blessing will be enriched, and one who waters will himself be watered. — Proverbs 11:25
devotionals.title: The Generous Soul
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for Your abundance. Make me a channel of blessing so that I may be refreshed.

devotionals.reflection: Money is a terrible foundation; it can disappear overnight. A righteous life is "organic"—it grows and flourishes because it is rooted in God.
devotionals.question: Where is my "trust" today—in my bank balance or in the Living God?
devotionals.scripture: Whoever trusts in his riches will fall, but the righteous will flourish like a green leaf. — Proverbs 11:28
devotionals.title: Trusting in Riches
devotionals.prayer: Lord, be my root and my source. I want to flourish like a green leaf in Your presence.

devotionals.reflection: A wise person doesn't just manage their own life; they "capture" the hearts and minds of others for God's Kingdom.
devotionals.question: How am I using my influence to draw others closer to Christ?
devotionals.scripture: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life, and whoever captures souls is wise. — Proverbs 11:30
devotionals.title: Winning Souls
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, help me to be a "fisher of men." Let my life be a "tree of life" for those who are lost.

devotionals.reflection: Solomon doesn't mince words! To grow, we must "love" the very things that correct us. Resisting correction is the hallmark of a fool.
devotionals.question: When was the last time I thanked someone for correcting me?
devotionals.scripture: Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge, but he who hates reproof is stupid. — Proverbs 12:1
devotionals.title: Loving Discipline
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me a heart that loves to learn, even when the lesson is painful.

devotionals.question: Are my current habits providing "roots" or just "leaves" that will blow away?
devotionals.reflection: You can't build a life on "bad" dirt. Wickedness is unstable soil. Only righteousness provides the "roots" that keep you standing in the storm.
devotionals.scripture: No one is established by wickedness, but the root of the righteous will never be moved. — Proverbs 12:3
devotionals.title: The Root of the Righteous
devotionals.prayer: Father, deepen my roots in Your Word. Establish me so that I cannot be moved.

devotionals.question: How am I being a "crown" to my spouse or the people I am closest to?
devotionals.reflection: Character in marriage is vital. A godly spouse is an honor ("crown"), while a contentious one is a hidden, painful disease.
devotionals.scripture: An excellent wife is the crown of her husband, but she who brings shame is like rottenness in his bones. — Proverbs 12:4
devotionals.title: An Excellent Wife
devotionals.prayer: Lord, help me to be a source of honor and health to my family.

devotionals.reflection: Godly character extends even to how we treat animals. A righteous heart is naturally compassionate toward all of God's creation.
devotionals.question: Does my compassion show up in the "small" things, like how I treat pets or nature?
devotionals.scripture: Whoever is righteous has regard for the life of his beast, but the mercy of the wicked is cruel. — Proverbs 12:10
devotionals.title: Merciful Even to Animals
devotionals.prayer: Creator God, thank You for all Your creatures. Help me to be a kind steward of Your world.

devotionals.question: What is the "land" God has given me to work, and am I being faithful to it?
devotionals.reflection: Success comes from "working your own land"—focusing on your specific responsibilities rather than chasing "get-rich-quick" fantasies.
devotionals.scripture: Whoever works his farmland will have plenty of bread, but he who follows worthless pursuits lacks sense. — Proverbs 12:11
devotionals.title: Working the Land
devotionals.prayer: Lord, keep me focused on the tasks You have assigned to me today.

devotionals.question: Am I "weaving a web" with my words, or am I walking in the freedom of the truth?
devotionals.reflection: Lies create a "web" that eventually traps the liar. Truth acts as a "get out of jail free" card because it keeps your path simple.
devotionals.scripture: An evil man is ensnared by the transgression of his lips, but the righteous escapes from trouble. — Proverbs 12:13
devotionals.title: The Trap of the Lips
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are the Truth. Keep my lips from the traps of exaggeration and deceit.

devotionals.question: Whose advice have I sought this week for the problems I am facing?
devotionals.reflection: A fool is "un-coachable." He thinks he knows everything. A wise man knows that his "own eyes" might be deceived.
devotionals.scripture: The way of a fool is right in his own eyes, but a wise man listens to advice. — Proverbs 12:15
devotionals.title: Right in His Own Eyes
devotionals.prayer: Father, save me from the delusion that I am always right. Help me to listen.

devotionals.question: Did I "stab" anyone today with a rash comment, or did I bring healing?
devotionals.reflection: Words can "stab" like a sword. We have the power to either wound people or "suture" their wounds with wise, kind speech.
devotionals.scripture: There is one whose rash words are like sword thrusts, but the tongue of the wise brings healing. — Proverbs 12:18
devotionals.title: The Rash Word
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let my tongue be a medicine for the brokenhearted people I meet today.

devotionals.question: Is there a gap between what I say and what I actually do?
devotionals.reflection: God finds lies "disgusting" (abomination). He takes delight in "faithfulness"—when our actions match our words.
devotionals.scripture: Lying lips are an abomination to the Lord, but those who act faithfully are his delight. — Proverbs 12:22
devotionals.title: Lying Lips
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, make me a person of my word. Let my life be a delight to the Father.

devotionals.question: Who can I "lighten the load" for today with a simple word of hope?
devotionals.reflection: Anxiety is a heavy weight. Sometimes the "cure" for someone's heavy heart is simply a "good word" of encouragement from you.
devotionals.scripture: Anxiety in a man's heart weighs him down, but a good word makes him glad. — Proverbs 12:25
devotionals.title: Anxiety and a Good Word
devotionals.prayer: Father, use me to lift someone's spirits today. Give me the right "good word" to share.

devotionals.question: Am I working hard enough today to earn my "freedom," or am I being slothful?
devotionals.reflection: Diligence leads to leadership and freedom. Laziness leads to being "controlled" by circumstances or by others.
devotionals.scripture: The hand of the diligent will rule, while the slothful will be put to forced labor. — Proverbs 12:24
devotionals.title: The Diligent vs. The Lazy
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me the energy to be diligent. I want to be a leader, not a follower of sloth.

devotionals.question: What "ruin" am I inviting by talking too much or too soon?
devotionals.reflection: Think of your mouth as a "gate." If you leave it wide open, anything can get in or out. Guarding that gate is a matter of survival.
devotionals.scripture: Whoever guards his mouth preserves his life; he who opens wide his lips comes to ruin. — Proverbs 13:3
devotionals.title: Guarding the Mouth
devotionals.prayer: Lord, set a guard over my mouth. Help me to be "slow to speak" and "quick to think."

devotionals.question: Am I "craving" things I am not willing to work for?
devotionals.reflection: Desiring success is not the same as achieving it. A "sluggard" wants the result without the work. Diligence fills the soul.
devotionals.scripture: The soul of the sluggard craves and gets nothing, while the soul of the diligent is richly supplied. — Proverbs 13:4
devotionals.title: The Soul of the Sluggard
devotionals.prayer: Father, transform my "cravings" into "commitments." Help me to work for what I desire.

devotionals.reflection: A righteous life has a "glow" that lasts. Wickedness is a temporary "lamp" that eventually flickers and dies in the dark.
devotionals.question: Does my "light" bring joy to those around me, or is it starting to flicker?
devotionals.scripture: The light of the righteous rejoices, but the lamp of the wicked will be put out. — Proverbs 13:9
devotionals.title: Light of the Righteous
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, let Your light shine through me. Keep my lamp burning bright for Your glory.

devotionals.question: What current "strife" in my life could be solved by a little more humility?
devotionals.reflection: Almost every argument is rooted in "insolence" (pride). If you want to end a conflict, start by asking for advice and listening.
devotionals.scripture: By insolence comes nothing but strife, but with those who take advice is wisdom. — Proverbs 13:10
devotionals.title: Pride and Strife
devotionals.prayer: Lord, deliver me from the need to always be right. Give me the wisdom to seek advice.

devotionals.reflection: The "little by little" principle is God's way. Slow, steady growth produces character and lasting wealth. Quick gain usually vanishes.
devotionals.question: Am I being faithful in the "little by little" process of my life and finances?
devotionals.scripture: Wealth gained hastily will dwindle, but whoever gathers little by little will increase it. — Proverbs 13:11
devotionals.title: Wealth Gained Hastily
devotionals.prayer: Father, teach me the value of patience. Help me to build my life one steady step at a time.

devotionals.question: What "deferred hope" am I currently waiting on, and am I trusting God with the timing?
devotionals.reflection: Waiting is hard and can drain our spirits. But when God finally fulfills a promise, it brings a massive surge of life and energy.
devotionals.scripture: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but a desire fulfilled is a tree of life. — Proverbs 13:12
devotionals.title: Hope Deferred
devotionals.prayer: Lord, refresh my heart while I wait. I believe that Your fulfillment will be a tree of life.

devotionals.reflection: To "despise" God's Word doesn't mean you hate it; it often just means you ignore it. Ignoring the "instruction manual" leads to a broken life.
devotionals.question: Do I "revere" God's commands enough to actually change my behavior today?
devotionals.scripture: Whoever despises the word brings destruction on himself, but he who reveres the commandment will be rewarded. — Proverbs 13:13
devotionals.title: Despising the Word
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me a holy respect for Your Word. Let me find my "reward" in obeying You.

devotionals.reflection: Wisdom is "contagious." So is folly. You are becoming like the five people you spend the most time with.
devotionals.question: Are my "companions" making me more like Christ or more like a fool?
devotionals.scripture: Whoever walks with the wise becomes wise, but the companion of fools will suffer harm. — Proverbs 13:20
devotionals.title: Walk with the Wise
devotionals.prayer: Lord, surround me with wise friends. Help me to be a wise companion to others as well.

devotionals.question: What kind of "inheritance" (spiritual or financial) am I preparing for the next generation?
devotionals.reflection: Wisdom thinks two generations ahead. A "good man" manages his life so that his grandchildren are blessed by his choices.
devotionals.scripture: A good man leaves an inheritance to his children's children... — Proverbs 13:22
devotionals.title: Leaving an Inheritance
devotionals.prayer: Father, help me to live for more than just myself. Let my life be a blessing to my future family.

devotionals.question: Am I avoiding a necessary "correction" in my home or work because I’m afraid of conflict?
devotionals.reflection: True love requires "discipline." Letting someone continue in a destructive path isn't kindness—it's neglect. Discipline is a form of investment.
devotionals.scripture: Whoever spares the rod hates his son, but he who loves him is diligent to discipline him. — Proverbs 13:24
devotionals.title: Diligent Discipline
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me the courage to discipline with love. Help me to lead those under my care toward Your truth.

devotionals.reflection: We are either "builders" or "wreckers." Our daily words and attitudes either add a brick to our "house" or pull one out.
devotionals.question: Am I building up my home and my relationships today, or am I "tearing them down" with my attitude?
devotionals.scripture: The wisest of women builds her house, but folly with her own hands tears it down. — Proverbs 14:1
devotionals.title: The Wise Woman Builds
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, make me a builder. Help me to use my hands and my words to construct something beautiful for You.

devotionals.reflection: Your "walk" is a direct reflection of your "worship." If you respect God, your path will be upright. If you are devious, you are showing a lack of respect for Him.
devotionals.question: Does my "walk" today prove that I fear the Lord?
devotionals.scripture: Whoever walks in uprightness fears the Lord, but he who is devious in his ways despises him. — Proverbs 14:2
devotionals.title: The Way of the Upright
devotionals.prayer: Father, let my integrity be my highest form of worship to You.

devotionals.question: Am I avoiding a "messy" situation that is actually necessary for my growth?
devotionals.reflection: Growth is messy. If you want "abundant crops," you have to deal with the "mess" of the ox. Don't let a desire for "neatness" stop you from producing.
devotionals.scripture: Where there are no oxen, the manger is clean, but abundant crops come by the strength of the ox. — Proverbs 14:4
devotionals.title: The Empty Manger
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me the patience to handle the "mess" that comes with the blessing of productivity.

devotionals.reflection: A "faithful" person has a default setting of truth. A "false" person "breathes" lies—deception is as natural to them as air.
devotionals.question: Is truth my "default setting," or do I find myself "breathing" out small deceptions?
devotionals.scripture: A faithful witness does not lie, but a false witness breathes out lies. — Proverbs 14:5
devotionals.title: A Faithful Witness
devotionals.prayer: Spirit of Truth, fill my lungs. Let every word I speak be faithful and true.

devotionals.reflection: If you have a "scoffing" (arrogant) heart, you can search for wisdom forever and never find it. To the humble, truth is "easy" to see.
devotionals.question: Is my "scoffing" attitude making it harder for me to understand God’s will?
devotionals.scripture: A scoffer seeks wisdom in vain, but knowledge is easy for a man of understanding. — Proverbs 14:6
devotionals.title: Knowledge to the Man of Sense
devotionals.prayer: Lord, soften my heart. Make Your knowledge "easy" for me to grasp through my humility.

devotionals.reflection: Some conversations are a waste of time. If there is no "knowledge" present, wisdom says it’s time to "leave the presence" of that person.
devotionals.question: Am I lingering in "foolish" conversations that are draining my spirit?
devotionals.scripture: Leave the presence of a fool, for there you do not meet words of knowledge. — Proverbs 14:7
devotionals.title: Leave the Fool
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me the discernment to know when to walk away from a fruitless argument.

devotionals.question: Am I being honest with myself about where my current path is leading?
devotionals.reflection: Wisdom is "discerning your own way"—knowing where you are going. Folly is "deceiving yourself" into thinking you’re fine when you’re not.
devotionals.scripture: The wisdom of the prudent is to discern his way, but the folly of fools is deceiving. — Proverbs 14:8
devotionals.title: The Wisdom of the Prudent
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me the "prudence" to see my own heart clearly. Don't let me deceive myself.

devotionals.question: Do I "mock" or minimize my own sin, or do I take it to the cross for forgiveness?
devotionals.reflection: A fool makes a joke out of "guilt" or sin. The wise person takes it seriously and finds "acceptance" and peace through repentance.
devotionals.scripture: Fools mock at the guilt offering, but the upright enjoy acceptance. — Proverbs 14:9
devotionals.title: Mocking at Sin
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for the "guilt offering" of Your life. I take my sin seriously and I rest in Your acceptance.

devotionals.reflection: There are parts of our internal world that only God can truly see. We are never fully "alone" because God "shares" the secrets of our hearts.
devotionals.question: Am I taking my "inner bitterness" to God, who understands it better than any "stranger"?
devotionals.scripture: The heart knows its own bitterness, and no stranger shares its joy. — Proverbs 14:10
devotionals.title: The Heart's Own Bitterness
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for knowing me fully. I share my deepest joys and sorrows with You today.

devotionals.question: Am I following my "feelings" or am I following the "map" of God's Word?
devotionals.reflection: This is one of the most famous warnings in the Bible. Our "feelings" are not a reliable guide. What "feels" right can be a dead end.
devotionals.scripture: There is a way that seems right to a man, but its end is the way to death. — Proverbs 14:12
devotionals.title: A Way That Seems Right
devotionals.prayer: Lord, protect me from the "way that seems right." Lead me in the way that is right.

devotionals.reflection: It’s okay not to be okay. Even when we are "laughing" on the outside, God sees the "ache" on the inside. He is a God of reality.
devotionals.question: Am I "masking" my pain with laughter, or am I being real with God about my "ache"?
devotionals.scripture: Even in laughter the heart may ache, and the end of joy may be grief. — Proverbs 14:13
devotionals.title: Laughter and Sorrow
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, Man of Sorrows and King of Joy, I bring my whole heart to You—the laughter and the ache.

devotionals.question: Am I "full" of my own ways, or am I "hungry" for God's ways today?
devotionals.reflection: Backsliding starts in the "heart." Eventually, you get "full" of your own bad choices until you are sick of them.
devotionals.scripture: The backslider in heart will be filled with the fruit of his own ways... — Proverbs 14:14
devotionals.title: Sated with His Own Ways
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I don't want to be filled with my own fruit. Fill me with the fruit of Your Spirit instead.

devotionals.reflection: Don't believe everything you hear or read on social media. A "prudent" person "gives thought"—they verify before they move.
devotionals.question: Am I "simple" (believing everything) or am I "prudent" (thinking through my steps)?
devotionals.scripture: The simple believes everything, but the prudent gives thought to his steps. — Proverbs 14:15
devotionals.title: The Simple vs. The Prudent
devotionals.prayer: Father, grant me a discerning mind. Help me to verify the truth before I act.

devotionals.reflection: Wisdom is "cautious." It sees the danger and "turns away." A fool is "reckless"—he runs toward the edge and thinks he won't fall.
devotionals.question: In what area of my life am I being "reckless" instead of "cautious"?
devotionals.scripture: One who is wise is cautious and turns away from evil, but a fool is reckless and careless. — Proverbs 14:16
devotionals.title: Fearing and Turning Away
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me the wisdom to be cautious. Help me to "turn away" from the things that are dangerous for my soul.

devotionals.question: What "triggered" me yesterday, and did I respond with wisdom or a quick temper?
devotionals.reflection: Anger is a temporary madness. When we let our "fuse" get too short, we end up doing things that make us look foolish. Wisdom lengthens the fuse.
devotionals.scripture: A man of quick temper acts foolishly, and a man of evil devices is hated. — Proverbs 14:17
devotionals.title: The Quick-Tempered
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me a cool spirit. Help me to breathe and seek Your perspective before I react in anger.

devotionals.reflection: Folly is a "hand-me-down" from our old nature. Knowledge is a "crown"—it’s something we actively put on that changes how people see us.
devotionals.question: Am I "wearing" my knowledge of God's Word today, or am I still dressed in my old "folly"?
devotionals.scripture: The simple inherit folly, but the prudent are crowned with knowledge. — Proverbs 14:18
devotionals.title: Crowned with Knowledge
devotionals.prayer: Father, let Your truth be my crown. May my choices today reflect the knowledge of Your ways.

devotionals.reflection: We cannot love God and "despise" the people He created. Generosity is the practical proof of a heart that values what God values.
devotionals.question: Is there someone in my neighborhood or workplace I have been "despising" instead of serving?
devotionals.scripture: Whoever despises his neighbor is a sinner, but blessed is he who is generous to the poor. — Proverbs 14:21
devotionals.title: Despising the Neighbor
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, give me Your eyes for the "least of these." Help me to be generous with my time and resources.

devotionals.reflection: Talking about a dream is easy; "toiling" for it is hard. God blesses the action, not just the aspiration.
devotionals.question: Am I doing more "talking" than "toiling" regarding my goals right now?
devotionals.scripture: In all toil there is profit, but mere talk leads only to poverty. — Proverbs 14:23
devotionals.title: All Hard Work
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me the grit to do the hard work. Let my actions speak louder than my words today.

devotionals.question: Does my "fear of the Lord" give me confidence, or am I still living in fear of people?
devotionals.reflection: Fearing God doesn't make you a coward; it makes you "confident." When you answer to the King of Kings, you don't have to fear anyone else.
devotionals.scripture: In the fear of the Lord one has strong confidence, and his children will have a refuge. — Proverbs 14:26
devotionals.title: A Strong Confidence
devotionals.prayer: Father, be my refuge and my confidence. I trust in Your power more than my problems.

devotionals.reflection: Great understanding is the ability to see the "why" behind someone's behavior. This perspective naturally makes us "slow to anger."
devotionals.question: Can I stop today to understand "why" someone is frustrating me before I get angry?
devotionals.scripture: Whoever is slow to anger has great understanding, but he who has a hasty temper exalts folly. — Proverbs 14:29
devotionals.title: Slow to Anger
devotionals.prayer: Lord, broaden my understanding. Help me to see others through Your lens of grace and patience.

devotionals.question: What "envy" am I holding onto that is currently making my "bones rot"?
devotionals.reflection: Jealousy is a physical toxin. It literally "rots" your vitality. A "tranquil heart" (peace) is better for your health than any supplement.
devotionals.scripture: A tranquil heart gives life to the flesh, but envy makes the bones rot. — Proverbs 14:30
devotionals.title: A Tranquil Heart
devotionals.prayer: God, I surrender my jealousy. Give me a tranquil heart that is satisfied in You alone.

devotionals.reflection: How we treat the "vulnerable" is a direct commentary on how we feel about God. To help the poor is a form of high worship.
devotionals.question: How have I "honored my Maker" by helping someone in need this week?
devotionals.scripture: Whoever oppresses a poor man insults his Maker, but he who is generous to the needy honors him. — Proverbs 14:31
devotionals.title: Oppressing the Poor
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for Your generosity toward me. Help me to honor You by being generous to others.

devotionals.question: Am I contributing to the "righteousness" of my community or to its "reproach"?
devotionals.reflection: Nations aren't built on politics alone, but on the "righteousness" of the people. Character is the true strength of any society.
devotionals.scripture: Righteousness exalts a nation, but sin is a reproach to any people. — Proverbs 14:34
devotionals.title: Exalting a Nation
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let Your righteousness start with me. Use my life to lift up my nation.

devotionals.question: When someone speaks harshly to me today, will I respond with "water" or "gasoline"?
devotionals.reflection: You can "de-escalate" a fight with the tone of your voice. A "soft answer" is like water on a fire; a "harsh word" is like gasoline.
devotionals.scripture: A soft answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger. — Proverbs 15:1
devotionals.title: A Soft Answer
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, Prince of Peace, help me to speak with softness and grace even when I’m being attacked.

devotionals.question: Whose spirit have I "broken" lately with my words, and how can I bring "healing" today?
devotionals.reflection: Your words can either "feed" someone's spirit or "break" it. A "gentle" tongue is life-giving and restorative.
devotionals.scripture: A gentle tongue is a tree of life, but perverseness in it breaks the spirit. — Proverbs 15:4
devotionals.title: A Gentle Tongue
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let my words be like a "tree of life" to everyone I speak with today.

devotionals.question: Am I willing to have "less" if it means I have more peace and more of God?
devotionals.reflection: Wealth isn't worth it if it comes with "trouble" (strife, guilt, or anxiety). "A little" with God is always "more" than a lot without Him.
devotionals.scripture: Better is a little with the fear of the Lord than great treasure and trouble with it. — Proverbs 15:16
devotionals.title: Better a Little
devotionals.prayer: Father, I choose You over treasure. Satisfy me with Your presence, even if I have but "a little."

devotionals.reflection: The "atmosphere" of your home is more important than the "menu." A simple meal served in love is better than a feast served in anger.
devotionals.question: How am I contributing to the "atmosphere" of my home or workplace today?
devotionals.scripture: Better is a dinner of herbs where love is than a fattened ox and hatred with it. — Proverbs 15:17
devotionals.title: A Dinner of Herbs
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let love be the main ingredient in all my relationships today.

devotionals.question: Does my work feel like "thorns" because I’m resisting the effort required?
devotionals.reflection: For a lazy person, every task feels like "thorns"—painful and difficult. For the upright, discipline creates a "level highway" of progress.
devotionals.scripture: The way of a sluggard is like a hedge of thorns, but the path of the upright is a level highway. — Proverbs 15:19
devotionals.title: The Way of the Lazy
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me a "highway" spirit. Help me to do my work with joy and discipline.

devotionals.question: Am I asking God for "words in season" before I speak to someone in crisis?
devotionals.reflection: Wisdom is about "timing." An "apt answer" is the right word at the right time. It brings joy to both the speaker and the hearer.
devotionals.scripture: To make an apt answer is a joy to a man, and a word in season, how good it is! — Proverbs 15:23
devotionals.title: The Joy of the Right Word
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me the "apt answer" for the people I encounter today. Let my words be timely and good.

devotionals.reflection: Greed is a "home-wrecker." When we chase money at any cost, our family is the one that pays the price.
devotionals.question: Is my "hustle" for more gain currently "troubling" my family or my peace?
devotionals.scripture: Whoever is greedy for unjust gain troubles his own household... — Proverbs 15:27
devotionals.title: Greedy for Gain
devotionals.prayer: Lord, protect my household from greed. Help me to be content with Your provision.

devotionals.reflection: Wisdom "ponders" (thinks) before it speaks. Foolishness "pours out" (blurts) whatever is on its mind. Reflection is the key to righteousness.
devotionals.question: Do I have a "filter" between my heart and my mouth today?
devotionals.scripture: The heart of the righteous ponders how to answer, but the mouth of the wicked pours out evil things. — Proverbs 15:28
devotionals.title: Pondering the Answer
devotionals.prayer: Father, slow me down. Help me to "ponder" my words before I let them out.

devotionals.reflection: We can make our spreadsheets and our five-year plans, but God has the final say. True peace comes from surrendering our plans to His "answer."
devotionals.question: What plan am I holding onto too tightly that I need to surrender to God's "final answer"?
devotionals.scripture: The plans of the heart belong to man, but the answer of the tongue is from the Lord. — Proverbs 16:1
devotionals.title: Plans of the Heart
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I make my plans, but I trust Your direction. Have Your way in my life today.

devotionals.question: Have I "rolled over" my current project or problem to the Lord, or am I still carrying it?
devotionals.reflection: To "commit" means to "roll over" the burden to God. When we work with God, He takes responsibility for the outcome.
devotionals.scripture: Commit your work to the Lord, and your plans will be established. — Proverbs 16:3
devotionals.title: Committing Your Work
devotionals.prayer: Father, I commit my work to You. I trust You to establish my plans according to Your will.

devotionals.question: How has God's "steadfast love" changed my behavior recently?
devotionals.reflection: God’s "steadfast love" is what clears our record. When we experience His faithfulness, we find the power to turn away from evil.
devotionals.scripture: By steadfast love and faithfulness iniquity is atoned for... — Proverbs 16:6
devotionals.title: Purgation by Steadfast Love
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for Your atonement. Let Your faithfulness be the reason I turn from sin today.

devotionals.reflection: You don't have to "manage" your enemies; you just have to "please" your God. He can handle the people who are against you.
devotionals.question: Am I spending more time worrying about my "enemies" than I am pleasing my Lord?
devotionals.scripture: When a man's ways please the Lord, he makes even his enemies to be at peace with him. — Proverbs 16:7
devotionals.title: Pleasing the Lord
devotionals.prayer: Father, I focus on pleasing You. I trust You to handle my critics and my conflicts.

devotionals.question: Can I trust God with the "individual steps" of my day, even if the "way" seems unclear?
devotionals.reflection: We see the big "way," but God sees the individual "steps." He is in the details of your commute, your meetings, and your conversations.
devotionals.scripture: The heart of man plans his way, but the Lord establishes his steps. — Proverbs 16:9
devotionals.title: The Heart and the Steps
devotionals.prayer: Lord, establish my steps today. Guide me where I need to go, one step at a time.

devotionals.question: Which "city" am I trying to "take" today while my own "spirit" is still out of control?
devotionals.reflection: Self-mastery is greater than world-mastery. Winning an argument or a promotion is nothing compared to winning the battle over your own temper.
devotionals.scripture: Whoever is slow to anger is better than the mighty, and he who rules his spirit than he who takes a city. — Proverbs 16:32
devotionals.title: Better Than a Warrior
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, grant me self-control. I want to rule my spirit before I try to rule anything else.

devotionals.reflection: There is no such thing as "luck" or "chance" in God's world. Even the "random" things are under His sovereign control.
devotionals.question: Can I trust that "unlucky" circumstances are actually part of God's "every decision"?
devotionals.scripture: The lot is cast into the lap, but its every decision is from the Lord. — Proverbs 16:33
devotionals.title: The Lot and the Lap
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I trust Your sovereignty over the "random" events of my life. Nothing surprises You.

devotionals.reflection: God uses "heat" (trials) to bring the impurities to the surface of our hearts. The "furnace" isn't for our destruction, but for our purification.
devotionals.question: What "heat" am I currently feeling, and what "dross" is God trying to remove from my heart?
devotionals.scripture: The crucible is for silver, and the furnace for gold, and the Lord tests hearts. — Proverbs 17:3
devotionals.title: Testing the Heart
devotionals.prayer: Father, refine me. Use the difficulties of my life to make me more like pure gold.

devotionals.question: Am I "repeating a matter" today that should be "covered" in love?
devotionals.reflection: Gossip is a friendship-killer. A wise friend knows how to "cover" a mistake with grace rather than "repeating" it to others.
devotionals.scripture: Whoever covers an offense seeks love, but he who repeats a matter separates close friends. — Proverbs 17:9
devotionals.title: Covering an Offense
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for covering my offenses. Help me to be a "covering" friend to those around me.

devotionals.reflection: Life isn't always fair, and sometimes good people get "fined" or "struck." But God sees the injustice and He is the ultimate Judge.
devotionals.question: How do I react when I am treated "unfairly" for doing the right thing?
devotionals.scripture: To impose a fine on a righteous man is not good, nor to strike the noble for their uprightness. — Proverbs 17:26
devotionals.title: Striking a Nobleman
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I trust Your justice. Help me to remain "noble" even when I am being treated poorly.

devotionals.reflection: A "cool spirit" is the opposite of "heated" emotion. Wisdom keeps the temperature low so that "understanding" can stay high.
devotionals.question: How "cool" is my spirit today? Am I letting my emotions dictate my words?
devotionals.scripture: Whoever restrains his words has knowledge, and he who has a cool spirit is a man of understanding. — Proverbs 17:27
devotionals.title: A Cool Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me a cool spirit. Help me to restrain my words and lead with understanding.

devotionals.question: Am I talking just to "fill the air," or can I be "silent" and listen?
devotionals.reflection: If you don't know what to say, say nothing! Silence is the best "camouflage" for a lack of wisdom.
devotionals.scripture: Even a fool who keeps silent is considered wise; when he closes his lips, he is deemed intelligent. — Proverbs 17:28
devotionals.title: Silence as Wisdom
devotionals.prayer: Lord, help me to know when to be silent. Let my words be few and full of Your truth.

devotionals.reflection: Isolation is a warning sign. When we pull away from godly community, we are usually trying to hide a selfish desire that "sound judgment" would stop.
devotionals.question: Am I "isolating" myself right now because I don't want to hear the truth from others?
devotionals.scripture: Whoever isolates himself seeks his own desire; he breaks out against all sound judgment. — Proverbs 18:1
devotionals.title: Seeking His Own Desire
devotionals.prayer: Father, keep me connected to Your people. Don't let my selfish desires lead me into isolation.

devotionals.reflection: When life gets overwhelming, we have a "Strong Tower." We don't have to build our own security; we just have to "run into" His.
devotionals.question: What "tower" am I trying to build for my own safety? Why not run to God instead?
devotionals.scripture: The name of the Lord is a strong tower; the righteous man runs into it and is safe. — Proverbs 18:10
devotionals.title: The Name of the Lord
devotionals.prayer: Lord, Your Name is my safety. I run to You today and find my security in Your presence.

devotionals.question: Is there any "haughtiness" in my heart today that is signaling a future fall?
devotionals.reflection: Haughtiness (pride) is the "rumbling" before the earthquake of destruction. If you want honor, you must first find the path of humility.
devotionals.scripture: Before destruction a man's heart is haughty, but humility comes before honor. — Proverbs 18:12
devotionals.title: Before the Fall
devotionals.prayer: Lord, humble me so that I don't have to be humiliated. I want Your honor, not my own.

devotionals.question: Am I "listening to understand" today, or just "waiting for my turn to speak"?
devotionals.reflection: We often "pre-load" our answers while people are still talking. Wisdom listens until the very end before it speaks.
devotionals.scripture: If one gives an answer before he hears, it is his folly and shame. — Proverbs 18:13
devotionals.title: Answering Before Hearing
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me ears that hear and a heart that waits. Let me not be shamed by my "hasty" answers.
