devotionals.title: Servant Greatness
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, give me Your towel. Help me to serve others with the same humility You showed.

devotionals.question: Am I trying to look "clean" for Jesus, or am I letting Him wash the parts of me that are messy?
devotionals.reflection: We must be willing to let Jesus see our "dirty feet" (our failures) and wash them. Vulnerability is the key to intimacy.
devotionals.scripture: If I do not wash you, you have no share with me. — John 13:8
devotionals.title: A Share with Him
devotionals.prayer: Lord, wash me. I bring my mess to You and ask for Your cleansing grace.

devotionals.reflection: Christianity is an "imitation" of a Person, not just the "following" of a list. We do to others what He did to us.
devotionals.question: How would I treat my "enemies" today if I followed the example of Jesus washing Judas' feet?
devotionals.scripture: For I have given you an example... — John 13:15
devotionals.title: As I Have Done
devotionals.prayer: Lord, help me to love as You loved. Give me the grace to serve even those who might hurt me.

devotionals.reflection: Our "badge" isn't a cross around our neck; it's the love in our hearts. Kindness is the ultimate apologetic for the Gospel.
devotionals.question: Is my "love" for other Christians visible enough for an unbeliever to notice?
devotionals.scripture: By this all people will know that you are my disciples, if you have love for one another. — John 13:35
devotionals.title: The Witness of Love
devotionals.prayer: Father, knit Your people together. Help us to love each other so well that the world takes notice.

devotionals.question: Does the reality of "Heaven" make my current "earthly troubles" seem smaller?
devotionals.reflection: We aren't just "wandering" through life. We are headed toward a specific, prepared home. This world is just the waiting room.
devotionals.scripture: I go to prepare a place for you. — John 14:2
devotionals.title: Prepared Place
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for preparing a home for me. Help me to live with my bags packed and my heart ready.

devotionals.reflection: In a world of "many paths," Jesus is the anchor. He doesn't just "show" the way; He is the way.
devotionals.question: When I am lost, do I look for a "map" or do I look for the "Person" of Jesus?
devotionals.scripture: I am the way, and the truth, and the life. — John 14:6
devotionals.title: The Only Way
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, be my Way today. I trust Your Truth and I rely on Your Life.

devotionals.question: How often will I "check in" with the Holy Spirit today before I speak or act?
devotionals.reflection: We have the "Spirit of Truth" living inside our very skin. We are never truly alone, and we are never without counsel.
devotionals.scripture: I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Helper... — John 14:16
devotionals.title: The Helper Within
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, thank You for being my internal Guide. Help me to be sensitive to Your nudges.

devotionals.reflection: An orphan has no protection and no inheritance. You have both. You are a child of the King, fully claimed and fully loved.
devotionals.question: Do I have an "orphan spirit" (worried about survival) or a "son/daughter spirit" (resting in my Father's wealth)?
devotionals.scripture: I will not leave you as orphans; I will come to you. — John 14:18
devotionals.title: Not an Orphan
devotionals.prayer: Father, I am Your child. I rest in Your provision and Your protection today.

devotionals.question: Will I "let" my heart be troubled today, or will I "receive" the peace He has already left for me?
devotionals.reflection: Jesus' peace isn't the "absence of trouble," but the "presence of God" in the trouble. It is a gift we must choose to receive.
devotionals.scripture: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. — John 14:27
devotionals.title: Real Peace
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I receive Your peace. I refuse to be afraid, because You are with me.

devotionals.reflection: This is the ultimate "power-off" statement. Any effort we make without Jesus is spiritually zero. Staying connected is our primary job.
devotionals.question: How much of my "striving" today is actually just "branch-effort" instead of "vine-life"?
devotionals.scripture: Apart from me you can do nothing. — John 15:5
devotionals.title: Abiding in the Vine
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I stay connected to You. Let Your life flow through me and produce fruit naturally.

devotionals.reflection: The standard of our love is the cross. We don't love others because they deserve it; we love them because He loved us first.
devotionals.question: Who is the "hardest" person for me to love today, and will I love them with "Jesus-love"?
devotionals.scripture: This is my commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you. — John 15:12
devotionals.title: Love Each Other
devotionals.prayer: Father, help me to love the unlovable. Give me Your heart for the people around me.

devotionals.reflection: The Gospels were just the "beginning." Jesus is still acting and teaching today—but now He does it through you by His Spirit.
devotionals.question: Do I realize that my life is a continuation of the "Acts of Jesus" in the world today?
devotionals.scripture: In the first book... I have dealt with all that Jesus began to do and teach... — Acts 1:1
devotionals.title: The Continuing Work
devotionals.prayer: Lord, continue Your work through me. Speak through my mouth and move through my hands today.

devotionals.question: Am I asking for God's power just to feel better, or to be a more effective witness for Him?
devotionals.reflection: The Spirit’s power isn't for our entertainment; it’s for our assignment. We are empowered specifically to be "witnesses."
devotionals.scripture: But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you... — Acts 1:8a
devotionals.title: Power for a Purpose
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, fall on me today. Give me the power I need to represent Jesus in my world.

devotionals.reflection: God’s vision is global. He cares about your neighbor and the person on the other side of the planet equally.
devotionals.question: How can I broaden my perspective today to care about what God is doing globally?
devotionals.scripture: ...and you will be my witnesses... to the end of the earth. — Acts 1:8b
devotionals.title: To the End of the Earth
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me a heart for the nations. Use my prayers and my resources to reach the ends of the earth.

devotionals.question: Am I so focused on the "future" return of Christ that I’m neglecting the "present" mission of Christ?
devotionals.reflection: It’s easy to get caught up in "spiritual gazing," but the angels reminded the disciples that there is work to be done while we wait for His return.
devotionals.scripture: Men of Galilee, why do you stand looking into heaven? — Acts 1:11
devotionals.title: Looking Up vs. Going Out
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I look forward to Your return, but help me to be busy with Your work until that day comes.

devotionals.question: Is there any "discord" in my heart toward another believer that is blocking the Spirit’s flow?
devotionals.reflection: Before the fire fell, there was unity. Power flows where there is peace and shared purpose among believers.
devotionals.scripture: All these with one accord were devoting themselves to prayer... — Acts 1:14
devotionals.title: One Accord
devotionals.prayer: Lord, knit my heart to my brothers and sisters. Help us to pray with one accord for Your glory.

devotionals.question: If God called me to step into a new role of leadership today, would my "accompaniment" with Jesus have prepared me for it?
devotionals.reflection: God’s work continues even when leaders fail. He always has a "Matthias" ready to step up and fill the gap.
devotionals.scripture: ...it is necessary that one of the men who have accompanied us... become a witness to his resurrection. — Acts 1:21-22
devotionals.title: Choosing a Replacement
devotionals.prayer: Father, help me to stay close to You so that I am always ready for whatever task You assign.

devotionals.reflection: The Spirit doesn't always come in a whisper; sometimes He breaks in with the force of a gale to shake us out of our comfort.
devotionals.question: Am I open to God "disrupting" my quiet, controlled life with the wind of His Spirit?
devotionals.scripture: Suddenly there came from heaven a sound like a mighty rushing wind... — Acts 2:2
devotionals.title: The Sound of Heaven
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, blow through my life. Move the stagnant air and bring the fresh breath of heaven.

devotionals.reflection: In the Old Testament, the fire stayed on the Tabernacle. Now, the fire rests on people. You are the new dwelling place of God.
devotionals.question: Do I treat my body and my life with the reverence due to a place where God's "fire" rests?
devotionals.scripture: And divided tongues as of fire appeared to them and rested on each one of them. — Acts 2:3
devotionals.title: Tongues of Fire
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let Your fire burn in me. Cleanse me and use me as Your living temple.

devotionals.question: When was the last time I spoke out loud about the "mighty works of God" to someone else?
devotionals.reflection: The first thing the Spirit did was enable people to talk about how great God is. A Spirit-filled life is a God-bragging life.
devotionals.scripture: ...we hear them telling in our own tongues the mighty works of God. — Acts 2:11
devotionals.title: Speaking the Wonders
devotionals.prayer: Father, fill my mouth with Your praise. Help me to tell others how wonderful You are.

devotionals.question: Does my joy in Christ look "odd" to the world, or do I blend in too perfectly?
devotionals.reflection: The world often mistakes spiritual joy for something worldly. The Spirit provides a "sober intoxication"—pure joy with total clarity.
devotionals.scripture: For these people are not drunk, as you suppose... — Acts 2:15
devotionals.title: Not Drunk, but Filled
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, give me a joy that requires an explanation. Let Your Spirit be my only "influence."

devotionals.question: Am I underestimating what God can do through me because of my age, gender, or status?
devotionals.reflection: The Spirit is no longer for the "elite" few. He is for everyone—young, old, male, female. You are included in this outpouring.
devotionals.scripture: ...I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh; your sons and your daughters shall prophesy... — Acts 2:17
devotionals.title: The Prophecy of Joel
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for including me. Pour Your Spirit out on me and speak through me today.

devotionals.reflection: Salvation isn't a complex ritual; it’s a cry for help. If you can call His name, you can find His grace.
devotionals.question: Who do I know that needs to be reminded that "calling on His name" is enough to start?
devotionals.scripture: And it shall come to pass that everyone who calls upon the name of the Lord shall be saved. — Acts 2:21
devotionals.title: Calling on the Name
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I call on Your name today. Save me from my fears, my sins, and my self-reliance.

devotionals.question: Can I trust that the "chaotic" parts of my life are still within God's "definite plan"?
devotionals.reflection: Even the worst day in history (the crucifixion) was part of God’s "definite plan." He can use even the "wrong" things to make things "right."
devotionals.scripture: ...this Jesus, delivered up according to the definite plan and foreknowledge of God... — Acts 2:23
devotionals.title: According to the Plan
devotionals.prayer: Father, I trust Your sovereignty. Even when I don't understand, I believe You are working for good.

devotionals.question: How does the "impossibility" of death holding Jesus give me hope for my own future?
devotionals.reflection: Death had no "grip" on Jesus because He was sinless. Since we are in Him, death no longer has a permanent grip on us either.
devotionals.scripture: God raised him up, loosing the pangs of death, because it was not possible for him to be held by it. — Acts 2:24
devotionals.title: Loosing the Pangs of Death
devotionals.prayer: King Jesus, You conquered the grave. I thank You for the life that death cannot touch.

devotionals.question: Is my "tongue" rejoicing today, or am I letting my complaints lead the way?
devotionals.reflection: Real faith moves from the heart to the tongue. If the heart is glad in God, the words will eventually follow.
devotionals.scripture: ...therefore my heart was glad, and my tongue rejoiced... — Acts 2:26
devotionals.title: Gladness of Heart
devotionals.prayer: Lord, gladden my heart. Let my words today be a reflection of Your goodness to me.

devotionals.reflection: Jesus isn't just "risen"; He is "reigning." He is in the position of ultimate power and authority over the universe.
devotionals.question: When I feel overwhelmed by the world's news, do I remember who is sitting at the "Right Hand"?
devotionals.scripture: Being therefore exalted at the right hand of God... — Acts 2:33
devotionals.title: Exalted at the Right Hand
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I acknowledge Your reign. You are the Lord of all, and I submit my day to Your authority.

devotionals.question: Will I allow God's Word to "cut" me today, or will I keep my armor up?
devotionals.reflection: The Word of God is a sword. Sometimes it hurts when it cuts through our pride, but that "wound" is the only way for grace to get in.
devotionals.scripture: Now when they heard this they were cut to the heart... — Acts 2:37
devotionals.title: Cut to the Heart
devotionals.prayer: Lord, search me. If there is pride or sin, cut it away so that I can be healed and whole.

devotionals.reflection: Faith requires a "turn" (repentance) and a "testimony" (baptism). It is a public commitment to a new direction.
devotionals.question: Is there any area of my life where I need to "repent"—to turn around and go God’s way?
devotionals.scripture: And Peter said to them, 'Repent and be baptized every one of you...' — Acts 2:38
devotionals.title: Repent and Be Baptized
devotionals.prayer: Father, I turn to You. Forgive my wandering and lead me in the way of life.

devotionals.reflection: God isn't just the God of the past; He is the God of your children’s future. His grace has a long reach.
devotionals.question: Am I praying for the "far off" people in my life, trusting that the promise is for them too?
devotionals.scripture: For the promise is for you and for your children and for all who are far off... — Acts 2:39
devotionals.title: The Promise is for You
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You that Your grace reaches across generations. I claim Your promise for my family today.

devotionals.question: Am I "devoted" to learning God's Word, or is it just something I fit in when I have time?
devotionals.reflection: The early church didn't start with programs; they started with the Word. Devotion to Truth is the bedrock of a healthy life.
devotionals.scripture: And they devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching... — Acts 2:42a
devotionals.title: Devoted to the Word
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me a hunger for Your Truth. Let Your Word be the foundation of my thoughts today.

devotionals.question: Who can I "break bread" with this week to encourage them in their faith?
devotionals.reflection: We weren't meant to walk alone. Faith is sustained through shared meals, shared lives, and shared prayers.
devotionals.scripture: ...and the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers. — Acts 2:42b
devotionals.title: The Fellowship
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for the gift of the Church. Help me to be an active and loving part of Your body.

devotionals.question: Have I lost my sense of "awe" for God? How can I recover it today?
devotionals.reflection: When God is moving, there is a sense of "awe." It’s a holy respect for the presence of the Almighty.
devotionals.scripture: And awe came upon every soul, and many wonders and signs were being done... — Acts 2:43
devotionals.title: Awe and Wonders
devotionals.prayer: Lord, restore my wonder. Help me to see Your greatness in the "signs" you are doing all around me.

devotionals.question: Is there something I "own" that God might want me to share with someone in need today?
devotionals.reflection: The Spirit-filled life is a generous life. When we realize everything belongs to God, we stop clutching our "stuff" so tightly.
devotionals.scripture: And all who believed were together and had all things in common. — Acts 2:44
devotionals.title: All Things in Common
devotionals.prayer: Father, everything I have is Yours. Open my hands to give as freely as You have given to me.

devotionals.reflection: You can eat a simple meal with "gladness" if Jesus is at the table. Contentment is a fruit of the Spirit, not a result of wealth.
devotionals.question: Can I find "gladness" in my current circumstances today, simply because I am with Christ?
devotionals.scripture: ...they received their food with glad and generous hearts... — Acts 2:46
devotionals.title: Glad and Generous Hearts
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, be the guest at my table. Let Your joy make my simple life feel like a feast.

devotionals.question: Am I living in a way that makes the Gospel "attractive" to those who don't know Him yet?
devotionals.reflection: Good character and genuine joy are attractive. When we live for God, He often grants us favor with those around us.
devotionals.scripture: ...praising god and having favor with all the people. — Acts 2:47
devotionals.title: Favor with All People
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me favor. Let my life be a "praise" to You that draws others toward Your light.

devotionals.question: Am I trying to "force" results, or am I trusting the Lord to "add" in His timing?
devotionals.reflection: We do the "praising" and "fellowshipping," but God does the "adding." We don't have to manufacture growth; we just have to be faithful.
devotionals.scripture: And the Lord added to their number day by day those who were being saved. — Acts 2:47
devotionals.title: Adding to the Number
devotionals.prayer: Father, I trust You with the results. I will be faithful to the work, and I leave the growth to You.

devotionals.reflection: Even Spirit-filled apostles kept their "appointment" with God. Consistency in prayer is the fuel for a life of power.
devotionals.question: Do I have a set "hour of prayer" today, or am I just hoping to find a few minutes?
devotionals.scripture: Now Peter and John were going up to the temple at the hour of prayer... — Acts 2:3:1
devotionals.title: The Hour of Prayer
devotionals.prayer: Lord, help me to be disciplined in my pursuit of You. I want to keep my appointments with Your Spirit.

devotionals.question: Who is "sitting by the gate" in my life that I have been walking past without looking?
devotionals.reflection: Before Peter could heal the man, he had to give him his full attention. Ministry starts with truly seeing people.
devotionals.scripture: And Peter directed his gaze at him, as did John, and said, 'Look at us.' — Acts 3:4
devotionals.title: Look at Us
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, give me Your eyes for people. Help me to stop and truly see those who are hurting.

devotionals.question: Am I making excuses about my lack of resources instead of using the "Power of the Name" I’ve been given?
devotionals.reflection: We often focus on what we don't have. God wants us to focus on what we do have: His Name and His Power.
devotionals.scripture: I have no silver and gold, but what I do have I give to you. — Acts 3:6
devotionals.title: Such as I Have
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for the Name of Jesus. I may not have much, but I have Him—and that is enough.

devotionals.reflection: The name of Jesus isn't a magic spell; it’s a legal authority. When we speak in His name, the "Owner" of the universe backs it up.
devotionals.question: Do I speak the name of Jesus with confidence, or with a "hope-so" attitude?
devotionals.scripture: In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, rise up and walk! — Acts 3:6
devotionals.title: In the Name of Jesus
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I stand in Your authority today. In Your name, I speak peace to my heart and strength to my life.

devotionals.question: Is there any "leaping" in my walk with God today, or have I become spiritually sluggish?
devotionals.reflection: When God heals, the response is unmistakable. A "leaping" heart is the sign of a soul that has been set free.
devotionals.scripture: And leaping up, he stood and began to walk... praising God. — Acts 3:8
devotionals.title: Leaping and Praising
devotionals.prayer: Lord, touch my life again. Give me a reason to leap for joy and praise Your name.

devotionals.reflection: Peter was quick to deflect the glory. We are just the "wires"; He is the "Electricity." Never mistake the tool for the Craftsman.
devotionals.question: If God uses me today, will I be quick to point the glory back to Him?
devotionals.scripture: Why do you stare at us, as though by our own power or piety we have made him walk? — Acts 3:12
devotionals.title: By Our Own Power?
devotionals.prayer: Father, use me, but keep me humble. Let all the "staring" and "praising" go to Jesus alone.

devotionals.question: Since Jesus is the "Author" of my life, will I let Him hold the pen today?
devotionals.reflection: You can't "kill" Life itself. Death was outmatched from the start. Because Jesus is the "Author," He gets to write the final chapter of your story too.
devotionals.scripture: ...and you killed the Author of Life, whom God raised from the dead. — Acts 3:15
devotionals.title: The Author of Life
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I hand You the pen. Write Your story through my life, even the parts that involve "revisions."

devotionals.question: Do I really believe in the power of Jesus' name, or is it just a way I end my prayers?
devotionals.reflection: It wasn't just the name; it was "faith in the name." Belief is the bridge that brings heaven’s power into earth’s problems.
devotionals.scripture: And his name—by faith in his name—has made this man strong... — Acts 3:16
devotionals.title: Faith in the Name
devotionals.prayer: Lord, increase my faith in Your name. I trust that Your name is higher than any problem I face.

devotionals.reflection: Repentance isn't just about "stopping sin"; it’s about "starting refreshment." God's presence is where we find our second wind.
devotionals.question: Do I need a "time of refreshing" today? Will I turn toward His presence to find it?
devotionals.scripture: Repent therefore... that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord. — Acts 3:19-20
devotionals.title: Times of Refreshing
devotionals.prayer: Father, I turn to You. Refresh my soul in Your presence and wash away my weariness.

devotionals.reflection: Turning from sin is actually a "blessing." God doesn't take away our sin to be a "killjoy," but to make us "joy-filled."
devotionals.question: Can I see God's "No" to sin as a "Yes" to my ultimate blessing?
devotionals.scripture: God... sent him to you first, to bless you by turning every one of you from your wickedness. — Acts 3:26
devotionals.title: To Bless You
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for the blessing of a clean heart. Help me to turn away from anything that steals my joy.

devotionals.question: Am I willing to be "annoying" to the world if it means being faithful to the Truth?
devotionals.reflection: The world is often "annoyed" by the hope of the Gospel. Light always irritates eyes that are used to the dark.
devotionals.scripture: ...greatly annoyed because they were teaching the people and proclaiming in Jesus the resurrection... — Acts 4:2
devotionals.title: Annoyed by the Truth
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, give me a thick skin and a tender heart. Let me speak the truth even when it’s unpopular.

devotionals.reflection: You can jail the preacher, but you can't jail the Word. Opposition often only serves to spread the message further.
devotionals.question: Am I discouraged by "opposition," or do I see that God's Word is still working in spite of it?
devotionals.scripture: But many of those who had heard the word believed... — Acts 4:4
devotionals.title: But Many Believed
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You that Your Word is unchained. Use even my "setbacks" to advance Your Kingdom.

devotionals.question: Am I trying to stand in my own "name" (reputation) or in the "Name of Jesus"?
devotionals.reflection: The world is obsessed with "credentials." The Apostles’ only credential was a Name. That Name is still our only valid "ID" in the spiritual realm.
devotionals.scripture: ...by what power or by what name did you do this? — Acts 4:7
devotionals.title: By What Name?
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I hide behind Your name. My only authority and my only identity is found in You.

devotionals.reflection: Spirit-filling isn't a one-time event; it’s a repeatable experience for specific moments of need. We can ask for a "fresh filling" for today's tasks.
devotionals.question: Do I need a "fresh filling" of the Spirit to handle a difficult conversation or task today?
devotionals.scripture: Then Peter, filled with the Holy Spirit, said to them... — Acts 4:8
devotionals.title: Filled with the Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, fill me now. Give me the wisdom and the words I need for this very hour.

devotionals.reflection: What the "experts" rejected, God made the most important part. God loves to use the "discards" to hold everything together.
devotionals.question: Have I been feeling like a "rejected stone"? Will I let the Master Builder put me where I belong?
devotionals.scripture: This Jesus is the stone that was rejected... which has become the cornerstone. — Acts 4:11
devotionals.title: The Cornerstone
devotionals.prayer: Lord, You are my cornerstone. Build my life on Your strength and use me for Your structure.

devotionals.question: Am I looking for "salvation" (peace, worth) in other names, or am I settled in Jesus?
devotionals.reflection: Jesus isn't a "suggested" path; He is the only rescue. This gives us great confidence—there is no need to look anywhere else.
devotionals.scripture: And there is salvation in no one else, for there is no other name... — Acts 4:12
devotionals.title: No Other Name
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are my only Savior. I stop searching elsewhere and I rest entirely in You.

devotionals.reflection: You don't need a degree to look like Christ; you just need to spend time with Him. His "fragrance" rubs off on those who stay close.
devotionals.question: Will people recognize today that I have "been with Jesus" by the way I talk and act?
devotionals.scripture: ...they recognized that they had been with Jesus. — Acts 4:13
devotionals.title: They Had Been with Jesus
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let the "scent" of Your presence stay on me today. May I reflect Your heart to everyone I meet.

devotionals.reflection: When you truly encounter the Living God, you don't need "training" to talk about Him. You just can't keep it in!
devotionals.question: Is my heart so full of Jesus today that His name "leaks out" of my conversation?
devotionals.scripture: ...for we cannot but speak of what we have seen and heard. — Acts 4:20
devotionals.title: We Cannot But Speak
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, fill me so much that I "cannot but speak" of Your goodness. Let my life be an overflow of Your grace.

devotionals.question: Does my prayer life start with my "problems" or with God's "Sovereignty"?
devotionals.reflection: When the Apostles were threatened, they didn't pray for "safety" first; they prayed to the "Sovereign Lord." Perspective changes everything.
devotionals.scripture: Sovereign Lord, who made the heaven and the earth... — Acts 4:24
devotionals.title: Sovereignty in the Storm
devotionals.prayer: Sovereign Lord, You made it all. I start my day by acknowledging Your power over every threat.

devotionals.reflection: Human rebellion against God is "in vain." It’s like throwing pebbles at a mountain. God's purposes will always stand.
devotionals.question: Why am I worrying about the "plots" of the world when I know they are "in vain" before God?
devotionals.scripture: Why did the Gentiles rage, and the peoples plot in vain? — Acts 4:25
devotionals.title: Why Do the Nations Rage?
devotionals.prayer: Father, I trust Your plan. Let the world rage; I will rest in Your peace.

devotionals.question: Am I praying for "easier circumstances" or for "more boldness" in my current ones?
devotionals.reflection: They didn't pray for the threats to stop; they prayed for the courage to continue. Boldness is a gift we must ask for.
devotionals.scripture: ...grant to your servants to continue to speak your word with all boldness. — Acts 4:29
devotionals.title: Grant to Your Servants
devotionals.prayer: Lord, don't necessarily take away the challenge, but give me the boldness to speak Your Word in the middle of it.

devotionals.question: Am I "speaking" enough today for God to have something to "stretch out His hand" toward?
devotionals.reflection: We speak the Word; God stretches out His hand. It’s a partnership of faith. We do the "speaking," He does the "stretching."
devotionals.scripture: ...while you stretch out your hand to heal... — Acts 4:30
devotionals.title: Stretch Out Your Hand
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I’ll speak. I ask You to stretch out Your hand and do what only You can do.

devotionals.reflection: Prayer moves the heart of God, and sometimes it moves the very ground we stand on. A praying church is a powerful church.
devotionals.question: Is my prayer life "shaking" anything lately, or has it become just a formal routine?
devotionals.scripture: And when they had prayed, the place... was shaken, and they were all filled... — Acts 4:31
devotionals.title: Shaken and Filled
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, shake me out of my apathy. Fill me again with Your power and Your purpose.

devotionals.question: How can I contribute to the "oneness" of my family or church community today?
devotionals.reflection: Unity isn't just "agreeing"; it’s sharing the same "soul" and "heart." It’s a supernatural bond that the Spirit creates.
devotionals.scripture: Now the full number of those who believed were of one heart and soul... — Acts 4:32
devotionals.title: One Heart and Soul
devotionals.prayer: Father, make us one. Remove the selfishness that causes division and give us one heart.

devotionals.question: Will I walk today in the "great grace" that Jesus provides for His people?
devotionals.reflection: Great grace is more than enough for "great trials." There is a specific kind of favor that rests on a unified, praying community.
devotionals.scripture: ...and great grace was upon them all. — Acts 4:33
devotionals.title: Great Grace
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let Your great grace be upon me. May it be visible to everyone I interact with today.

devotionals.reflection: The early church took care of their own. Spiritual health is always reflected in how we handle our material wealth.
devotionals.question: Who around me has a "need" that I have the "resource" to meet today?
devotionals.scripture: There was not a needy person among them... — Acts 4:34
devotionals.title: No Needy Person
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, help me to see needs as opportunities to show Your love. Make me a channel of Your provision.

devotionals.reflection: Barnabas was known for what he gave (encouragement). What "nickname" would people give me based on my primary character trait?
devotionals.question: Could I be a "Barnabas" to someone today who is feeling discouraged or lonely?
devotionals.scripture: ...Joseph, who was also called by the apostles Barnabas (which means son of encouragement). — Acts 4:36
devotionals.title: Son of Encouragement
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me a "Son/Daughter of Encouragement." Let my words build others up and give them hope.

devotionals.question: Is there any "pretending" in my spiritual life right now? Will I be honest with God today?
devotionals.reflection: God isn't looking for "perfect" people, but He is looking for "honest" ones. Pretending to be more "holy" than we are is a dangerous game.
devotionals.scripture: Ananias, why has Satan filled your heart to lie to the Holy Spirit...? — Acts 5:3
devotionals.title: Lying to the Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, I want to be real with You. Search my heart and remove all hypocrisy and pretense.

devotionals.reflection: We often worry about what people think, but our primary accountability is to the Creator. All sin is ultimately against Him.
devotionals.question: How would my choices change today if I remembered that every action is done "before God"?
devotionals.scripture: You have not lied to man but to God. — Acts 5:4
devotionals.title: Not to Man, but to God
devotionals.prayer: Father, help me to live with a healthy "fear of the Lord"—a deep reverence for Your presence in my life.

devotionals.question: Do I treat my relationship with God with "holy awe," or have I become too casual with His holiness?
devotionals.reflection: Fear here is a holy awe. It’s the realization that God is holy and not to be trifled with. Holiness is the beauty of the Church.
devotionals.scripture: And great fear came upon the whole church and upon all who heard... — Acts 5:11
devotionals.title: Great Fear
devotionals.prayer: Holy God, I worship You. Let Your holiness be reflected in my life and in Your Church.

devotionals.question: What "wonder" (kindness, help, prayer) can God do through my hands today?
devotionals.reflection: God uses human hands to do His work. You are His "hands" in your workplace, your home, and your community today.
devotionals.scripture: Now many signs and wonders were regularly done... by the hands of the apostles. — Acts 5:12
devotionals.title: Hands of the Apostles
devotionals.prayer: Lord, take my hands and use them. May my actions be a "sign" of Your love to someone else.

devotionals.question: Do I believe that God can still "add believers" in my community, even in a difficult culture?
devotionals.reflection: Even in the face of judgment and fear, the Church grew. God’s Truth is unstoppable when it is lived out in purity.
devotionals.scripture: And more than ever believers were added to the Lord... — Acts 5:14
devotionals.title: More Than Ever
devotionals.prayer: Father, add to Your Kingdom. Use our purity and our passion to draw people to Yourself.

devotionals.reflection: This isn't about Peter’s shadow; it’s about the "overflow" of God's presence. When you are full of God, your very "atmosphere" changes.
devotionals.question: What kind of "shadow" (influence) am I casting on the people I walk past today?
devotionals.scripture: ...they even carried out the sick... that as Peter came by at least his shadow might fall on some of them. — Acts 5:15
devotionals.title: Shadows of Grace
devotionals.prayer: Lord, fill me so much that my mere presence brings peace and hope to others.

devotionals.reflection: When human laws clash with God's commands, our loyalty is clear. We serve a higher Throne.
devotionals.question: Is there a "man-pleasing" habit I need to break so I can be fully obedient to God?
devotionals.scripture: But Peter and the apostles answered, 'We must obey God rather than men.' — Acts 5:29
devotionals.title: Obeying God Over Men
devotionals.prayer: King of Kings, I choose to obey You first. Give me the courage to follow Your Word, no matter the cost.

devotionals.question: Does my reputation at work and home reflect a life "full of the Spirit"?
devotionals.reflection: Even "practical" tasks in the church require spiritual depth. God values a good reputation and a wise heart, regardless of the job title.
devotionals.scripture: Pick out... seven men of good repute, full of the Spirit and of wisdom. — Acts 6:3
devotionals.title: Good Repute
devotionals.prayer: Lord, build my character. May I be a person of integrity and wisdom in every task I do.

devotionals.reflection: To lead others, we must first be led by God. Leaders must protect their time with the Lord to have anything worth giving to people.
devotionals.question: Am I protecting my "inner circle" of time with God, or letting the "busyness" of ministry crowd it out?
devotionals.scripture: But we will devote ourselves to prayer and to the ministry of the word. — Acts 6:4
devotionals.title: Prayer and the Word
devotionals.prayer: Father, help me keep first things first. Let my service flow from my devotion.

devotionals.reflection: Grace and power are a potent combination. Grace opens the door, and power does the work. Stephen didn't have one without the other.
devotionals.question: Do I lead with "grace" when I use the "power" or talents God has given me?
devotionals.scripture: And Stephen, full of grace and power, was doing great wonders... — Acts 6:8
devotionals.title: Full of Grace and Power
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, fill me with Your grace so that Your power can work through me without pride.

devotionals.question: Does my "face" show the peace of God when I am under pressure or being criticized?
devotionals.reflection: Even under false accusation, Stephen had a supernatural peace. When you are focused on God's glory, the world's attacks can't dim your light.
devotionals.scripture: ...they saw that his face was like the face of an angel. — Acts 6:15
devotionals.title: Face of an Angel
devotionals.prayer: Lord, keep my eyes fixed on You. Let Your peace be visible to those who oppose me.

devotionals.question: Is there any area where I am currently "stiff-necked" toward God's direction?
devotionals.reflection: Resistance isn't always shouting; sometimes it's just a "stiff neck" that refuses to bow. A soft heart is the Spirit's favorite workshop.
devotionals.scripture: You stiff-necked people... you always resist the Holy Spirit. — Acts 7:51
devotionals.title: Resisting the Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, soften my heart. I choose to yield to Your nudge rather than resist it.

devotionals.reflection: Stephen looked past the stones and saw the Son. Our perspective determines our endurance.
devotionals.question: Am I looking at the "stones" being thrown at me, or at the Savior standing for me?
devotionals.scripture: But he, full of the Holy Spirit, gazed into heaven and saw the glory of God... — Acts 7:55
devotionals.title: Seeing the Glory
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I look to You. Show me Your glory so that the trials of this world fade away.

devotionals.question: How does it change my day to know that Jesus is personally attentive to my struggles?
devotionals.reflection: Usually, Jesus is described as sitting in authority. Here, He stands—perhaps to welcome His first martyr home. He notices your sacrifice.
devotionals.scripture: ...and Jesus standing at the right hand of God. — Acts 7:56
devotionals.title: Standing at the Right Hand
devotionals.prayer: Savior, thank You for standing for me. I find my strength in Your personal care.

devotionals.reflection: Stephen died exactly like Jesus lived: forgiving His enemies. The ultimate proof of the Spirit is the power to love those who hurt us.
devotionals.question: Is there someone I need to release from the "debt" of their sin against me today?
devotionals.scripture: And falling to his knees he cried out... 'Lord, do not hold this sin against them.' — Acts 7:60
devotionals.title: Do Not Hold This Sin
devotionals.prayer: Father, forgive them. Don't let bitterness take root in my heart. Fill me with Your mercy.

devotionals.reflection: The story looks like it's ending in defeat, but God was already setting the stage for the conversion of the man who would write half the New Testament.
devotionals.question: Can I trust God's plan even when it looks like the "bad guys" are winning?
devotionals.scripture: And Saul approved of his execution. — Acts 8:1
devotionals.title: Saul’s Approval
devotionals.prayer: Sovereign Lord, nothing is outside Your reach. I trust that You can turn even an enemy into a brother.

devotionals.question: Is my current "disruption" actually a "distribution" of God's grace to a new place?
devotionals.reflection: Persecution didn't stop the Church; it just "unpacked" it. What looked like a disaster was actually a distribution strategy.
devotionals.scripture: And they were all scattered... those who were scattered went about preaching the word. — Acts 8:1, 4
devotionals.title: Scattered for Growth
devotionals.prayer: Lord, use my "scattered" moments. Wherever I am sent, let me bring Your Word with me.

devotionals.reflection: When the Gospel arrives, joy follows. Healing and hope are the "calling cards" of the Kingdom of God.
devotionals.question: Does my presence in my city/neighborhood bring "much joy" because of the Gospel I carry?
devotionals.scripture: So there was much joy in that city. — Acts 8:8
devotionals.title: Great Joy in That City
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, make me a bringer of joy. Let the "city" around me be better because You are working through me.

devotionals.reflection: Simon wanted the Spirit's power for his own ego. God sees past our "religious" activity straight into our motives.
devotionals.question: Am I seeking God's power for His glory, or for my own reputation?
devotionals.scripture: But Peter said to him... 'your heart is not right before God.' — Acts 8:20-21
devotionals.title: Heart Not Right
devotionals.prayer: Lord, purify my heart. I don't want Your gifts for my gain; I want them for Your Kingdom.

devotionals.reflection: Philip left a massive revival to talk to one man in a desert. God values the "one" as much as the "thousands."
devotionals.question: Am I sensitive enough to the Spirit's nudge to "leave the crowd" for a single soul?
devotionals.scripture: And the Spirit said to Philip, 'Go over and join this chariot.' — Acts 8:29
devotionals.title: The Nudge of the Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me ears to hear Your whispers. Lead me to the "chariots" You want me to join today.

devotionals.reflection: Ministry often starts with a simple, curious question. We don't have to preach a sermon; we just have to start a conversation.
devotionals.question: Who can I ask a "curiosity question" to today to open a door for the Gospel?
devotionals.scripture: So Philip ran to him and heard him reading... and said, 'Do you understand what you are reading?' — Acts 8:30
devotionals.title: Do You Understand?
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me the right questions. Help me to engage people with kindness and interest.

devotionals.reflection: Every part of the Bible is a road that leads to Jesus. We just have to know how to follow the signs.
devotionals.question: Do I know the Word well enough to show someone how it points to Jesus?
devotionals.scripture: Then Philip opened his mouth, and beginning with this Scripture he told him the good news... — Acts 8:35
devotionals.title: Starting from This Scripture
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, teach me the Word. Help me to see Jesus on every page so I can show Him to others.

devotionals.question: Am I putting "barriers" in front of people that God hasn't put there?
devotionals.reflection: When someone's heart is ready, they don't want to wait. The Gospel is for anyone, anywhere, at any time.
devotionals.scripture: See, here is water! What prevents me from being baptized? — Acts 8:36
devotionals.title: Searching for Jesus
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for Your radical accessibility. Help me to welcome anyone who is seeking You.

devotionals.question: Who have I "given up on" that God is still pursuing?
devotionals.reflection: Saul was at his most hateful right before he met Love Himself. No one is "too far gone" for a Damascus Road encounter.
devotionals.scripture: But Saul, still breathing threats and murder against the disciples... — Acts 9:1
devotionals.title: Breathing Threats
devotionals.prayer: Lord, have mercy on those who "breathe threats." Intercept them with Your grace just as You did for Saul.

devotionals.question: Do I realize how closely Jesus identifies with my pain and my struggles?
devotionals.reflection: Jesus didn't say "Why are you hurting the church?" He said "Why are you hurting Me?" To touch a believer is to touch Jesus.
devotionals.scripture: ...he heard a voice saying to him, 'Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?' — Acts 9:4
devotionals.title: Why Do You Persecute Me?
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for Your deep connection to me. It gives me courage to know You are with me in every battle.

devotionals.question: Is my relationship with God based on "Religion" or on the Person of "Jesus"?
devotionals.reflection: Saul knew about "God," but he didn't know "Jesus." Everything changes when the "Deity" we fear becomes the "Savior" we love.
devotionals.scripture: And he said, 'Who are you, Lord?' And he said, 'I am Jesus...' — Acts 9:5
devotionals.title: I Am Jesus
devotionals.prayer: Lord Jesus, reveal Yourself to me more clearly. I want to know You, not just about You.

devotionals.question: Am I willing to be "led by the hand" by others when God makes me realize my own blindness?
devotionals.reflection: The "mighty" Pharisee became a "blind" man who had to be led. God often humbles us so that He can truly lead us.
devotionals.scripture: Saul rose from the ground... so they led him by the hand... — Acts 9:8
devotionals.title: Led by the Hand
devotionals.prayer: Father, I surrender my pride. I am willing to be small so that You can be great in me.

devotionals.reflection: Ananias had to overcome his fear to call his enemy "Brother." Grace turns "them" into "us."
devotionals.question: Is there someone "scary" or "different" that God is asking me to reach out to as a "brother/sister"?
devotionals.scripture: So Ananias departed... and laying his hands on him he said, 'Brother Saul...' — Acts 9:17
devotionals.title: Brother Saul
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me Ananias's courage. Help me to see people through the lens of Your grace, not my fear.

devotionals.question: What "scales" of old thinking or prejudice need to fall from my eyes today?
devotionals.reflection: When we meet Jesus and are filled with the Spirit, we see the world—and our own past—completely differently.
devotionals.scripture: And immediately something like scales fell from his eyes... — Acts 9:18
devotionals.title: Scales Falling
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, clear my vision. Help me to see the world as it really is in the light of Christ.

devotionals.question: Is my life "proclaiming" that Jesus is the Son of God, or am I keeping that truth to myself?
devotionals.reflection: True conversion results in a change of message. Saul went from "arresting the name" to "announcing the Name."
devotionals.scripture: And immediately he proclaimed Jesus... saying, 'He is the Son of God.' — Acts 9:20
devotionals.title: He Is the Son
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let my message be clear. May everything I do say, "Jesus is the Son of God."

devotionals.question: Can I be a "bridge" for someone today who is being judged or kept at a distance?
devotionals.reflection: Everyone was afraid of Saul, but Barnabas took a risk on him. We all need a "Barnabas" to speak for us when we are new or misunderstood.
devotionals.scripture: But Barnabas took him and brought him to the apostles... — Acts 9:27
devotionals.title: Encouraging the Fearful
devotionals.prayer: Father, make me a Barnabas. Help me to see the potential in others and bring them into the fold.

devotionals.reflection: Peace and growth come when the Church honors God's holiness. "Fear of the Lord" isn't being afraid; it's being "in awe."
devotionals.question: Am I "walking in the fear of the Lord" today, or have I become too casual with His presence?
devotionals.scripture: So the church... had peace and was being built up... walking in the fear of the Lord... — Acts 9:31
devotionals.title: Walking in the Fear of the Lord
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I reverence You. Build me up and give me peace as I walk in the awe of who You are.

devotionals.reflection: The Spirit is our "Comforter." When the Church is comforted by Him, it becomes a magnet for others.
devotionals.question: Am I drawing from the "comfort of the Holy Spirit" today, or am I seeking comfort in the world?
devotionals.scripture: ...and in the comfort of the Holy Spirit, it multiplied. — Acts 9:31
devotionals.title: Comfort of the Holy Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, comfort my heart. Let Your peace in me be an invitation to those around me.

devotionals.question: Am I "imitating" Jesus in the way I handle the "dead" or difficult situations in my life?
devotionals.reflection: Peter did exactly what he saw Jesus do. When we walk with the Master, we start to speak and act with His authority.
devotionals.scripture: But Peter put them all outside... and said, 'Tabitha, arise.' — Acts 9:40
devotionals.title: Tabitha, Arise
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I want to act like You. Give me the authority and the compassion to bring life where there is death.

devotionals.question: Who around me is "seeking" God, and how can I be the "Peter" sent to give them the answer?
devotionals.reflection: God hears the prayers of those who are seeking Him, even if they don't have the full picture yet. He is moving to meet the seekers.
devotionals.scripture: A devout man who feared God... and prayed continually to God. — Acts 10:2
devotionals.title: Cornelius’s Prayer
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You that You hear the seekers. Send me to someone today who is looking for You.

devotionals.question: What "labels" am I putting on people that God is asking me to remove?
devotionals.reflection: Peter had to unlearn his religious prejudices to reach the Gentiles. God’s grace is broader than our traditions.
devotionals.scripture: What God has made clean, do not call common. — Acts 10:15
devotionals.title: What God Has Made Clean
devotionals.prayer: Lord, cleanse my perspective. Help me to see every human being as someone made clean by Your potential grace.

devotionals.question: Do I show partiality in my heart based on race, politics, or status?
devotionals.reflection: This was a massive shift. The Gospel is for every tribe, tongue, and nation. There are no "second-class" citizens in the Kingdom.
devotionals.scripture: So Peter opened his mouth and said: 'Truly I understand that God shows no partiality...' — Acts 10:34
devotionals.title: God Shows No Partiality
devotionals.prayer: Father, forgive my partiality. Give me Your heart for all people without exception.

devotionals.question: If someone summarized my week, would they say I "went about doing good"?
devotionals.reflection: This is the "resume" of Jesus. He didn't just "talk" good; He "did" good. Our lives should follow that same pattern.
devotionals.scripture: ...how he went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil... — Acts 10:38
devotionals.title: He Went About Doing Good
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I want to be like You. Help me to be a force for good and healing in my world today.

devotionals.reflection: The message is simple: Believe and be forgiven. It’s the "everyone" that makes the Gospel so powerful.
devotionals.question: Do I really believe that "everyone" can be forgiven, even those who have done terrible things?
devotionals.scripture: To him all the prophets bear witness that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness... — Acts 10:43
devotionals.title: Everyone Who Believes
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for the "everyone." Thank You that it included me.

devotionals.question: Am I leaving room for God to "interrupt" my plans and move in His own way?
devotionals.reflection: God didn't wait for Peter to finish his sermon! The Spirit moves whenever hearts are open, regardless of our "liturgy."
devotionals.scripture: While Peter was still saying these things, the Holy Spirit fell on all who heard the word. — Acts 10:44
devotionals.title: The Spirit Falls on the Outsiders
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, fall on us. Don't wait for my "permission"—move in Your power today.

devotionals.reflection: Sometimes the hardest thing for religious people to do is get out of God’s way. Our job is to follow His lead, not police His grace.
devotionals.question: Am I "standing in God's way" by being too critical or traditional?
devotionals.scripture: If then God gave the same gift to them... who was I that I could stand in God's way? — Acts 11:17
devotionals.title: Who Was I to Stand in God’s Way?
devotionals.prayer: Father, help me to get out of the way. Let Your grace flow wherever You want it to go.

devotionals.question: Have I fully "turned" to the Lord today, or am I still looking back at my old life?
devotionals.reflection: Turning is the key. Believing isn't just an intellectual "yes"; it's a physical "turn" of the whole life toward Jesus.
devotionals.scripture: ...and a great number who believed turned to the Lord. — Acts 11:21
devotionals.title: A Great Number Turned
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I turn to You with all my heart. Be the center of my focus and the goal of my life.

devotionals.question: Do I look for "the grace of God" in others, or do I look for their faults?
devotionals.reflection: You can "see" grace. You see it in changed lives, new joy, and radical generosity. Barnabas looked for the good and celebrated it.
devotionals.scripture: When he came and saw the grace of God, he was glad... — Acts 11:23
devotionals.title: Seeing the Grace
devotionals.prayer: Lord, help me to "see the grace." Make me a person who celebrates Your work in others.

devotionals.question: Do I have a "steadfast purpose of heart" today to be faithful, regardless of my feelings?
devotionals.reflection: Feeling "glad" isn't enough; we need "purpose of heart." We must decide beforehand that we will be faithful when things get tough.
devotionals.scripture: ...and he exhorted them all to remain faithful to the Lord with steadfast purpose of heart. — Acts 11:23
devotionals.title: Purpose of Heart
devotionals.prayer: Father, strengthen my resolve. Help me to remain faithful to You through every high and low.

devotionals.reflection: A "Christian" is a "little Christ." People saw so much of Jesus in the disciples that they had to give them a new name.
devotionals.question: If someone observed me today, would they think to call me a "little Christ"?
devotionals.scripture: And in Antioch the disciples were first called Christians. — Acts 11:26
devotionals.title: Called Christians
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, be so visible in me that people can't help but see Your character in mine.

devotionals.reflection: When the doors are locked, prayer is the key. The Church’s most powerful weapon isn't political influence; it's earnest prayer.
devotionals.question: What "prison" am I facing (or is someone I know facing) that needs "earnest prayer" today?
devotionals.scripture: So Peter was kept in prison, but earnest prayer for him was made to God by the church. — Acts 12:5
devotionals.title: Earnest Prayer
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I pray earnestly for those who are bound. Shake the foundations and open the doors.

devotionals.question: Am I "knocking" on the door of an answered prayer that I’m too cynical to believe in?
devotionals.reflection: Sometimes God answers our prayers so quickly that we don't even believe it's Him. He is better to us than our small faith expects.
devotionals.scripture: They said, 'It is his angel!' But Peter continued knocking... — Acts 12:15-16
devotionals.title: It Is His Angel!
devotionals.prayer: Father, forgive my unbelief. Thank You for answering even my "weak" and skeptical prayers.

devotionals.reflection: Taking credit for what God does is dangerous territory. Herod forgot he was just a man; God reminded him.
devotionals.question: Am I "robbing" God of His glory by taking credit for my successes?
devotionals.scripture: Immediately an angel... struck him down, because he did not give God the glory... — Acts 12:23
devotionals.title: Not Giving God the Glory
devotionals.prayer: Lord, all glory belongs to You. I am just a servant; You are the King.

devotionals.question: Am I building my life on things that "pass away" or on the "Word that increases"?
devotionals.reflection: Kings come and go, persecutors rise and fall, but the Word of God just keeps growing. It is the most resilient force on earth.
devotionals.scripture: But the word of God increased and multiplied. — Acts 12:24
devotionals.title: The Word Increased
devotionals.prayer: Father, build my life on Your Word. I trust in the things that are eternal and growing.

devotionals.reflection: God’s specific direction often comes during corporate worship and fasting. When we focus on Him, He speaks clearly about our next steps.
devotionals.question: Am I "worshiping and fasting" enough to hear God's specific "set apart" calls for my life?
devotionals.scripture: While they were worshiping... the Holy Spirit said, 'Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul...' — Acts 13:2
devotionals.title: Set Apart for Me
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, I am listening. Set me apart for the work You have for me in this season.

devotionals.reflection: There is a difference between "going" and "being sent." When the Spirit sends us, He goes before us to prepare the way.
devotionals.question: Do I feel "sent" into my day today, or am I just "going" through the motions?
devotionals.scripture: So, being sent out by the Holy Spirit, they went down... — Acts 13:4
devotionals.title: Being Sent Out
devotionals.prayer: Lord, send me. I want to move under the direction and power of Your Spirit today.

devotionals.question: Am I asking for discernment today to see through the lies and deceptions of the world?
devotionals.reflection: Paul (Saul) had the discernment to see through spiritual deception. We need the Spirit to help us see the "wolves" in "sheep's clothing."
devotionals.scripture: But Saul... looked intently at him and said, 'You son of the devil...' — Acts 13:9-10
devotionals.title: Full of Deceit
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me sharp spiritual eyes. Help me to recognize the truth and expose the lies.

devotionals.reflection: It’s not just the miracles that convert; it’s the "teaching." The Word of God has its own "astonishing" power to change minds.
devotionals.question: When was the last time I was "astonished" by a truth from God's Word?
devotionals.scripture: Then the proconsul believed... for he was astonished at the teaching of the Lord. — Acts 13:12
devotionals.title: Astonished at the Teaching
devotionals.prayer: Father, keep me in awe of Your Truth. Let Your Word astonish me and those I share it with.

devotionals.reflection: Paul took every opportunity to speak. He didn't wait for a "perfect" platform; he just used the one he had.
devotionals.question: What "platform" (social media, a coffee break, a meeting) do I have today to ask people to "listen"?
devotionals.scripture: So Paul stood up, and motioning with his hand said: 'Men of Israel... listen.' — Acts 13:16
devotionals.title: Men of Israel, Listen
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me the courage to speak up. Help me to use every opportunity to share Your story.

devotionals.reflection: Being "after God's heart" means being willing to do "all His will." It’s a heart of total availability.
devotionals.question: Is my heart "after God's heart" today? Am I ready to do all of His will?
devotionals.scripture: ...I have found in David... a man after my heart, who will do all my will. — Acts 13:22
devotionals.title: A Man After My Heart
devotionals.prayer: Father, make my heart like Yours. I want to be someone who does everything You ask of me.

devotionals.question: Am I keeping Jesus at the center of my "Good News," or am I getting distracted by other things?
devotionals.reflection: Everything in the Gospel comes back to "This Man"—Jesus. He is the only source of true forgiveness and freedom.
devotionals.scripture: Let it be known to you... that through this man forgiveness of sins is proclaimed to you. — Acts 13:38
devotionals.title: Through This Man
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are the Center. I proclaim Your name and Your forgiveness as the only hope.

devotionals.reflection: Everything means everything. Past guilt, present shame, future fear—the Law couldn't do it, but Jesus did.
devotionals.question: Is there "anything" I’m still letting hold me captive, even though Jesus has freed me from "everything"?
devotionals.scripture: ...by him everyone who believes is freed from everything... — Acts 13:39
devotionals.title: Freed from Everything
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I walk in total freedom. Thank You for breaking every chain that the Law couldn't touch.

devotionals.question: Who can I "urge to continue in grace" today? Who needs a "keep going" word?
devotionals.reflection: Starting is easy; "continuing" is the work. We need to urge each other to stay in the flow of grace every single day.
devotionals.scripture: ...many Jews and devout converts... followed Paul and Barnabas, who... urged them to continue in the grace of God. — Acts 13:43
devotionals.title: Urged to Continue
devotionals.prayer: Father, help me to persist. Give me the words to encourage others to stay the course.

devotionals.reflection: This happened right after they were kicked out of the city! You can be "persecuted" and "filled with joy" at the same time.
devotionals.question: Is my "joy" dependent on how people treat me, or on how the Spirit fills me?
devotionals.scripture: And the disciples were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit. — Acts 13:52
devotionals.title: Filled with Joy and the Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, fill me with Your joy. Let it be my strength even when I face rejection or difficulty.

devotionals.question: Am I waiting to feel "bold," or will I speak "boldly" and trust God to back me up?
devotionals.reflection: Boldness isn't self-confidence; it's God-confidence. When we speak, He "bears witness" by moving in people's hearts.
devotionals.scripture: So they remained for a long time, speaking boldly for the Lord, who bore witness to the word of his grace... — Acts 14:3
devotionals.title: Speaking Boldly for the Lord
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I’ll speak for You. I trust You to bear witness to Your Word with Your power.

devotionals.question: Am I looking for the "faith" in others that is ready for God's touch?
devotionals.reflection: Paul didn't heal everyone; he looked for "faith." There is a spiritual "readiness" we should look for in others.
devotionals.scripture: Paul... seeing that he had faith to be made well, said... 'Stand upright...' — Acts 14:9-10
devotionals.title: Faith to Be Made Well
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me discernment. Help me to see where You are already working in people's hearts.

devotionals.question: Am I letting people "idolize" me, or am I pointing them to the "Living God"?
devotionals.reflection: When the crowd tried to worship them as gods, Paul and Barnabas tore their clothes in protest. We must never accept the praise that belongs to God.
devotionals.scripture: Men, why are you doing these things? We also are men, of like nature with you... — Acts 14:15
devotionals.title: Just Men Like You
devotionals.prayer: Father, take the glory. I am just a man/woman; You are the King of Kings.

devotionals.question: Do I notice the "witness" of God's goodness in the weather and the food I enjoy today?
devotionals.reflection: God's kindness in nature (rain, food, sunshine) is a "witness" to His goodness. Even "unbelievers" are experiencing His grace daily.
devotionals.scripture: ...yet he did not leave himself without witness, for he did good by giving you rains from heaven... — Acts 14:17
devotionals.title: He Did Not Leave Himself Without Witness
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for the "witness" of Your common grace. Thank You for the rain and the sunshine.

devotionals.question: Who can I "strengthen" today through a verse, a prayer, or a shared testimony?
devotionals.reflection: Conversion is the birth; strengthening is the growth. We need to "strengthen the souls" of those around us.
devotionals.scripture: ...strengthening the souls of the disciples, encouraging them to continue in the faith... — Acts 14:22
devotionals.title: Strengthening the Souls
devotionals.prayer: Father, use me to build up the faith of others. Let my words be a source of strength today.

devotionals.question: Am I surprised by "tribulations," or do I see them as part of the road to the Kingdom?
devotionals.reflection: Paul didn't preach a "prosperity" Gospel. He told them the truth: the road to the Kingdom is paved with trials.
devotionals.scripture: ...and saying that through many tribulations we must enter the kingdom of God. — Acts 14:22
devotionals.title: Through Many Tribulations
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me endurance. Help me to walk through the trials with my eyes on the Prize.

devotionals.question: Is there someone I am "over-mothering" spiritually that I need to "commit to the Lord" today?
devotionals.reflection: Eventually, we have to "let go" and trust people to the Lord. He is a better Shepherd for them than we could ever be.
devotionals.scripture: And when they had appointed elders... they committed them to the Lord in whom they had believed. — Acts 14:23
devotionals.title: Committed to the Lord
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I commit my loved ones to You. You are their Shepherd; I trust You to lead them.

devotionals.reflection: The "year" (and the block) ends with a report of victory. God opened a "door of faith" to the world, and it can't be shut.
devotionals.question: As I look back on this season, what is the "all that God has done" that I can declare?
devotionals.scripture: And when they arrived... they declared all that God had done with them... — Acts 14:27
devotionals.title: Declaring All That God Had Done
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the "door of faith." Thank You for everything You’ve done in me and through me this year.

devotionals.reflection: Not all conflict is bad. Sometimes we have to "debate" and stand our ground to protect the purity of the Gospel.
devotionals.question: Am I willing to speak up when the truth of "Grace alone" is being compromised?
devotionals.scripture: Paul and Barnabas had no small dissension and debate with them... — Acts 15:2
devotionals.title: No Small Dissension
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me the wisdom to know when to be flexible and when to stand firm for the Truth.

devotionals.reflection: Peter reminds everyone that even the "experts" are saved by grace. There is no "merit-based" entrance into God's family.
devotionals.question: Am I trying to "earn" my way today, or am I resting in the finished grace of Jesus?
devotionals.scripture: But we believe that we will be saved through the grace of the Lord Jesus... — Acts 15:11
devotionals.title: Saved Through Grace
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for Your grace. I stop striving and start resting in Your unmerited favor.

devotionals.reflection: Religion often adds "troubles" (rules) to people. The Gospel is about removing burdens, not adding them.
devotionals.question: Am I making it "troublesome" for others to find Jesus by adding my own personal rules or preferences?
devotionals.scripture: Therefore my judgment is that we should not trouble those... who turn to God. — Acts 15:19
devotionals.title: Not Troubling the Gentiles
devotionals.prayer: Lord, help me clear the way for others to find You. Let me be a bridge, not a barrier.

devotionals.reflection: The early church leaders were respected because they had "skin in the game." Sacrifice is the true mark of leadership.
devotionals.question: What have I "risked" lately for the sake of the Name of Jesus?
devotionals.scripture: ...men who have risked their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ. — Acts 15:26
devotionals.title: Men Who Risked Lives
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You gave Your life for me. Help me to live a life of courageous risk for Your glory.

devotionals.reflection: The early church moved in partnership with the Spirit. Their decisions weren't just "votes," they were "discernment."
devotionals.question: Am I looking for what "seems good to the Spirit" today, or just what seems good to me?
devotionals.scripture: For it has seemed good to the Holy Spirit and to us... — Acts 15:28
devotionals.title: It Seemed Good to the Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, lead my decisions. May my plans align with what seems good to You.

devotionals.reflection: Even "giants of faith" like Paul and Barnabas had disagreements. God can still use "separated paths" to double the reach of the Gospel.
devotionals.question: Can I trust that God is at work even when a relationship ends or changes direction?
devotionals.scripture: And there arose a sharp disagreement, so that they separated... — Acts 15:39
devotionals.title: Sharp Disagreement
devotionals.prayer: Lord, heal my hurts from disagreements. Use every path I take to spread Your love.

devotionals.question: Who is a "church" (a believer) near me that I can go and strengthen today?
devotionals.reflection: Our job is never just "conversion." We are called to keep showing up to "strengthen" those who already believe.
devotionals.scripture: And he went through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches. — Acts 15:41
devotionals.title: Strengthening the Churches
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me the words and the energy to build up the souls of my brothers and sisters.

devotionals.reflection: Timothy was chosen for the mission because he had a solid reputation in his local community. Character is the foundation of calling.
devotionals.question: If Paul were looking for a teammate today, what would my "local brothers/sisters" say about me?
devotionals.scripture: He [Timothy] was well spoken of by the brothers... — Acts 16:2
devotionals.title: A Good Report
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let my private character be worthy of a "good report" in public.

devotionals.question: Am I frustrated by a "closed door" right now, or can I trust it’s the Spirit’s redirection?
devotionals.reflection: A "No" from God is just as much a lead as a "Yes." Closed doors are often God’s way of protecting our time for the best door.
devotionals.scripture: ...having been forbidden by the Holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia. — Acts 16:6
devotionals.title: Forbidden by the Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, thank You for Your "No." I trust You to lead me to the right place at the right time.

devotionals.question: Who is crying out for "help" in my world today? Am I listening for the "Macedonian Call"?
devotionals.reflection: When God closes one door, He opens a "Macedonia." There is always someone, somewhere, crying out for "help."
devotionals.scripture: A vision appeared to Paul... 'Come over to Macedonia and help us.' — Acts 16:9
devotionals.title: The Macedonian Call
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me ears to hear the cry of the needy. Send me where the "help" is most needed.

devotionals.reflection: Lydia was a successful businesswoman, but she still needed her heart "opened." God is the one who makes the Word "click."
devotionals.question: Am I praying for God to "open the hearts" of the people I’ll be talking to today?
devotionals.scripture: The Lord opened her heart to pay attention to what was said by Paul. — Acts 16:14
devotionals.title: The Lord Opened Her Heart
devotionals.prayer: Lord, open hearts. Let Your Word find a home in the people I meet today.

devotionals.reflection: Jesus has authority over spiritual darkness. We don't have to fear the "occult" or "darkness" when we stand in His Name.
devotionals.question: Am I standing in the authority of Jesus over the "darkness" (depression, fear, temptation) in my life?
devotionals.scripture: I command you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her. — Acts 16:18
devotionals.title: In the Name of Jesus
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I stand in Your Name. I command the darkness to flee from my heart and my home.

devotionals.question: What is my "midnight" song today? Will I sing even if my "feet are in the stocks"?
devotionals.reflection: You can lock a believer's feet, but you can't lock their song. Worship in the "midnight" is the most powerful kind of worship.
devotionals.scripture: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God... — Acts 16:25
devotionals.title: Singing in the Stocks
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me a song in the night. Let my worship be louder than my circumstances.

devotionals.question: Who is "listening" to my response to my current trial? Am I giving them a reason to hope?
devotionals.reflection: People are always watching how Christians handle pain. Your response to suffering is your loudest sermon.
devotionals.scripture: ...and the prisoners were listening to them. — Acts 16:25
devotionals.title: The Prisoners Were Listening
devotionals.prayer: Father, use my trials as a testimony. Let others hear Your praise through my pain.

devotionals.reflection: God can shake the foundations of any prison. He is the God of the "sudden" breakthrough.
devotionals.question: Do I believe God can "suddenly" unfasten the bonds I’ve been struggling with for years?
devotionals.scripture: And suddenly there was a great earthquake... and everyone's bonds were unfastened. — Acts 16:26
devotionals.title: Sudden Earthquake
devotionals.prayer: Lord, shake the foundations. Bring a "sudden" breakthrough to the areas where I am bound.

devotionals.question: Am I more focused on "escaping" my problems or on "ministering" to the people around them?
devotionals.reflection: Paul cared more about the jailer’s soul than his own escape. True freedom allows us to love our "enemies."
devotionals.scripture: But Paul cried with a loud voice, 'Do not harm yourself, for we are all here.' — Acts 16:28
devotionals.title: Do Yourself No Harm
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, give me Your heart for the "jailers" in my life. Help me to seek their good above my own.

devotionals.reflection: This is the ultimate question of humanity. Every heart is asking it, even if they don't use these exact words.
devotionals.question: How am I answering the "What must I do?" question for people through my life and words?
devotionals.scripture: Then he brought them out and said, 'Sirs, what must I do to be saved?' — Acts 16:30
devotionals.title: What Must I Do?
devotionals.prayer: Father, lead me to the seekers. Help me to be ready with the answer of the Gospel.

devotionals.reflection: The answer is a Person, not a program. Belief is the only requirement for a total life-transformation.
devotionals.question: Is my faith still simple? Do I still "Believe in the Lord Jesus" as my only hope?
devotionals.scripture: And they said, 'Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved...' — Acts 16:31
devotionals.title: Believe in the Lord Jesus
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I believe in You. You are my Lord and my Savior. I trust You with my whole life.

devotionals.reflection: The jailer went from "inflicting" wounds to "washing" them. True repentance always results in practical acts of love.
devotionals.question: Is my repentance "washing the wounds" of those I have hurt in the past?
devotionals.scripture: And he took them the same hour of the night and washed their wounds... — Acts 16:33
devotionals.title: Washing the Wounds
devotionals.prayer: Lord, show me where I need to make amends. Turn my heart from "harming" to "healing."

devotionals.reflection: Salvation is a family affair. One person’s faith can change the "atmosphere" of an entire home.
devotionals.question: Am I praying for my "entire household" to experience the joy of believing in God?
devotionals.scripture: ...and he rejoiced along with his entire household that he had believed in God. — Acts 16:34
devotionals.title: Rejoicing with the Household
devotionals.prayer: Father, bring salvation to my home. Let the joy of Your Spirit fill every room and every heart.

devotionals.reflection: Faith isn't "blind"; it is "reasoned." God invites us to use our minds to understand His Truth.
devotionals.question: Am I "reasoning" with the Word, or am I just skimming it without thinking?
devotionals.scripture: And Paul went in... and on three Sabbath days he reasoned with them from the Scriptures... — Acts 17:2
devotionals.title: Reasoning from Scriptures
devotionals.prayer: Lord, engage my mind. Help me to understand the "whys" of Your Word so I can explain them to others.

devotionals.reflection: When the Gospel is lived out, it disrupts the status quo. It turns the world "upside down" (which is actually "right-side up" in God's eyes).
devotionals.question: Is my life "disruptive" to the world's way of doing things, or am I just blending in?
devotionals.scripture: These men who have turned the world upside down have come here also... — Acts 17:6
devotionals.title: Turning the World Upside Down
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, use me to turn my "world" right-side up. Let Your Kingdom values challenge the culture around me.

devotionals.question: How "eager" am I to hear from God today? Am I leaned-in or leaned-back?
devotionals.reflection: The "noble" heart is the "eager" heart. It doesn't come with skepticism, but with a hunger to know the Truth.
devotionals.scripture: Now these Jews were more noble... they received the word with all eagerness... — Acts 17:11a
devotionals.title: Receiving with Eagerness
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me an eager heart. I want to receive Your Word with joy and excitement today.

devotionals.reflection: Don't take a human's word for it—check the Source! Daily examination of the Bible is the only way to stay on track.
devotionals.question: Have I "checked the Source" for the thoughts and ideas I’ve let into my head today?
devotionals.scripture: ...examining the Scriptures daily to see if these things were so. — Acts 17:11b
devotionals.title: Examining Daily
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me a "Berean." Help me to compare everything I hear to the Truth of Your Word.

devotionals.reflection: When we see people following "fake gods" (money, fame, comfort), it should "provoke" us to share the True God with them.
devotionals.question: What "idols" in my city/culture are provoking my heart to share the Gospel?
devotionals.scripture: ...his spirit was provoked within him as he saw that the city was full of idols. — Acts 17:16
devotionals.title: Provoked by Idols
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, break my heart for what breaks Yours. Let the lostness of others provoke me to love and action.

devotionals.question: Where can I find "common ground" with a non-believer today to start a spiritual conversation?
devotionals.reflection: Paul looked for "common ground" to start the conversation. We can find "echoes" of God in people's longings and arts.
devotionals.scripture: For as I passed along... I found also an altar with this inscription: 'To the unknown God.' — Acts 17:23
devotionals.title: To the Unknown God
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me wisdom. Help me to see the "altars" people have built and point them to the True God.

devotionals.question: Do I live today with the awareness that God is "actually not far" from me right now?
devotionals.reflection: God isn't "hiding." He is right there, waiting to be found. He is closer to you than your own breath.
devotionals.scripture: Yet he is actually not far from each one of us... — Acts 17:27
devotionals.title: He Is Not Far
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for Your proximity. I reach out for You today, knowing You are right here.

devotionals.question: How does it change my confidence to know that I am "moving" within the life of God today?
