devotionals.reflection: We are "submerged" in God. Our very existence is sustained by His power. We are never out of His reach.
devotionals.scripture: In him we live and move and have our being... — Acts 17:28
devotionals.title: In Him We Live
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I live and move in You. Let every breath I take be a response to Your sustaining grace.

devotionals.reflection: God is patient, but there comes a time when the Truth is clear and a decision must be made. Today is that day.
devotionals.question: Is there something I’ve been "ignoring" that God is now calling me to "repent" of?
devotionals.scripture: The times of ignorance God overlooked, but now he commands all people everywhere to repent... — Acts 17:30
devotionals.title: Overlooked Ignorance
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for Your patience. I choose to turn from my ignorance and follow Your Light.

devotionals.reflection: Justice is coming. This is bad news for the unrepentant, but great news for the oppressed. God will make everything right.
devotionals.question: Does the reality of a "fixed day" of judgment make me more urgent about sharing the Gospel?
devotionals.scripture: ...because he has fixed a day on which he will judge the world... — Acts 17:31
devotionals.title: Fixed a Day
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for Your justice. Help me to live in a way that is ready for that Day.

devotionals.reflection: Don't be discouraged by "mockers." Paul didn't "win" the whole city of Athens, but he found the "some" who were ready.
devotionals.question: Am I focused on the "mockers" who reject me, or the "seekers" who need me?
devotionals.scripture: Now when they heard of the resurrection... some mocked... but some men joined him and believed. — Acts 17:32, 34
devotionals.title: Some Mocked, Some Joined
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I leave the results to You. I will keep speaking, trusting You to find the "some" who will believe.

devotionals.reflection: Being "occupied" means being "filled up" or "consumed." When the Word occupies us, it naturally spills out in our testimony.
devotionals.question: What is "occupying" my mind today—worries, news, or the Word of God?
devotionals.scripture: Paul was occupied with the word, testifying to the Jews that the Christ was Jesus. — Acts 17:18:5
devotionals.title: Occupied with the Word
devotionals.prayer: Lord, occupy my heart with Your Word. Let Your Truth be the main thing on my mind all day long.

devotionals.question: Have I done my part to share the truth with the people in my life?
devotionals.reflection: We are responsible for sharing the message; we are not responsible for how people respond to it. Do your part and leave the rest to God.
devotionals.scripture: ...he shook out his garments and said to them, 'Your blood be on your own heads! I am innocent.' — Acts 18:6
devotionals.title: Your Blood on Your Heads
devotionals.prayer: Father, help me to be a faithful messenger. I release the weight of people's responses to You.

devotionals.reflection: Even the "Great Apostle" felt fear. Jesus’ antidote to fear is his Presence: "For I am with you."
devotionals.question: What am I "afraid" to say today? Will I trust that Jesus is "with me" in the conversation?
devotionals.scripture: And the Lord said to Paul... 'Do not be afraid, but go on speaking and do not be silent...' — Acts 18:9
devotionals.title: Do Not Be Afraid
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I hear Your voice: "Do not be silent." Give me the courage to keep speaking for You.

devotionals.reflection: God sees the "hidden" believers and the "future" believers that we can't see yet. Every city is full of His potential.
devotionals.question: Can I look at my city today with "Jesus-eyes"—seeing the "many people" who belong to Him?
devotionals.scripture: ...for I have many in this city who are my people. — Acts 18:10
devotionals.title: Many People in This City
devotionals.prayer: Lord, show me Your people. Help me to find the ones You have already prepared for Your Kingdom.

devotionals.reflection: Fervent literally means "boiling." Apollos had a heat and a passion for God that couldn't be ignored.
devotionals.question: Is my spirit "boiling" for God, or has it become lukewarm?
devotionals.scripture: He [Apollos] had been instructed in the way of the Lord. And being fervent in spirit... — Acts 18:25
devotionals.title: Fervent in Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, set me on fire. Let my passion for You be felt by everyone I meet.

devotionals.question: Who am I "mentoring" or "being mentored by" to understand the way of God "more accurately"?
devotionals.reflection: Even a great preacher like Apollos needed "more accurate" instruction. We should always be in "learning mode."
devotionals.scripture: Priscilla and Aquila heard him... and explained to him the way of God more accurately. — Acts 18:26
devotionals.title: Explaining More Accurately
devotionals.prayer: Lord, keep me teachable. Send "Priscillas and Aquilas" into my life to help me grow.

devotionals.question: How can I be a "great help" to someone in my church or small group today?
devotionals.reflection: Our goal is to be a "great help" to other believers. Grace is the power that enables us to be useful to the Body.
devotionals.scripture: ...he arrived and greatly helped those who through grace had believed. — Acts 18:27
devotionals.title: Greatly Helped
devotionals.prayer: Father, use my gifts to help Your people. Let my life be a contribution to the strength of Your Church.

devotionals.question: Am I living with the "Power" of the Spirit today, or just the "Rules" of religion?
devotionals.reflection: It’s possible to have "half the story" (knowing about baptism/repentance) but missing the "Power" (the Spirit). We need the whole package.
devotionals.scripture: And he said to them, 'Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you believed?' — Acts 19:2
devotionals.title: Did You Receive the Holy Spirit?
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, I receive You. Fill me completely and give me the power to live for You.

devotionals.question: Does "the name of Jesus" carry the most weight in my identity today?
devotionals.reflection: Everything comes back to the Name. Our identity, our power, and our future are all tied up in "the name of the Lord Jesus."
devotionals.scripture: On hearing this, they were baptized into the name of the Lord Jesus. — Acts 19:5
devotionals.title: Into the Name of Jesus
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I belong to Your Name. I walk in Your identity and I trust in Your power.

devotionals.question: Am I making my "hands" available for God to do something "extraordinary" today?
devotionals.reflection: God does the "extraordinary," but He uses "hands" to do it. You are the channel; He is the Source.
devotionals.scripture: And God was doing extraordinary miracles by the hands of Paul... — Acts 19:11
devotionals.title: Miracles by the Hands
devotionals.prayer: Lord, here are my hands. Do something through me today that only You can do.

devotionals.question: Does the "darkness" recognize me as someone who belongs to Jesus?
devotionals.reflection: You can't "fake" spiritual authority. The enemy only respects those who are truly "in Christ."
devotionals.scripture: The evil spirit answered them, 'Jesus I know, and Paul I recognize, but who are you?' — Acts 19:15
devotionals.title: Jesus I Know... But Who Are You?
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me "known in hell" as one of Your faithful warriors. I stand in Your true authority.

devotionals.reflection: When God’s power is real, people stop "playing church." The Name of Jesus becomes the most important thing in town.
devotionals.question: Am I "extolling" (praising/lifting up) the Name of Jesus today in a way that others can see?
devotionals.scripture: And fear fell upon them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was extolled. — Acts 19:17
devotionals.title: Fear Fell Upon Them All
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I extol Your Name. You are higher than any power and more wonderful than any dream.

devotionals.reflection: Real revival involves "cleaning out the closet." We have to drag our hidden sins into the light to be free of them.
devotionals.question: Is there a "secret practice" I need to "divulge" to God (or a trusted friend) today to be free?
devotionals.scripture: Also many of those who were now believers came, confessing and divulging their practices. — Acts 19:18
devotionals.title: Confessing and Divulging
devotionals.prayer: Father, I bring my secrets into Your light. Forgive me and cleanse me from every hidden sin.

devotionals.reflection: They didn't just "stop" doing it; they "burned" the bridges back to it. Sometimes we have to destroy the things that tempted us.
devotionals.question: Is there something I need to "burn" (get rid of) today so I can't go back to my old ways?
devotionals.scripture: And a number of those who had practiced magic arts brought their books... and burned them... — Acts 19:19
devotionals.title: Burning the Books
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me the courage to "burn the books." I want no way back to my old life.

devotionals.reflection: No amount of "magic," "idolatry," or "opposition" can stop the Word when it starts to "prevail." It is the ultimate winner.
devotionals.question: Am I trusting that the Word will "prevail" in my difficult situation, or am I giving up?
devotionals.scripture: So the word of the Lord continued to increase and prevail mightily. — Acts 19:20
devotionals.title: The Word Prevailed
devotionals.prayer: Father, Your Word prevails. I stand on Your promises, knowing they are stronger than any problem.

devotionals.question: Am I prepared for the "stir" that might happen if I live out the Truth at work or school?
devotionals.reflection: The Gospel causes a "stir" because it threatens the "idols" that make people money. Truth is always a threat to a crooked system.
devotionals.scripture: About that time there arose no little stir concerning the Way. — Acts 19:23
devotionals.title: No Little Stir
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me a steady heart in the middle of the "stir." Let me represent "the Way" with grace and courage.

devotionals.reflection: The world has its own "chants" and "idols." They are loud, but they are empty. Don't be intimidated by the volume of the crowd.
devotionals.question: Am I letting the "clamor" of the world drown out the "whisper" of the Spirit?
devotionals.scripture: ...they were enraged and were crying out, 'Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!' — Acts 19:28
devotionals.title: Great is Artemis!
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are the only Great One. I refuse to be intimidated by the world's loud idols.

devotionals.reflection: Paul’s message was simple: If you can "make" it, it isn't "God." We serve a Creator, not a creation.
devotionals.question: Am I trusting in "gods made with hands" (bank accounts, technology, self-effort) today?
devotionals.scripture: ...this Paul has... turned away a great many people, saying that gods made with hands are not gods. — Acts 19:26
devotionals.title: God Not Made with Hands
devotionals.prayer: Creator God, I worship You. I turn away from everything man-made and I lean on Your eternal power.

devotionals.question: Am I getting sucked into the "confusion" of the culture, or am I standing in the peace of Christ?
devotionals.reflection: Most people in the "anti-God" crowd don't even know why they are there. Chaos is the hallmark of the enemy; peace is the hallmark of God.
devotionals.scripture: Now some cried out one thing, some another, for the assembly was in confusion... — Acts 19:32
devotionals.title: Confusion in the Assembly
devotionals.prayer: Lord, keep me out of the confusion. Give me a clear mind and a peaceful heart in a chaotic world.

devotionals.reflection: God used a "town clerk" to protect His people. God can use anyone—even a secular official—to accomplish His purposes.
devotionals.question: Do I trust that God can use "unlikely" people to protect and provide for me today?
devotionals.scripture: And when he had said these things, he dismissed the assembly. — Acts 19:41
devotionals.title: Dismissed the Assembly
devotionals.prayer: Sovereign Lord, thank You for Your protection. I trust that You have "town clerks" and "officials" in place to work for my good.

devotionals.reflection: Paul’s "final lap" was focused on one thing: encouragement. People need "many words" of hope to stay the course.
devotionals.question: How many "words of encouragement" can I give away today? Who needs to hear "keep going"?
devotionals.scripture: ...he went through those regions and had given them much encouragement... — Acts 20:2
devotionals.title: Encouraging Them with Many Words
devotionals.prayer: Father, fill my mouth with encouragement. Let me be a "cheerleader" for Your people today.

devotionals.question: Am I faithful to the "routines" of gathering and breaking bread with other believers?
devotionals.reflection: The "Lord's Day" (Sunday) was already becoming the rhythm of the Church. Routine is the container for revival.
devotionals.scripture: On the first day of the week, when we were gathered together to break bread... — Acts 20:7
devotionals.title: The First Day of the Week
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for the rhythm of the Church. Help me to be faithful to the "gathering" and the "breaking of bread."

devotionals.question: Am I "falling asleep" spiritually? Is it time for me to "wake up" and rejoin the mission?
devotionals.reflection: Even during the best preaching, people get tired or distracted. But God is the God of the "second chance" (and the "second life").
devotionals.scripture: And a young man named Eutychus... fell into a deep sleep... and fell down from the third story... — Acts 20:9
devotionals.title: Eutychus Falls
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, wake me up! Don't let me fall into the "sleep" of apathy or distraction.

devotionals.question: Am I "alarmed" by the "falls" of others, or do I trust that God can restore their life?
devotionals.reflection: Paul didn't panic. A leader who knows God can stay calm in the middle of a tragedy.
devotionals.scripture: But Paul went down and bent over him... 'Do not be alarmed, for his life is in him.' — Acts 20:10
devotionals.title: Do Not Be Alarmed
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me a calm spirit. Help me to speak life into the "falls" of those around me.

devotionals.reflection: Integrity means telling people what they need to hear, not just what they want to hear. A true friend doesn't "shrink back."
devotionals.question: Is there a "profitable" truth I’ve been shrinking back from sharing with someone I love?
devotionals.scripture: ...how I did not shrink from declaring to you anything that was profitable... — Acts 20:20
devotionals.title: Not Shrinking Back
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me the courage to speak the "profitable" truth with love and grace.

devotionals.question: Am I willing to be "constrained by the Spirit" to go somewhere, even if I don't know the outcome?
devotionals.reflection: Paul was a "prisoner" of the Spirit. He didn't know what would happen, but he knew Who was leading him.
devotionals.scripture: And now, behold, I am going to Jerusalem, constrained by the Spirit... — Acts 20:22
devotionals.title: Bound by the Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I am Yours. Constrain me, lead me, and use me—wherever You want me to go.

devotionals.question: What "value" am I placing on my comfort vs. the "value" of God's mission for me?
devotionals.reflection: Paul’s "math" was different. His life was worth nothing compared to the mission of the Gospel.
devotionals.scripture: But I do not account my life of any value nor as precious to myself... — Acts 20:24a
devotionals.title: Not Accounting My Life
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, my life is Yours. I count my comfort as nothing compared to the joy of finishing Your race.

devotionals.reflection: Success isn't "winning" the race; it's "finishing" it. Stay in your lane and keep running until the end.
devotionals.question: Am I focused on my own "course," or am I trying to run someone else's?
devotionals.scripture: ...if only I may finish my course and the ministry that I received... — Acts 20:24b
devotionals.title: Finishing My Course
devotionals.prayer: Father, help me to finish well. I want to be faithful to the specific ministry You’ve given me.

devotionals.question: As this block ends, will I "commend" myself to the "Word of Grace" for the next season?
devotionals.reflection: When the leaders leave, the "Word of Grace" remains. It is the only thing that can truly "build you up" and give you an inheritance.
devotionals.scripture: And now I commend you to God and to the word of his grace, which is able to build you up... — Acts 20:32
devotionals.title: The Word of His Grace
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I commend myself to You. Build me up by Your Word and lead me into the inheritance You have for me.

devotionals.reflection: Sometimes "love" tries to talk us out of "sacrifice." True community eventually steps back and trusts God’s difficult path for us.
devotionals.question: Is there a "hard path" I am avoiding because others are worried for me, or vice versa?
devotionals.scripture: Since he would not be persuaded, we ceased and said, 'Let the will of the Lord be done.' — Acts 21:14
devotionals.title: Let the Will be Done
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I stop my own persuasion. Let Your will—not my comfort—be the final word today.

devotionals.question: What am I still "holding onto" so tightly that it makes me afraid of following Jesus fully?
devotionals.reflection: Paul’s secret was that he had already "died" to himself years ago. You can’t threaten a man who has nothing to lose.
devotionals.scripture: For I am ready not only to be imprisoned but even to die... for the name. — Acts 21:13
devotionals.title: Ready to Die
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I am Yours. Take my fears and replace them with a resolve that the world cannot shake.

devotionals.reflection: Paul didn't just tell stories; he gave God the credit. When we share our "wins," the goal is to make God look big.
devotionals.question: Who can I tell today about a "win" in my life, ensuring they know it was God's doing?
devotionals.scripture: And when they heard it, they glorified God. — Acts 21:20
devotionals.title: They Glorified God
devotionals.prayer: Father, take the glory for every good thing. I refuse to steal the praise that belongs to You.

devotionals.reflection: Truth often causes a "stir." Don't mistake a lack of peace in the room for a lack of God's presence in the person.
devotionals.question: Am I okay with "disturbing the peace" if it means standing for the Truth of the Gospel?
devotionals.scripture: ...the Jews from Asia... stirred up the whole crowd and laid hands on him. — Acts 21:27
devotionals.title: The Stirred-Up Crowd
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me a steady heart when the crowd stirs. Help me to be a non-anxious presence.

devotionals.reflection: God used a Roman arrest to save Paul from a Jewish lynch mob. God often uses our "problems" as His "protections."
devotionals.question: Can I see my current "restriction" (a job, a rule, a delay) as God’s way of shielding me?
devotionals.scripture: Then the tribune came up and arrested him... and commanded him to be bound. — Acts 21:33
devotionals.title: Rescued by the Enemy
devotionals.prayer: Sovereign Lord, thank You for the "arrests" that keep me from worse disasters. I trust Your timing.

devotionals.reflection: Paul looked for the "gap." Even while being dragged away, he was looking for a way to speak.
devotionals.question: What "barracks" (difficult environment) am I in right now where I could ask, "May I say something?"
devotionals.scripture: As Paul was about to be brought into the barracks, he said... 'May I say something to you?' — Acts 21:37
devotionals.title: May I Say Something?
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, give me the right words at the most unlikely times. Let me speak hope into the chaos.

devotionals.reflection: Paul started his defense with his story. Your personal history with God is the one argument no one can refute.
devotionals.question: Do I know my "before and after" story well enough to tell it in three minutes?
devotionals.scripture: And he said: 'I am a Jew... educated at the feet of Gamaliel...' — Acts 22:3
devotionals.title: My Own Testimony
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for my story. Help me to share how You changed me with clarity and humility.

devotionals.question: Is there a step of obedience I’ve been "waiting" on? What am I waiting for?
devotionals.reflection: Faith isn't a spectator sport. There comes a moment where "thinking about it" must turn into "acting on it."
devotionals.scripture: And now why do you wait? Rise and be baptized and wash away your sins... — Acts 22:16
devotionals.title: Why Do You Wait?
devotionals.prayer: Father, I rise today. I stop the hesitation and I step into the obedience You’ve called me to.

devotionals.reflection: God’s plans are often bigger than our comfort zones. He doesn't send us "near" just to keep us safe; He sends us "far" to make us useful.
devotionals.question: Am I willing to go "far away" (culturally or physically) for the sake of the Gospel?
devotionals.scripture: And he said to me, 'Go, for I will send you far away to the Gentiles.' — Acts 22:21
devotionals.title: Go, For I Will Send You
devotionals.prayer: Lord, send me. My "Yes" is on the table before I even know the destination.

devotionals.reflection: Paul used his legal rights to further the Gospel. We should use every "status" we have (citizenship, education, job) for God's purposes.
devotionals.question: How can I use my "standing" or "privilege" today to protect the vulnerable or share the Truth?
devotionals.scripture: But Paul said... 'I am a Roman citizen by birth.' — Acts 22:28
devotionals.title: Citizen of Heaven
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the doors my "citizenship" opens. Let me use them for Your Kingdom.

devotionals.question: Can I say today that I am living "before God" with a good conscience?
devotionals.reflection: Success is being able to look in the mirror and know you've been faithful to God. A clear conscience is the best pillow.
devotionals.scripture: Brothers, I have lived my life before God in all good conscience up to this day. — Acts 23:1
devotionals.title: A Good Conscience
devotionals.prayer: Lord, search me. Cleanse any hidden motives so I can stand before You with nothing to hide.

devotionals.question: Do I feel the "standing by" presence of Jesus in my current struggle?
devotionals.reflection: When the room is dark and the future is uncertain, Jesus "stands by" His people. You are never truly alone in the "barracks."
devotionals.scripture: The following night the Lord stood by him and said, 'Take courage...' — Acts 23:11
devotionals.title: The Lord Stood by Him
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for staying with me. I take courage because You are right here.

devotionals.reflection: God’s "Must" overrides man’s "No." If God says you are going to Rome, no plot in Jerusalem can stop you.
devotionals.question: What "Rome" (God-given goal) has God put in my heart that I am worrying about?
devotionals.scripture: ...so you must testify also in Rome. — Acts 23:11
devotionals.title: You Must Testify in Rome
devotionals.prayer: Sovereign God, Your "Must" is my anchor. I trust that You will get me to where You want me to be.

devotionals.reflection: God uses "ordinary" family members and "accidental" eavesdropping to thwart the plans of the enemy.
devotionals.question: Do I trust that God is exposing the "ambushes" against me before they even reach me?
devotionals.scripture: Now the son of Paul's sister heard of their ambush... — Acts 23:16
devotionals.title: The Plot Exposed
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for Your hidden protections. You see the traps I don't, and You lead me around them.

devotionals.reflection: God provided 470 Roman soldiers to protect one apostle. When God wants you moved, He will provide the "escort."
devotionals.question: Am I worrying about my "safety" instead of trusting God's massive "provision"?
devotionals.scripture: Get ready two hundred soldiers... to go as far as Caesarea. — Acts 23:23
devotionals.title: A Great Bodyguard
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the "soldiers" of Your providence. I am safe in Your hands.

devotionals.question: Am I letting the world’s "labels" for Christians discourage me or define me?
devotionals.reflection: Don't let others define your faith with their labels. You aren't part of a "sect"; you are walking in "The Way."
devotionals.scripture: But this I confess... according to the Way, which they call a sect, I worship the God of our fathers. — Acts 24:14
devotionals.title: The Way They Call a Sect
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I am a follower of The Way. I find my identity in Your Truth, not the world’s opinion.

devotionals.question: What "pains" do I need to take today to clear my conscience with someone?
devotionals.reflection: Having a clear conscience takes "pains" (effort). It means making things right quickly and living transparently.
devotionals.scripture: So I always take pains to have a clear conscience toward both God and man. — Acts 24:16
devotionals.title: Always a Clear Conscience
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, nudge me toward honesty. Help me to live a life with nothing to hide.

devotionals.reflection: The Gospel should "alarm" the comfortable. We don't just talk about "love"; we talk about the "judgment" that makes love necessary.
devotionals.question: Am I "toning down" the Gospel to keep people from being "alarmed"?
devotionals.scripture: And as he reasoned about righteousness and self-control and the coming judgment, Felix was alarmed... — Acts 24:25
devotionals.title: Reasoning on Righteousness
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, give me the boldness to speak the whole Truth—righteousness, self-control, and Your return.

devotionals.question: Is there something God is asking me to do "today" that I am pushing off until a "convenient time"?
devotionals.reflection: Felix waited for a "convenient time" that never came. Tomorrow is the devil’s favorite day to repent.
devotionals.scripture: ...Go away for the present. When I get a convenient time I will summon you. — Acts 24:25
devotionals.title: Convenient Time
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I stop procrastinating. Today is the day of obedience. I hear Your voice now.

devotionals.reflection: Two years! Sometimes God’s "fast track" includes a long "waiting room." Silence from God isn't an absence of God.
devotionals.question: How am I using my "prison time" (waiting for the next season)? Am I bitter or am I building?
devotionals.scripture: But when two years had elapsed... Felix left Paul in prison. — Acts 24:27
devotionals.title: Left Him in Prison
devotionals.prayer: Father, I trust You with the clock. Even in the "two-year" delays, I believe You are working.

devotionals.question: Am I wasting my breath on "small" critics instead of appealing to my "High Priest" in Heaven?
devotionals.reflection: Paul knew when to stop arguing with "small" leaders and appeal to the Highest Power. He was aiming for the heart of the Empire.
devotionals.scripture: I appeal to Caesar. — Acts 25:11
devotionals.title: I Appeal to Caesar
devotionals.prayer: King Jesus, I appeal to You. You are the ultimate Authority over my life and my case.

devotionals.question: Do I realize that my "decisions" today are the "vehicles" God is using to get me to my "Rome"?
devotionals.reflection: God uses our own "appeals" and legal choices to fulfill His ancient promises to us. Your "choices" are part of His "plan."
devotionals.scripture: You have appealed to Caesar; to Caesar you shall go. — Acts 25:12
devotionals.title: To Caesar You Shall Go
devotionals.prayer: Lord, guide my appeals and my choices. Let them lead me exactly where You want me to go.

devotionals.question: Am I being "dazzled" by the world's "pomp" (wealth, fame) while ignoring the spiritual power I carry?
devotionals.reflection: The world brings "pomp"; Paul brought "Power." Never be intimidated by the "show" when you have the "Substance."
devotionals.scripture: So on the next day Agrippa and Bernice came with great pomp... — Acts 25:23
devotionals.title: Great Pomp
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are the only one truly worthy of "pomp." I worship the King, not the costumes of men.

devotionals.reflection: Even a pagan governor recognized that Paul had done nothing "illegal." The only "charge" against him was the Gospel.
devotionals.question: If I were "charged" today, would there be enough "evidence" of my faith to convict me?
devotionals.scripture: For it seems to me unreasonable... to send a prisoner without indicating the charges... — Acts 25:27
devotionals.title: Not Without a Charge
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let my only "crime" be that I love You too much and serve You too well.

devotionals.reflection: Paul didn't see himself as a "victim," but as "fortunate." Gratitude is a weapon that changes the atmosphere of a trial.
devotionals.question: Can I find a reason to "consider myself fortunate" in the middle of my current challenge?
devotionals.scripture: I consider myself fortunate that it is before you, King Agrippa, I am to make my defense... — Acts 26:2
devotionals.title: I Consider Myself Fortunate
devotionals.prayer: Father, I thank You for this opportunity. I refuse the "victim" label and I claim the "witness" label.

devotionals.question: How is God using my "past" (even the "strict" or "wrong" parts) to help me reach people today?
devotionals.reflection: God doesn't "waste" your past. He used Paul’s "strictest" training to make him the most effective defender of Grace.
devotionals.scripture: ...according to the strictest party of our religion I have lived as a Pharisee. — Acts 26:5
devotionals.title: According to the Strictest Party
devotionals.prayer: Lord, use my history. Take every lesson I learned "before Jesus" and use it for Your glory.

devotionals.question: What "impossible" thing am I afraid to ask for because I think it’s "too incredible" for God?
devotionals.reflection: If God is God, "impossible" is just a word. Resurrection is only "incredible" if your God is too small.
devotionals.scripture: Why is it thought incredible... that God raises the dead? — Acts 26:8
devotionals.title: Why Is It Incredible?
devotionals.prayer: God of the Resurrection, nothing is too hard for You. I believe You can raise what is dead in my life.

devotionals.question: Where am I currently "kicking" against God’s direction and only hurting myself?
devotionals.reflection: A "goad" was a sharp stick used to move oxen. Resisting God’s will only hurts us. Submission is the path to peace.
devotionals.scripture: It is hard for you to kick against the goads. — Acts 26:14
devotionals.title: Kicking Against the Goads
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I stop kicking. I yield to Your "nudge" and I follow Your lead.

devotionals.question: Who can I help "see" the Truth today by simply pointing them to the Light?
devotionals.reflection: This is the mission: Eyes opened, Darkness turned, Power switched. We are "optometrists" for the soul.
devotionals.scripture: ...to open their eyes, so that they may turn from darkness to light... — Acts 26:18
devotionals.title: To Open Their Eyes
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, use me to open eyes. Let my life be a "flashlight" that helps others find their way out of the dark.

devotionals.question: Am I still being faithful to the "vision" (the call) God gave me a year ago?
devotionals.reflection: Success isn't "being popular"; it’s being "not disobedient." Staying true to what God told you in the light is the key to surviving the dark.
devotionals.scripture: Therefore, O King Agrippa, I was not disobedient to the heavenly vision... — Acts 26:19
devotionals.title: Not Disobedient to the Vision
devotionals.prayer: Lord, keep me faithful. I want to stand before You and say I was not disobedient to Your call.

devotionals.reflection: Paul didn't survive on "grit"; he survived on "Help from God." It is a daily delivery that never fails.
devotionals.question: Have I asked for my "Help from God" today, or am I trying to "grit" it out on my own?
devotionals.scripture: To this day I have had the help that comes from God... — Acts 26:22
devotionals.title: Help from God
devotionals.prayer: Father, I need Your help today. I can't do this without Your sustaining grace.

devotionals.reflection: Paul didn't need "new" revelations; he just needed the "True" ones. Stay anchored in the Word and you’ll never go astray.
devotionals.question: Am I looking for "new/shiny" truths, or am I anchored in the "Old/True" Word of God?
devotionals.scripture: ...saying nothing but what the prophets and Moses said... — Acts 26:22
devotionals.title: Saying Nothing But...
devotionals.prayer: Lord, keep me in the Scriptures. Let my message be nothing but what You have already spoken.

devotionals.reflection: When the world can't explain your joy or your peace, they call you "crazy." Take it as a compliment.
devotionals.question: Does my "sanity" look like the world's, or am I "crazy" enough to believe the Gospel?
devotionals.scripture: Paul, you are out of your mind; your great learning is driving you out of your mind. — Acts 26:24
devotionals.title: You Are Out of Your Mind!
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, if I’m "out of my mind," let it be for You. I’d rather be "crazy" with You than "sane" without You.

devotionals.reflection: Agrippa was "almost" persuaded. "Almost" is the saddest word in the spiritual dictionary. Decisions must be made.
devotionals.question: Is there someone in my life who is "almost" there? How can I pray for their "final step" today?
devotionals.scripture: Agrippa said to Paul, 'In a short time would you persuade me to be a Christian?' — Acts 26:28
devotionals.title: Persuaded to be a Christian?
devotionals.prayer: Lord, finish the work in the "almosts." Turn their curiosity into commitment today.

devotionals.reflection: Paul wanted everyone to have his "Joy," but none of his "Chains." That is the heart of a true evangelist.
devotionals.question: Do I want others to experience the "freedom" I have in Christ, regardless of my own "chains"?
devotionals.scripture: Whether short or long, I would to God that... all who hear me this day might become such as I am—except for these chains. — Acts 26:29
devotionals.title: Whether Short or Long
devotionals.prayer: Father, bless my "listeners" today. Let them find the joy I have found in You.

devotionals.question: What "shore" of safety is God asking me to leave today so I can "put to sea" with Him?
devotionals.reflection: Every "embarking" involves a risk. When we follow God, we have to leave the "safety" of the shore and trust the "Ship" of His Will.
devotionals.scripture: And embarking in a ship... we put to sea. — Acts 27:2
devotionals.title: We Put to Sea
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I’m on board. I trust You with the winds and the waves as we move toward Rome.

devotionals.question: Am I looking for the "kindnesses" God is sending me through "unlikely" people today?
devotionals.reflection: God can give you "favor" even with your "captors." Kindness is a tool God uses to refresh His servants on long journeys.
devotionals.scripture: Julius treated Paul kindly and gave him leave to go to his friends... — Acts 27:3
devotionals.title: Treated with Kindness
devotionals.prayer: Thank You, Lord, for the "Julius" in my life. Thank You for the unexpected favors and refreshments.

devotionals.question: Am I listening to the "warning signs" the Spirit is giving me about a certain path?
devotionals.reflection: There is a "spiritual calendar" and a "natural calendar." Wisdom knows when it’s "dangerous" to move, even if others are pushing.
devotionals.scripture: ...because the fast was already over, sailing was now dangerous. — Acts 27:9
devotionals.title: Sailing was Dangerous
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me discernment. Help me to know when to "wait in the harbor" and when to sail.

devotionals.question: Am I prepared to be "right" but still have to endure the "storm" with those who ignored me?
devotionals.reflection: Wisdom is often ignored by those in a hurry. Just because you are "warned" doesn't mean you won't have to go through the storm anyway.
devotionals.scripture: Paul advised them, saying, 'Sirs, I perceive that the voyage will be with injury...' — Acts 27:10
devotionals.title: Men, I Perceive...
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me patience with those who won't listen. Help me to be a source of strength when the "injury" comes.

devotionals.reflection: The "majority" is often wrong in spiritual matters. Truth isn't a democracy; it’s a revelation.
devotionals.question: Am I following the "majority" or am I following the "Message" God gave me?
devotionals.scripture: ...the majority decided to put to sea from there... — Acts 27:12
devotionals.title: The Majority Advised
devotionals.prayer: Lord, help me stand with the Truth, even when the "majority" is sailing in the other direction.

devotionals.question: Am I being "fooled" by a "gentle wind" in an area where God has already said "No"?
devotionals.reflection: Don't trust a "gentle breeze" if God has already given a "warning." Success isn't always a sign of God’s approval.
devotionals.scripture: Now when the south wind blew gently, supposing that they had obtained their purpose... — Acts 27:13
devotionals.title: A Moderate South Wind
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, keep me from being deceived by easy circumstances. I want Your will, not just an easy path.

devotionals.question: Am I "surprised" by the "Northeaster" in my life right now? Will I stay on the ship?
devotionals.reflection: Suddenly the weather changes. Storms don't mean you are "out of God’s will"; they just mean you are on the "high seas."
devotionals.scripture: But soon a tempestuous wind, called the Northeaster, struck down... — Acts 27:14
devotionals.title: A Tempestuous Wind
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I’m not jumping overboard. I trust You even when the "tempestuous wind" strikes.

devotionals.question: Is there a "storm" I need to stop "fighting" and start "trusting" God to "drive" me through?
devotionals.reflection: Sometimes you can't "row" against the storm; you just have to "give way" and let God "drive" you where He wants.
devotionals.scripture: And when the ship was caught... we gave way to it and were driven along. — Acts 27:15
devotionals.title: We Gave Way to It
devotionals.prayer: Father, I stop the "rowing." I give way to Your Spirit. Drive me where You want me to go.

devotionals.question: Am I at the point of "abandoning hope" in my own strength? (Good! Now watch God.)
devotionals.reflection: God often waits until "all hope is abandoned" to show that He is the only Hope left. Despair is the "backdoor" to a miracle.
devotionals.scripture: ...all hope of our being saved was at last abandoned. — Acts 27:20
devotionals.title: All Hope Abandoned
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are my only Hope. When the stars are hidden and the storm is loud, I look to You.

devotionals.reflection: One person with "Word from God" can change the morale of an entire ship. Your "Take Heart" could save someone today.
devotionals.question: Who can I tell to "Take Heart" today based on a promise I’ve found in God's Word?
devotionals.scripture: I urge you to take heart, for there will be no loss of life among you... — Acts 27:22
devotionals.title: Take Heart!
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me an encourager. Let my "Take Heart" be infectious to everyone in this storm.

devotionals.question: Do I live today as someone who "belongs to God," or as someone who is an "orphan" in the storm?
devotionals.reflection: Security comes from knowing Who you belong to. If you "belong" to the Creator of the Sea, the sea cannot swallow you.
devotionals.scripture: For this very night there stood before me an angel of the God to whom I belong... — Acts 27:23
devotionals.title: To Whom I Belong
devotionals.prayer: Father, I belong to You. I am Your property, and You take care of what is Yours.

devotionals.question: Do I believe it will be "exactly" as God said, or am I "hedging my bets" just in case?
devotionals.reflection: Faith isn't "vague hope"; it’s the conviction that it will be "exactly as I have been told." God’s Word is literal.
devotionals.scripture: So take heart, men, for I have faith in God that it will be exactly as I have been told. — Acts 27:25
devotionals.title: I Have Faith in God
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I believe You. Let it be "exactly" as You have spoken. I stand on Your literal Word.

devotionals.question: Am I willing to "lose the ship" if it means "saving the life" God has for me?
devotionals.reflection: Breakthrough often involves a "wreck." You might lose the "ship" (the business, the plan), but you will save the "life."
devotionals.scripture: But we must run aground on some island. — Acts 27:26
devotionals.title: We Must Run Aground
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I’m okay with the "wreck" as long as I’m with You. I trust Your "running aground" plan.

devotionals.question: What "anchors" am I letting down today to keep me from "hitting the rocks" of despair?
devotionals.reflection: When we are "fearing the rocks," we need "anchors." Prayer, Word, Fellowship, and Praise are the four anchors for any storm.
devotionals.scripture: And fearing that we might run on the rocks, they let down four anchors... — Acts 27:29
devotionals.title: Fearing the Rocks
devotionals.prayer: Father, I let down my anchors. I am grounded in Your Truth, and I wait for the dawn.

devotionals.reflection: Sometimes the most spiritual thing you can do is just "wait for the day." The sun will rise; you just have to stay anchored.
devotionals.question: Am I "praying for day" (patience) or am I trying to "force the dawn" in my own strength?
devotionals.scripture: ...they let down four anchors from the stern and prayed for day. — Acts 27:29
devotionals.title: Praying for Day
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I wait for the day. I trust that the morning is coming and Your mercies will be new.

devotionals.question: Am I tempted to "jump ship" right before the miracle? Will I stay the course?
devotionals.reflection: God’s promises often have "conditions." We have to "stay in the ship" (stay in the Church, stay in the Word) to see the salvation.
devotionals.scripture: Paul said... 'Unless these men stay in the ship, you cannot be saved.' — Acts 27:31
devotionals.title: Unless You Stay
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I’m staying in the ship. I won't run away when things get tough; I will stay and see Your salvation.

devotionals.question: Am I neglecting my "strength" (rest/food) while trying to "believe" for a miracle?
devotionals.reflection: Paul was "spiritual," but he was also "practical." You can't fight a spiritual battle on an empty stomach and no sleep.
devotionals.scripture: Therefore I urge you to take some food. It will give you strength... — Acts 27:34
devotionals.title: Take Some Food
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the practical. Help me to eat, rest, and gain the strength I need for the final stretch.

devotionals.reflection: God is interested in the "details." If He numbers the hairs on your head, He certainly cares about the "major" things too.
devotionals.question: How does God's "hair-level" detail of care give me peace about my "big" problems today?
devotionals.scripture: ...for not a hair is to perish from the head of any of you. — Acts 27:34
devotionals.title: Not a Hair Will Perish
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for Your detailed care. I trust You with every "hair" of my life.

devotionals.reflection: Paul "said grace" in the middle of a shipwreck. Gratitude is a witness that transcends our environment.
devotionals.question: Will I "give thanks" today even if my "ship" is currently falling apart?
devotionals.scripture: And when he had said these things, he took bread, and giving thanks to God in the presence of all... — Acts 27:35
devotionals.title: Giving Thanks in Public
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I give You thanks. Right here, in the middle of the mess, I acknowledge Your goodness.

devotionals.question: Who can I "infect" with encouragement today by simply being "steady" in my own faith?
devotionals.reflection: Courage is contagious. When you eat and give thanks, others feel like they can do it too.
devotionals.scripture: Then they all were encouraged and ate some food themselves. — Acts 27:36
devotionals.title: All Were Encouraged
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me a "contagious" believer. Let my peace become someone else's strength.

devotionals.reflection: It wasn't "pretty"—they arrived on planks and pieces of the ship—but they arrived. God’s "arrival" isn't always "elegant," but it is always "certain."
devotionals.question: Am I "complaining" about how I’m arriving (the broken planks) or am I "celebrating" that I’m arriving?
devotionals.scripture: And so it was that all were brought safely to land. — Acts 27:44
devotionals.title: Reaching the Land
devotionals.prayer: Father, I made it! Thank You for the "broken planks" that carried me to the shore. I am safe.

devotionals.reflection: God provides "unusual" kindness in "unlikely" places. Sometimes the "natives" (the world) show more grace than the "religious."
devotionals.question: Am I open to receiving "unusual kindness" from people who don't even know Jesus yet?
devotionals.scripture: The native people showed us unusual kindness... — Acts 28:2
devotionals.title: Unusual Kindness
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for the "fire" and the "kindness" of strangers. Help me to be grateful for every source of help.

devotionals.question: What "viper" of criticism or "bad luck" am I letting cling to me? Will I shake it off today?
devotionals.reflection: After the storm comes the "viper." The enemy always has one last "bite" ready. Just "shake it off" and keep going.
devotionals.scripture: He, however, shook off the creature into the fire and suffered no harm. — Acts 28:5
devotionals.title: Shaking Off the Viper
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I shake it off. I refuse to let the enemy’s "bite" stop me from the mission. I suffer no harm in You.

devotionals.question: Am I living for the "mind-changing" opinions of people, or the "unchanging" Word of God?
devotionals.reflection: People’s opinions are "fickle." One minute you are a "murderer," the next you are a "god." Don't live for either label.
devotionals.scripture: ...they changed their minds and said that he was a god. — Acts 28:6
devotionals.title: He Is a God
devotionals.prayer: Father, I don't care what they call me. I only care what You call me.

devotionals.question: How can I be "hospitable" to someone today to open a door for the Spirit?
devotionals.reflection: As we end this block, we see that "Hospitality" is the final bridge to the Gospel in Acts. Kindness opens the door for the Kingdom.
devotionals.scripture: ...who received us and entertained us hospitably for three days. — Acts 28:7
devotionals.title: Welcome the Gospel
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me a host for Your Gospel. Use my home, my time, and my kindness to "entertain" Your Truth in the hearts of others.

devotionals.question: Who can I "lay hands on" (offer a physical gesture of prayer/comfort) today?
devotionals.reflection: Paul didn't just "wish him well"; he took action. Prayer combined with the "laying on of hands" is a physical sign of spiritual power.
devotionals.scripture: Paul visited him and prayed, and putting his hands on him, healed him. — Acts 28:8
devotionals.title: Prayed and Laid Hands
devotionals.prayer: Lord, use my hands to bring Your healing touch to someone who is hurting.

devotionals.reflection: When you bless people with God's power, they often respond with "honors." Use those resources to fuel the next leg of the mission.
devotionals.question: Am I being a "blessing" to my community in a way that makes them want to support God's work?
devotionals.scripture: They also honored us greatly, and when we were about to sail, they put on board whatever we needed. — Acts 28:10
devotionals.title: Honored Us Greatly
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the "provisions" that come through the people I serve. Let me use them wisely.

devotionals.question: What "Rome" (long-term goal) have I finally reached? Am I taking a moment to say, "And so I came"?
devotionals.reflection: Five words that represent years of struggle, shipwrecks, and prayers. God's "Arrival" is inevitable, even if the journey is long.
devotionals.scripture: And so we came to Rome. — Acts 28:14
devotionals.title: We Came to Rome
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for getting me here. I acknowledge Your faithfulness in every mile of the journey.

devotionals.reflection: Even the "Great Apostle" needed to see his friends to "take courage." We are designed for "face-to-face" fellowship.
devotionals.question: Who can I "see" today (in person or video) to give and receive a boost of courage?
devotionals.scripture: ...on seeing them, Paul thanked God and took courage. — Acts 28:15
devotionals.title: Taking Courage
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for my brothers and sisters. Their presence is Your "courage" to me.

devotionals.reflection: Paul was "in Rome," but he was still "chained." God can use you even when your "freedom" is limited by a soldier (or a circumstance).
devotionals.question: How can I be "effective for the Gospel" even within the "chains" of my current limitations?
devotionals.scripture: Paul was allowed to stay by himself, with the soldier who guarded him. — Acts 28:16
devotionals.title: Under Guard
devotionals.prayer: Father, I am Your prisoner, not the world’s. Use me right where I am, even in my "confinement."

devotionals.reflection: Paul didn't use his platform to "vent" about his unfair treatment. He stayed focused on the "Hope of Israel."
devotionals.question: Am I "venting" about my problems, or am I "pointing" to my Hope?
devotionals.scripture: ...though I had done nothing against our people... yet I was delivered as a prisoner. — Acts 28:17
devotionals.title: Nothing Against My People
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, help me to stop talking about my "chains" and start talking about Your "Grace."

devotionals.question: Am I living for my "own kingdom" today, or am I "expounding" the Kingdom of God?
devotionals.reflection: The "Kingdom of God" is the big picture. It’s not just about "me and Jesus"; it’s about "God and the World."
devotionals.scripture: From morning till evening he expounded to them, testifying to the kingdom of God... — Acts 28:23
devotionals.title: Expounding the Kingdom
devotionals.prayer: Lord, Your Kingdom come! Let my life be a testimony to Your reign and Your rule.

devotionals.question: Am I "convinced" today, or am I letting "disbelief" creep in because of my circumstances?
devotionals.reflection: The same message produces different results in different hearts. Don't take "disbelief" as a personal failure.
devotionals.scripture: And some were convinced... while others disbelieved. — Acts 28:24
devotionals.title: Some Disbelieved
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, convince me again. I choose to be among the "some" who believe.

devotionals.question: Who is the "Gentile" (the person outside my circle) that I need to "send" the Gospel to today?
devotionals.reflection: The Gospel is "Sent." It is a missionary message that always moves toward the "outsider."
devotionals.scripture: Therefore let it be known to you that this salvation... has been sent to the Gentiles. — Acts 28:28
devotionals.title: This Salvation is Sent
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for sending salvation to me. Help me to "send" it to someone else.

devotionals.question: Is my "home" (or my heart) an "open door" where I welcome everyone who comes to me?
devotionals.reflection: Acts ends not with a "period," but with an "open door." Paul’s rented house was a "Welcome Center" for the Kingdom.
devotionals.scripture: He lived there two whole years... and welcomed all who came to him. — Acts 28:30
devotionals.title: Welcoming All
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me a "welcomer." Let my life be an invitation for others to meet You.

devotionals.question: Do I believe that the Gospel is "unhindered" in my life today, despite my obstacles?
devotionals.reflection: The book of Acts ends with the word "unhindered" (in Greek). Nothing can stop the Gospel!
devotionals.scripture: ...proclaiming the kingdom of God... with all boldness and without hindrance. — Acts 28:31
devotionals.title: Proclaiming with Boldness
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I speak Your Word with boldness. I believe that nothing can "hinder" Your work in me.

devotionals.reflection: Paul starts his letter to the Romans with his "ID Card": Servant and Called. You are first a "Servant," then a "Leader."
devotionals.question: Do I see myself as a "Servant of Christ Jesus" before I see myself as anything else?
devotionals.scripture: Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle... — Romans 1:1
devotionals.title: Servant of Christ Jesus
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I am Your servant. I accept Your "Call" on my life today.

devotionals.question: Am I trying to "invent" my own way to God, or am I following the "Gospel of God"?
devotionals.reflection: The Gospel isn't a human invention; it belongs to God. He is the Author, the Content, and the Power of the message.
devotionals.scripture: ...set apart for the gospel of God. — Romans 1:1
devotionals.title: The Gospel of God
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for Your Gospel. I stand on the Truth that You have provided.

devotionals.reflection: The Resurrection is the "Seal" on Jesus' identity. If He rose, He is who He said He was. Period.
devotionals.question: Does the reality of the Resurrection give me "Power" for my daily life today?
devotionals.scripture: ...and was declared to be the Son of God in power... by his resurrection. — Romans 1:4
devotionals.title: Declared to be the Son
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are the Son of God. I live in the power of Your Resurrection today.

devotionals.question: Is my "faith" producing "obedience" in my choices today?
devotionals.reflection: Faith isn't just "believing"; it’s "obeying." We trust God so much that we do what He says.
devotionals.scripture: ...to bring about the obedience of faith for the sake of his name. — Romans 1:5
devotionals.title: Obedience of Faith
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I trust You. Help me to "obey" Your Word out of a heart of love and faith.

devotionals.question: How does "belonging to Jesus" change my sense of security and worth today?
devotionals.reflection: You are "Called to Belong." You are not an orphan; you are a member of the Family of God.
devotionals.scripture: ...including you who are called to belong to Jesus Christ. — Romans 1:6
devotionals.title: Called to Belong
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for calling me. I am proud to belong to Jesus Christ.

devotionals.question: Can I sit for a moment and just "feel" the truth that I am "Loved by God"?
devotionals.reflection: You are "Loved by God." That is your primary status. You aren't just "useful" to Him; You are "cherished" by Him.
devotionals.scripture: To all those in Rome who are loved by God and called to be saints... — Romans 1:7
devotionals.title: Loved by God
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for Your love. Let me live today as someone who is deeply loved by You.

devotionals.reflection: Is your "faith" something people talk about? A strong faith leaves a "scent" that others notice.
devotionals.question: What is being "proclaimed" about my faith in my workplace or my neighborhood?
devotionals.scripture: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ... because your faith is proclaimed in all the world. — Romans 1:8
devotionals.title: Faith is Proclaimed
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let my faith be "talked about" because it is real and visible to others.

devotionals.reflection: Real service isn't just "doing things"; it’s serving with your "spirit." It’s an internal devotion that moves external actions.
devotionals.question: Is my "service" for God coming from my "spirit," or am I just going through the motions?
devotionals.scripture: For God is my witness, whom I serve with my spirit in the gospel... — Romans 1:9
devotionals.title: Serving with the Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Father, I serve You with my spirit. Let my heart be in everything I do for Your Gospel.

devotionals.question: Who can I "mutually encourage" today by simply sharing what God is doing in my life?
devotionals.reflection: Even the "Apostle Paul" needed to be encouraged by the faith of "ordinary" Romans. We all need each other.
devotionals.scripture: ...that we may be mutually encouraged by each other's faith, both yours and mine. — Romans 1:12
devotionals.title: Mutually Encouraged
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for the Body of Christ. Help us to "mutually encourage" one another today.

devotionals.question: Do I feel an "obligation" to share the Grace I’ve received with those who haven't heard it?
devotionals.reflection: Paul felt "indebted" to share the Gospel. He didn't see it as a "hobby," but as a "debt" he owed to the world.
devotionals.scripture: I am under obligation both to Greeks and to barbarians... — Romans 1:14
devotionals.title: Under Obligation
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I am a debtor to Your Grace. Help me to "repay" that debt by sharing Your Love with everyone.

devotionals.question: Am I "eager" to share the Good News, or does it feel like a "chore"?
devotionals.reflection: Eagerness is the opposite of "duty." Paul loved the Gospel so much he couldn't wait to talk about it.
devotionals.scripture: So I am eager to preach the gospel to you also who are in Rome. — Romans 1:15
devotionals.title: Eager to Preach
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me an "eager" heart. Let me be excited to tell others about Jesus.

devotionals.reflection: The world might think it’s "foolish," but the Gospel is the only thing that actually works. Don't be ashamed of the Truth.
devotionals.question: Is there any area of my life where I am "ashamed" to be known as a follower of Jesus?
devotionals.scripture: For I am not ashamed of the gospel... — Romans 1:16a
devotionals.title: Not Ashamed
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I am not ashamed. You are my Glory and my Strength. I lift Your Name high.

devotionals.question: Am I relying on my "advice" to change people, or am I relying on the "Power" of the Gospel?
devotionals.reflection: The Gospel isn't "advice"; it’s "Power." It’s the "Dynamite" (Dunamis) of God that blasts through sin and death.
devotionals.scripture: ...for it is the power of God for salvation to everyone who believes. — Romans 1:16b
devotionals.title: The Power of God
devotionals.prayer: Lord, unleash Your power. Let the Gospel change me and everyone I speak to today.

devotionals.question: Am I "living by faith" today, or am I living by "sight" and "worry"?
devotionals.reflection: Faith isn't a one-time "prayer"; it’s a "living." We breathe faith, walk faith, and act faith every single day.
devotionals.scripture: For in it the righteousness of God is revealed... As it is written, 'The righteous shall live by faith.' — Romans 1:17
devotionals.title: The Righteous Shall Live by Faith
devotionals.prayer: Father, I live by faith. I trust Your righteousness and I walk in Your Truth today.

devotionals.reflection: People don't "miss" the truth; they "suppress" it. The problem isn't a "lack of evidence"; it’s a "willful rejection."
devotionals.question: Am I "suppressing" any truth in my own life because it’s "uncomfortable"?
devotionals.scripture: For the wrath of God is revealed... against all... who by their unrighteousness suppress the truth. — Romans 1:18
devotionals.title: Suppressing the Truth
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I welcome Your Truth. I refuse to "suppress" what You are trying to show me.

devotionals.question: Have I taken a moment today to "clearly perceive" God's glory in the "things that have been made"?
devotionals.reflection: Nature is God's "Billboard." The stars, the trees, and the ocean all shout "Creator!" No one has an excuse to ignore Him.
devotionals.scripture: For his invisible attributes... have been clearly perceived... in the things that have been made. — Romans 1:20
devotionals.title: Clearly Perceived
devotionals.prayer: Creator God, I see Your hand everywhere. Thank You for the "witness" of Your creation.

devotionals.reflection: Human "wisdom" that leaves out God is the ultimate "folly." True wisdom begins with the Fear of the Lord.
devotionals.question: Am I relying on my "own wisdom" today, or am I asking for the "Wisdom from Above"?
devotionals.scripture: Claiming to be wise, they became fools. — Romans 1:22
devotionals.title: Thinking They Were Wise
devotionals.prayer: Father, keep me from being a "wise fool." Give me Your Wisdom and Your Perspective.

devotionals.question: What "bad trades" am I tempted to make today? (Comfort for Truth? Popularity for Integrity?)
devotionals.reflection: Sin is always a "bad trade." We trade the "Eternal Creator" for a "Temporary Creature." It’s a losing deal every time.
devotionals.scripture: ...because they exchanged the truth about God for a lie... — Romans 1:25
devotionals.title: Exchanged the Truth
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are the Truth. I refuse to trade You for any lie the world offers.

devotionals.reflection: We were made to "worship." If we don't worship God, we will worship "stuff." Idolatry is just "misplaced worship."
devotionals.question: What "creature" (person, thing, dream) am I tempted to "worship" today?
devotionals.scripture: ...and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator. — Romans 1:25
devotionals.title: Worshiped the Creature
devotionals.prayer: Creator God, You alone are worthy of my worship. I turn my heart away from "stuff" and toward You.

devotionals.reflection: Sometimes God's judgment is simply "letting us have what we want." A heart "given up" to its own desires is a dangerous place.
devotionals.question: Am I asking God to "let me have my way," or am I asking Him to "have His way" in me?
devotionals.scripture: Therefore God gave them up in the lusts of their hearts... — Romans 1:24
devotionals.title: Gave Them Up
devotionals.prayer: Lord, don't "give me up" to my own desires. Keep me close to Your heart and Your will.

devotionals.reflection: It’s one thing to "sin"; it’s another to "celebrate" it. Our culture often "gives approval" to what God has decreed against.
devotionals.question: Am I "giving approval" to things that God calls "unrighteous" just to be "nice"?
devotionals.scripture: Though they know God's righteous decree... they not only do them but give approval to those who practice them. — Romans 1:32
devotionals.title: Knowing God’s Decree
devotionals.prayer: Father, help me to stand for Your decrees with grace. Let me love the sinner but never "approve" the sin.

devotionals.question: Am I "judging others" for things that I am also doing in my own heart?
devotionals.reflection: It’s easy to point at "the world’s" sins while ignoring our own "religious" ones. We are all on level ground before the Judge.
devotionals.scripture: Therefore you have no excuse, O man... for in passing judgment on another you condemn yourself. — Romans 1:2:1
devotionals.title: No Excuse, O Man
devotionals.prayer: Lord, be the Judge of my heart. I stop pointing fingers and I start repenting of my own pride.

devotionals.question: Am I responding to "God's kindness" today by "turning" (repenting) toward Him?
devotionals.reflection: God doesn't "shame" us into change; He "kindnesses" us into it. His goodness is the most powerful motivation for transformation.
devotionals.scripture: ...God's kindness is meant to lead you to repentance. — Romans 2:4
devotionals.title: Kindnesses Leads to Repentance
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for Your kindness. It softens my heart and makes me want to be like You.

devotionals.reflection: God’s "silence" isn't "approval." It’s "patience." He is giving us time to turn before the judgment comes.
devotionals.question: Am I "presuming" on God’s patience, or am I taking my "turn" seriously today?
devotionals.scripture: ...do you presume on the riches of his kindness and forbearance and patience? — Romans 2:4
devotionals.title: Patience and Forbearance
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for Your forbearance. I don't take Your patience for granted; I turn to You now.

devotionals.reflection: A "hard heart" is like a "spiritual sponge" that only soaks up judgment. A "soft heart" soaks up grace.
devotionals.question: Is my heart "hard" toward God’s Word today? How can I ask Him to "soften" it?
devotionals.scripture: But because of your hard and impenitent heart you are storing up wrath for yourself... — Romans 2:5
devotionals.title: Hard and Impenitent Heart
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, break the "hardness" of my heart. Make me "repentant" and "open" to Your voice.

devotionals.reflection: Faith is the "root," but works are the "fruit." On the final day, God looks for the "evidence" of a changed life.
devotionals.question: If God "rendered to me" based on my "works" today, what story would my actions tell?
devotionals.scripture: He will render to each one according to his works. — Romans 2:6
devotionals.title: To Each According to Works
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let my "works" be a reflection of my "faith." May my actions show that I belong to You.

devotionals.question: Am I "showing up" to do good today, even if I don't "feel" like it?
devotionals.reflection: God values "patient continuance." It’s not about "flashes of brilliance"; it’s about "showing up" every day to do good.
devotionals.scripture: To those who by patience in well-doing seek for glory and honor... — Romans 2:7
devotionals.title: Patient Continuance
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me the "patience" to keep doing what is right. I seek Your Glory, not my own.

devotionals.reflection: God doesn't have "favorites" based on race, culture, or religious background. He sees every soul with the same perfect justice.
devotionals.question: Am I treating people with the same "impartiality" that God shows me?
devotionals.scripture: For God shows no partiality. — Romans 2:11
devotionals.title: No Partiality with God
devotionals.prayer: Lord, remove my biases. Help me to see every person as someone made in Your Image.

devotionals.question: What "truth" did I "hear" recently that I still need to "do"?
devotionals.reflection: Hearing a sermon doesn't make you holy; "doing" it does. Knowledge without action is just "religious entertainment."
devotionals.scripture: For it is not the hearers of the law who are righteous... but the doers of the law... — Romans 2:13
devotionals.title: Doers of the Law
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I want to be a "doer." Help me to put Your Word into practice in my real life today.

devotionals.reflection: Every human has a "God-given compass" called a conscience. It "accuses" or "excuses" us because we know there is a Right and a Wrong.
devotionals.question: Am I listening to my "conscience" today, or am I trying to "drown it out"?
devotionals.scripture: ...they show that the work of the law is written on their hearts... — Romans 2:15
devotionals.title: Law Written on Hearts
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for my conscience. Keep it sensitive to Your Spirit so I can walk in Your Way.

devotionals.reflection: God doesn't just see our "public" lives; He sees our "secret" thoughts. Integrity is being the same person in the dark.
devotionals.question: What "secrets" am I keeping that I need to bring into the light of God’s grace today?
devotionals.scripture: ...on that day when, according to my gospel, God judges the secrets of men... — Romans 2:16
devotionals.title: Judging the Secrets
devotionals.prayer: Lord, search my "secrets." Cleanse my hidden thoughts and make me "whole" inside and out.

devotionals.reflection: Knowing the Law is a responsibility. If you know "what is excellent," you are expected to live it out.
devotionals.question: Am I living up to the "instruction" I’ve received, or am I just "approving" it in theory?
devotionals.scripture: ...and know his will and approve what is excellent, because you are instructed in the law. — Romans 2:18
devotionals.title: Instruction in the Law
devotionals.prayer: Father, help me to live "what is excellent." Let my life match the "instruction" of Your Word.

devotionals.reflection: The hardest person to "teach" is often the "teacher." We must always be the first "student" of the truths we preach.
devotionals.question: Am I "teaching myself" the lessons I am trying to tell others?
devotionals.scripture: You then who teach others, do you not teach yourself? — Romans 2:21
devotionals.title: You Who Teach Others
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let me be a "student" first. May I live the message before I speak it to anyone else.

devotionals.reflection: Religious hypocrisy "dishonors God" because it makes the Gospel look "fake" to the world.
devotionals.question: Is there any area of "hypocrisy" in my life that is "dishonoring" God’s reputation?
devotionals.scripture: You who boast in the law dishonor God by breaking the law. — Romans 2:23
devotionals.title: Dishonoring God
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, forgive my hypocrisy. Make me "real" so that others will see Your honor in my life.

devotionals.question: How am I "carrying the Name of God" today in my interactions with non-believers?
devotionals.reflection: This is the ultimate warning: Our "bad behavior" causes others to "hate God." We carry His Name; we must carry it well.
devotionals.scripture: For... the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you. — Romans 2:24
devotionals.title: Blasphemed Among Gentiles
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let Your Name be "hallowed" because of me, not "blasphemed." Help me to represent You well.

devotionals.question: Has the Spirit done "heart surgery" on me lately, or am I just sticking to the "letter" of the rules?
devotionals.reflection: God doesn't care about "external rituals"; He cares about "internal surgery." The Spirit wants to "cut away" our old heart.
devotionals.scripture: ...circumcision is a matter of the heart, by the Spirit, not by the letter. — Romans 2:29
devotionals.title: A Matter of the Heart
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, do Your work in my heart. Cut away the pride and the selfishness that the "rules" can't reach.

devotionals.question: Whose "praise" am I seeking today—my boss's, my friends', or my Father's?
devotionals.reflection: If you live for "man’s praise," you are a slave. If you live for "God’s praise," you are free.
devotionals.scripture: His praise is not from man but from God. — Romans 2:29
devotionals.title: Praise Not from Man
devotionals.prayer: Father, I live for Your "Well Done." I care more about Your smile than the applause of men.

devotionals.reflection: People fail. Leaders fall. But God’s faithfulness is "un-nullify-able." He is the one Constant in a shifting world.
devotionals.question: Am I letting someone else’s "faithlessness" shake my trust in "God's faithfulness"?
devotionals.scripture: What if some were unfaithful? Does their faithlessness nullify the faithfulness of God? — Romans 3:3
devotionals.title: What if Some Were Unfaithful?
devotionals.prayer: Lord, let God be true though every man were a liar! I rest in Your unchanging Reliability.

devotionals.question: Have I stopped making "excuses" to God and simply admitted, "Lord, I need Your help"?
devotionals.reflection: Before the Law of God, we have no "excuses" left. We just stand in "silence" and admit our need.
devotionals.scripture: ...so that every mouth may be stopped, and the whole world may be held accountable... — Romans 3:19
devotionals.title: Every Mouth Stopped
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I stop talking. I admit my need. I am accountable to You, and I look to You for mercy.

devotionals.question: Do I really believe that I am "no better" than the "worst" sinner without God’s grace?
devotionals.reflection: This is the "bad news" that makes the "Good News" so good. We are all "in the same boat" of sin.
devotionals.scripture: None is righteous, no, not one... — Romans 3:10
devotionals.title: None is Righteous
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the "Equality of the Need." I humble myself before You today.

devotionals.reflection: We didn't "find" God; He "found" us. Left to ourselves, we would never even look for Him.
devotionals.question: Am I grateful today that God "sought me out" when I wasn't even looking for Him?
devotionals.scripture: ...no one understands; no one seeks for God. — Romans 3:11
devotionals.title: None Seeks for God
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for finding me. Thank You for seeking the lost and bringing me home.

devotionals.reflection: This is the "root" of every sin: a lack of "Awe" for God. When we don't "Fear" Him, we don't follow Him.
devotionals.question: How can I cultivate a healthy "Fear (Awe) of God" in my heart today?
devotionals.scripture: There is no fear of God before their eyes. — Romans 3:18
devotionals.title: No Fear of God
devotionals.prayer: Holy God, restore my wonder. Help me to live with the "Fear of the Lord" before my eyes all day.

devotionals.question: Am I using the Word to "see my need" (the mirror) or am I trying to "fix myself" (self-effort)?
devotionals.reflection: The Law is like a "mirror." It shows you that your face is "dirty," but it can't "wash" it. Only the Gospel can do the "washing."
devotionals.scripture: For by works of the law no human being will be justified... since through the law comes knowledge of sin. — Romans 3:20
devotionals.title: Knowledge of Sin
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for the "Mirror" of Your Word. Now, I look to the "Cross" for the "Washing."

devotionals.question: Am I trying to be "righteous" by my "rules," or by the "Righteousness of God" in Christ?
devotionals.reflection: God has provided a "New Way" to be right with Him—it’s not through "trying harder," it’s through "trusting Him."
devotionals.scripture: But now the righteousness of God has been manifested apart from the law... — Romans 3:21
devotionals.title: Apart from the Law
devotionals.prayer: Father, I receive Your "Righteousness." I stop trying to "earn" it and I start "trusting" it.

devotionals.question: Am I aware of my "shortfall" today? Am I leaning into the "Grace that closes the gap"?
devotionals.reflection: Sin literally means "missing the mark." We all "fall short" of God's perfect standard. But Grace closes the gap.
devotionals.scripture: ...for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God. — Romans 3:23
devotionals.title: All Have Sinned
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I fall short. Thank You for being the "Bridge" that carries me across to the Father.

devotionals.reflection: Justified means "Declared Righteous." And it’s a "Gift." You can't "buy" it; you can only "receive" it.
devotionals.question: Have I truly "accepted the gift" of justification today, or am I still trying to "pay" for it?
devotionals.scripture: ...and are justified by his grace as a gift... — Romans 3:24
devotionals.title: Justified as a Gift
devotionals.prayer: Father, I receive the Gift! Thank You for "justifying" me by Your Grace through the "redemption" in Christ.

devotionals.reflection: Propitiation means "the sacrifice that satisfies justice." Jesus took the "hit" so we could get the "mercy."
devotionals.question: Do I realize the "price" that was paid for my "peace" today?
devotionals.scripture: ...whom God put forward as a propitiation by his blood, to be received by faith. — Romans 3:25
devotionals.title: Through His Blood
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for Your blood. Thank You for being the sacrifice that "satisfied" the debt I owed.

devotionals.reflection: God didn't "ignore" our sin (that would be "unjust"); He "paid" for our sin (that is "just"). He is perfectly Fair and perfectly Kind.
devotionals.question: How does God's "Justice" and "Mercy" meeting at the Cross give me confidence today?
devotionals.scripture: ...so that he might be just and the justifier of the one who has faith in Jesus. — Romans 3:26
devotionals.title: Both Just and Justifier
devotionals.prayer: Holy God, You are "Just" and "Justifier." I worship the perfect balance of Your character.

devotionals.question: Is there any "boasting" (pride) in my heart today? Will I "exclude" it by looking at the Cross?
devotionals.reflection: There is no room for "ego" in the Kingdom. We are all "beggars telling other beggars where to find bread."
devotionals.scripture: Then what becomes of our boasting? It is excluded. — Romans 3:27
devotionals.title: Boasting Excluded
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I have nothing to boast of except Your Cross. I humble myself and I give You all the praise.

devotionals.reflection: Righteousness isn't a "wage" earned by work; it's a "credit" given for trust. God looks for the heart that takes Him at His word.
devotionals.question: Am I trying to "work off" my debt to God, or am I "believing" it is already paid?
devotionals.scripture: Abraham believed God, and it was counted to him as righteousness. — Romans 4:3
devotionals.title: Abraham Believed
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I believe You. Count my faith as righteousness today, just as You did for Abraham.

devotionals.reflection: The greatest blessing isn't a full bank account; it's a "zeroed-out" sin account. Joy starts where condemnation ends.
devotionals.question: Do I feel "blessed" today because my sins are "not counted," or am I still carrying the weight?
devotionals.scripture: Blessed is the man against whom the Lord will not count his sin. — Romans 4:8
devotionals.title: David’s Blessing
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the blessing of forgiveness. I rejoice that my sins are covered by Grace.

devotionals.reflection: Rituals follow faith; they don't create it. God’s relationship with you is based on your heart long before any "outward" religious ceremony.
devotionals.question: Am I putting too much stock in my "religious activities" and not enough in my "heart connection" to God?
devotionals.scripture: He received the sign... as a seal of the righteousness that he had by faith while he was still uncircumcised. — Romans 4:11
devotionals.title: The Seal of Faith
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, keep my heart primary. Let my outward actions be a "seal" of an inward reality.

devotionals.question: Am I trying to "be good enough" to get God's promise, or am I "trusting enough" to receive it?
devotionals.reflection: You don't "inherit" God's promises by being "perfect"; you inherit them by being "his." The promise is a family gift.
devotionals.scripture: For the promise... did not come through the law but through the righteousness of faith. — Romans 4:13
devotionals.title: Not Through the Law
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for the promise. I receive my inheritance today through faith, not my own effort.

devotionals.question: What "impossible" situation am I facing where I need to "believe against hope" today?
devotionals.reflection: When the "natural" facts say "No," faith says "God said Yes." True hope thrives when the odds are zero.
devotionals.scripture: In hope he believed against hope, that he should become the father of many nations... — Romans 4:18
devotionals.title: Hope Against Hope
devotionals.prayer: Father, my hope is in Your Word, not my circumstances. I believe what You have promised.

devotionals.question: Am I "considering" my problems more than I am "considering" my Promise-Keeper?
