devotionals.reflection: Faith doesn't "ignore" the facts; it just "subordinates" them to a Higher Truth. Abraham knew he was "dead," but God was "Life."
devotionals.scripture: He did not weaken in faith when he considered his own body... which was as good as dead. — Romans 4:19
devotionals.title: Not Weakening in Faith
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I see the "dead" areas of my life, but I see You more. Strengthen my faith today.

devotionals.reflection: Being "fully convinced" is the end of the argument. If God said it, He is able to perform it.
devotionals.question: Am I "half-convinced" or "fully convinced" of God's ability to help me today?
devotionals.scripture: ...fully convinced that God was able to do what he had promised. — Romans 4:21
devotionals.title: Fully Convinced
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I am fully convinced. You are Able. I rest in Your power to do exactly what You said.

devotionals.reflection: The Cross paid the "Fine"; the Resurrection proved the "Check cleared." You are legally free because He is alive.
devotionals.question: How does the "Live Status" of Jesus change my confidence in my "Legal Status" before God?
devotionals.scripture: [Jesus] was delivered up for our trespasses and raised for our justification. — Romans 4:25
devotionals.title: Raised for Justification
devotionals.prayer: Savior, thank You for the Resurrection. I walk in total justification because You are alive.

devotionals.reflection: The war is over. You don't have to "make peace" with God; you already "have peace" because of Jesus.
devotionals.question: Am I living like a "soldier at war" with God, or like a "child at peace" with a Father?
devotionals.scripture: Therefore, since we have been justified by faith, we have peace with God... — Romans 5:1
devotionals.title: Peace with God
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the peace. I lay down my weapons of guilt and I rest in Your love.

devotionals.question: How am I using my "access" today? Am I talking to God, or just thinking about Him?
devotionals.reflection: You aren't just "saved"; you are "welcomed." You have a "Backstage Pass" to the Throne of Grace 24/7.
devotionals.scripture: Through him we have also obtained access by faith into this grace in which we stand... — Romans 5:2
devotionals.title: Access by Faith
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for the "Access." I come boldly to Your throne today with my needs and my praise.

devotionals.question: What "suffering" (difficulty) am I in right now that is "producing" something good in me?
devotionals.reflection: We don't rejoice for the pain, but in the process. Suffering is the "gym" where endurance, character, and hope are built.
devotionals.scripture: ...but we rejoice in our sufferings, knowing that suffering produces endurance... — Romans 5:3
devotionals.title: Rejoicing in Sufferings
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, use this trial to build endurance in me. I rejoice in what You are building, even if I don't like the "gym."

devotionals.reflection: This isn't just a "fact"; it's a "feeling." The Spirit "pours" God's affection into our internal experience.
devotionals.question: Have I asked the Spirit to "pour" a fresh sense of God's love into my heart today?
devotionals.scripture: ...God's love has been poured into our hearts through the Holy Spirit... — Romans 5:5
devotionals.title: Love Poured Out
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, pour it out! Let me feel the depth and the warmth of the Father's love for me right now.

devotionals.reflection: Jesus didn't wait for you to "get strong" or "get religious." He died for you when you were at your worst.
devotionals.question: Does my "weakness" today make me run from God or run to Him?
devotionals.scripture: For while we were still weak, at the right time Christ died for the ungodly. — Romans 5:6
devotionals.title: While We Were Weak
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for dying for me when I was "ungodly." I come to You in my weakness today.

devotionals.reflection: Love is a "verb." God didn't just "say" He loved us; He "proved" it on the Cross.
devotionals.question: If I doubt God's love today, will I look at my "feelings" or will I look at the "Cross"?
devotionals.scripture: but God shows his love for us in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us. — Romans 5:8
devotionals.title: God Shows His Love
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the demonstration. The Cross is all the proof I ever need that You love me.

devotionals.question: Am I worrying about "judgment" when I have already been "justified" by His blood?
devotionals.reflection: If Jesus did the "hard work" (dying for enemies), He will certainly do the "easy work" (keeping His friends).
devotionals.scripture: ...how much more shall we be saved by him from the wrath of God. — Romans 5:9
devotionals.title: Saved from Wrath
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I am safe in You. Thank You for saving me from the wrath I deserved.

devotionals.question: Am I living as a "friend" of God today, or am I still keeping my distance?
devotionals.reflection: You went from "Enemy" to "Friend" to "Family." Reconciliation is the "Happy Ending" of the Gospel.
devotionals.scripture: For if while we were enemies we were reconciled to God... much more... shall we be saved by his life. — Romans 5:10
devotionals.title: Reconciled to God
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I rejoice in our reconciliation. Thank You for making me Your friend and Your brother/sister.

devotionals.question: Do I see the "power of one" decision? Am I choosing the "Adam way" or the "Jesus way" today?
devotionals.reflection: We are all "connected." Adam's one "No" broke the world; but Jesus' one "Yes" fixed it.
devotionals.scripture: Therefore, just as sin came into the world through one man, and death through sin... — Romans 5:12
devotionals.title: One Man’s Sin
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I turn away from the "Adam" in me and I lean into the "Jesus" in me.

devotionals.question: Am I letting the "trespass" (the bad news) loom larger in my mind than the "Free Gift" (the Good News)?
devotionals.reflection: Grace is "unfair" in the best way possible. The gift of Life is far more powerful than the curse of Death.
devotionals.scripture: But the free gift is not like the trespass. — Romans 5:15
devotionals.title: The Free Gift
devotionals.prayer: Father, the Gift is better! I celebrate the "Much More" of Your grace over my sin.

devotionals.question: Am I "reigning in life" today, or is "death" (fear, habit, sin) still reigning over me?
devotionals.reflection: You were meant to "reign," not just "survive." Grace puts you back on the "throne" of your own life and choices.
devotionals.scripture: ...death reigned through that one man, much more will those who receive... reign in life... — Romans 5:17
devotionals.title: Death Reigned vs. Grace Reigns
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I receive the "abundance of grace." I choose to reign with You over my habits and my fears today.

devotionals.question: Am I trying to "get an A+" today, or am I resting in the "Perfect Score" Jesus already got for me?
devotionals.reflection: Jesus' life was the "Perfect Score" that was put on your "Report Card." You get the credit for His "A+."
devotionals.scripture: ...so one act of righteousness leads to justification and life for all men. — Romans 5:18
devotionals.title: One Act of Righteousness
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for Your "One Act." I stand in Your righteousness, not my own.

devotionals.question: Am I afraid that I’ve "exhausted" God's grace? (Hint: It’s impossible.)
devotionals.reflection: You can't out-sin God's grace. No matter how "deep" the hole you dig, His grace is "deeper" still.
devotionals.scripture: ...but where sin increased, grace abounded all the more. — Romans 5:20
devotionals.title: Grace Abounded More
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for "Abounding Grace." Where I failed, Your grace was even bigger.

devotionals.question: If a "dead man" can't sin, why am I still "listening" to my old sinful desires today?
devotionals.reflection: You aren't a "sinner trying to be holy"; you are a "saint who is dead to sin." Your relationship to sin has been "legally terminated."
devotionals.scripture: How can we who died to sin still live in it? — Romans 6:2
devotionals.title: Dead to Sin
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I am dead to sin. I refuse to respond to its "voice" because I don't belong to it anymore.

devotionals.question: Am I still "carrying around" the guilt of the "Old Me" that has already been buried?
devotionals.reflection: The "Old You" is gone. Baptism is the "Funeral" for your past. You don't have to carry a "corpse" around.
devotionals.scripture: We were buried therefore with him by baptism into death... — Romans 6:4
devotionals.title: Buried with Him
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I leave the "Old Me" in the grave. I walk away from my past and into Your future.

devotionals.reflection: You have a "New Battery." The Resurrection life of Jesus is the "Power Source" for your daily walk.
devotionals.question: How "new" does my life feel today? Am I "walking" or am I "limping"?
devotionals.scripture: ...so we too might walk in newness of life. — Romans 6:4
devotionals.title: Walk in Newness
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I walk in "Newness." Give me the energy and the joy of Your resurrection life today.

devotionals.question: Am I staying "united" to the Vine today, or am I trying to "produce" fruit on my own?
devotionals.reflection: You are "Grafted" into Jesus. His "Sap" (Life) flows into your "Branches." You can't help but produce His fruit.
devotionals.scripture: For if we have been united with him in a death like his, we shall certainly be united with him in a resurrection... — Romans 6:5
devotionals.title: United with Him
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I stay in You. Let Your life flow through me so that Your character becomes my own.

devotionals.question: Am I "plugging in" to my old habits today, or am I keeping them "brought to nothing"?
devotionals.reflection: The "Body of Sin" has been "Unplugged." It has no power unless you give it permission.
devotionals.scripture: We know that our old self was crucified with him in order that the body of sin might be brought to nothing... — Romans 6:6
devotionals.title: Our Old Self was Crucified
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the Cross. My "Old Self" is crucified. I am no longer a slave to sin.

devotionals.question: What does it look like for me to be "Alive to God" in my first meeting/task today?
devotionals.reflection: Consider is a math term. It means "Count it as a fact." Count yourself as "Alive to God" and act accordingly.
devotionals.scripture: So you also must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus. — Romans 6:11
devotionals.title: Alive to God
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I consider myself "Alive to God." I am awake to Your presence and ready for Your lead.

devotionals.reflection: Sin is a "dethroned king." It has no "right" to rule you. You have the power to say "No" to the passions of your body.
devotionals.question: Who is "reigning" on the throne of my heart this morning—my "Passions" or my "Prince"?
devotionals.scripture: Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body, to make you obey its passions. — Romans 6:12
devotionals.title: Let Not Sin Reign
devotionals.prayer: Lord, You are King. I refuse to let "Sin" take the throne today. I obey You.

devotionals.question: How will I use my "instruments" (hands/mouth/mind) to "do righteousness" today?
devotionals.reflection: Your eyes, hands, and feet are "Tools." You can use them to "build" God's Kingdom or to "break" it.
devotionals.scripture: Present your members to God as instruments for righteousness. — Romans 6:13
devotionals.title: Instruments for Righteousness
devotionals.prayer: Father, I present my body to You. Use my hands to help, my mouth to encourage, and my feet to follow You.

devotionals.reflection: Law says "Do this or else." "Grace" says "I’ve done this, now follow Me." Grace is the "Atmosphere" that kills sin's power.
devotionals.question: Am I living in the "Fear of the Law" or the "Freedom of Grace" today?
devotionals.scripture: For sin will have no dominion over you, since you are not under law but under grace. — Romans 6:14
devotionals.title: Under Grace, Not Law
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You that Sin has no "dominion." I am safe in the atmosphere of Your Grace.

devotionals.reflection: Everyone is a "slave" to something. You either serve your "Urges" or you serve your "Creator." Choose your Master.
devotionals.question: Who/What am I "obeying" today? Am I serving a "dead-end" master or the "Living Lord"?
devotionals.scripture: ...you are slaves of the one whom you obey, either of sin... or of obedience... — Romans 6:16
devotionals.title: Slaves to Obedience
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I choose to be Your "Slave." Your service is the only true freedom I have ever found.

devotionals.question: Is my obedience today "from the heart," or am I just "doing my duty"?
devotionals.reflection: God doesn't want "grudging" obedience; He wants "Heart-Obedience." He wants you to want what He wants.
devotionals.scripture: But thanks be to God... you have become obedient from the heart... — Romans 6:17
devotionals.title: Obedient from the Heart
devotionals.prayer: Father, change my "wants." Give me a heart that loves to do Your will.

devotionals.reflection: You have been "Emancipated." The shackles are off. Now, use your freedom to do what is Right.
devotionals.question: What "freedom" will I exercise today? Will I choose the "Right" path just because I can?
devotionals.scripture: And having been set free from sin, you became slaves of righteousness. — Romans 6:18
devotionals.title: Free from Sin
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for the freedom. I use my liberty to serve You and love others.

devotionals.reflection: Sanctification is the process of becoming like Jesus. It’s a "step-by-step" presenting of our lives to God.
devotionals.question: What is the "next step" in my sanctification today? What small area can I "present" to Him?
devotionals.scripture: ...so now present your members as slaves to righteousness leading to sanctification. — Romans 6:19
devotionals.title: To Sanctification
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, make me more like Jesus. I give You this specific area of my life to "set apart" for Your use.

devotionals.reflection: Sin’s fruit is "Rot"; God’s fruit is "Life." Every "Godly" choice you make today adds to the "Life" in your soul.
devotionals.question: What "fruit" am I growing today? Is it "Rot" or is it "Life"?
devotionals.scripture: ...but now... the fruit you get leads to sanctification and its end, eternal life. — Romans 6:22
devotionals.title: The End is Life
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I want the fruit of Life. Help me to make choices that lead to "more life" in me and around me.

devotionals.reflection: A "Wage" is what you earn. A "Gift" is what you receive. Don't work for the "Death" wage; receive the "Life" gift.
devotionals.question: Am I trying to "earn" God's favor? (The pay is terrible.) Will I just "receive" the Gift today?
devotionals.scripture: For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus... — Romans 6:23
devotionals.title: Wages vs. Gift
devotionals.prayer: Father, I receive the Gift. I thank You that Eternal Life is found in "Christ Jesus our Lord" and nowhere else.

devotionals.question: Am I trying to "please the Law" (the dead husband), or am I "loving Jesus" (the Living Groom)?
devotionals.reflection: The "Law" was a "Marriage" to a husband we couldn't please. Now, we are "Married" to Jesus, who loves us as we are.
devotionals.scripture: But now we are released from the law, having died to that which held us captive... — Romans 6:7:6
devotionals.title: Released from the Law
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I am Yours. Thank You for releasing me from the pressure of the Law and bringing me into the romance of Grace.

devotionals.reflection: The "Old Way" is "Rules"; the "New Way" is "Relationship." The Spirit gives you the "Want-to" that the "Code" couldn't.
devotionals.question: Am I "serving by the Spirit" today (with joy), or by the "Code" (with a checklist)?
devotionals.scripture: ...so that we serve in the new way of the Spirit and not in the old way of the written code. — Romans 7:6
devotionals.title: Serving in the New Way
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, lead my service. Let my "doing" flow from my "being" in You.

devotionals.question: Am I "blaming the Law" (the X-ray) for my "sin" (the break)? Will I look for the "Doctor"?
devotionals.reflection: The Law isn't the "problem"; our "sin" is. The Law is the "X-ray" that shows the "broken bone" of our soul.
devotionals.scripture: So the law is holy, and the commandment is holy and righteous and good. — Romans 7:12
devotionals.title: The Law is Holy
devotionals.prayer: Lord, Your Law is good. It shows me how much I need You. I come to You for healing today.

devotionals.reflection: This is the "Honest Apostle." Even the best Christians struggle with the "Old Self" trying to take over. You are not alone in the struggle.
devotionals.question: What "good I want to do" am I struggling with today? Will I admit my "powerlessness" to God?
devotionals.scripture: For I do not do the good I want, but the evil I do not want is what I keep on doing. — Romans 7:19
devotionals.title: The Inner Conflict
devotionals.prayer: Father, I’m struggling. I want to do right, but I feel the "pull" of the old way. Help me!

devotionals.question: Am I "surprised" by the spiritual battle today? Will I put on the armor?
devotionals.reflection: There is a "War" inside you. Don't be surprised by the "attacks" of your old nature. It’s a sign that the "New You" is alive.
devotionals.scripture: But I see in my members another law waging war against the law of my mind... — Romans 7:23
devotionals.title: Another Law in My Members
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I am in a war. Give me Your strength to fight the "law of sin" that dwells in my members.

devotionals.reflection: This is the "Bottom of the Pit." When you realize you can't "save yourself," you are finally ready for a "Deliverer."
devotionals.question: Have I reached the "Wretched Man" moment where I stop trying and start crying out for help?
devotionals.scripture: Wretched man that I am! Who will deliver me from this body of death? — Romans 7:24
devotionals.title: Wretched Man!
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I am wretched without You. Deliver me! I can't do this on my own.

devotionals.reflection: The answer to "Who will deliver me?" is "Jesus!" The struggle ends in "Thanks," not "Trying Harder."
devotionals.question: Am I saying "Thanks" today for the "Rescue" Jesus has already provided?
devotionals.scripture: Thanks be to God through Jesus Christ our Lord! — Romans 7:25
devotionals.title: Thanks Be to God
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You! You are my Deliverer. I rest in Your victory over my "body of death."

devotionals.reflection: This is the "Great Declaration." The Judge has banged the gavel. You are "Not Guilty"—not because you are innocent, but because you are "In Christ."
devotionals.question: Do I "feel" condemned today? Will I listen to my "Feelings" or to the "Verdict" of Romans 8:1?
devotionals.scripture: There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus. — Romans 8:1
devotionals.title: No Condemnation
devotionals.prayer: Father, I hear Your verdict: "No Condemnation." I walk in the freedom of Your "Not Guilty" declaration.

devotionals.reflection: The "Law of Aerodynamics" (the Spirit) overcomes the "Law of Gravity" (Sin). You can "fly" because a Greater Power is at work.
devotionals.question: Am I trying to "flap my wings" (self-effort), or am I "trusting the wind" of the Spirit to lift me?
devotionals.scripture: For the law of the Spirit of life... has set you free from the law of sin and death. — Romans 8:2
devotionals.title: Set Free by the Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, lift me up. Set me free from the "gravity" of my old habits today.

devotionals.reflection: Walking is about "Direction," not "Perfection." Keep your face turned toward the Spirit, and your feet will follow.
devotionals.question: Which "direction" am I "walking" in this morning? Toward the "Flesh" (self) or the "Spirit" (God)?
devotionals.scripture: ...who walk not according to the flesh but according to the spirit. — Romans 8:4
devotionals.title: Walking According to the Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I choose to walk with Your Spirit. Guide my steps and keep my heart aligned with Yours.

devotionals.question: What am I "setting my mind on" today? (Problems? Politics? Or the Promises of the Spirit?)
devotionals.reflection: Your "Mind" is the "Steering Wheel" of your life. What you "set your mind on" is where your life will go.
devotionals.scripture: For those who live according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh... — Romans 8:5
devotionals.title: Setting the Mind
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, help me to "set my mind" on things that bring Life and Peace.

devotionals.reflection: You can "test" your focus by your "feelings." If you feel "death" (anxiety/guilt), check your mind. if you feel "Life/Peace," you are on track.
devotionals.question: Do I have "Life and Peace" in my soul right now? If not, what do I need to "re-set" my mind on?
devotionals.scripture: For to set the mind on the flesh is death, but to set the mind on the spirit is life and peace. — Romans 8:6
devotionals.title: Life and Peace
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I want Your Life and Your Peace. I re-set my mind on You and Your Word.

devotionals.question: Is there any "hostility" (independence) in my heart today? Will I surrender it to the Father?
devotionals.reflection: Self-Focus is actually "God-Hostility." We can't please God while we are trying to be our own "gods."
devotionals.scripture: For the mind that is set on the flesh is hostile to God... — Romans 8:7
devotionals.title: Hostile to God
devotionals.prayer: Father, I am not Your enemy. I surrender my "Independent Mind" and I submit to Your Love.

devotionals.reflection: You are a "Temple." The Creator of the Universe has moved into your "Living Room." You are never alone.
devotionals.question: Do I live with the "Awe" that the Spirit of God is "dwelling" inside of me right now?
devotionals.scripture: You, however, are not in the flesh but in the spirit, if in fact the Spirit of God dwells in you. — Romans 8:9
devotionals.title: Spirit Dwelling in You
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, thank You for Your indwelling. Make Yourself "at home" in my heart today.

devotionals.question: Am I living with a "Slave-Mentality" (fear) or an "Adopted-Mentality" (love) today?
devotionals.reflection: You aren't a "Servant" in the house; you are a "Son/Daughter." You have the "Keys" to the Father's heart.
devotionals.scripture: For you did not receive the spirit of slavery... but you have received the Spirit of adoption... — Romans 8:15
devotionals.title: Adopted as Sons
devotionals.prayer: Abba, Father! I cry out to You as Your child. Thank You for adopting me and making me Your own.

devotionals.reflection: Abba is the Aramaic word for "Daddy/Papa." It’s the highest form of intimacy. God is your "Deity," but He is also your "Dad."
devotionals.question: Have I talked to my "Abba" today with the simplicity and trust of a little child?
devotionals.scripture: ...by whom we cry, 'Abba! Father!' — Romans 8:15
devotionals.title: Abba! Father!
devotionals.prayer: Abba, I love You. I trust You with my needs, my fears, and my day. Hold me close.

devotionals.reflection: Everything that belongs to Jesus now belongs to you. You are "Spiritually Wealthy" beyond your wildest dreams.
devotionals.question: Am I living like a "Beggarly Orphan" or like a "Fellow Heir" to the Kingdom of God?
devotionals.scripture: ...and if children, then heirs—heirs of God and fellow heirs with Christ... — Romans 8:17
devotionals.title: Fellow Heirs with Christ
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for sharing Your inheritance with me. I walk in Your abundance today.

devotionals.reflection: Put your "Trial" on one side of the scale and "Glory" on the other. The Glory side crashes the scale to the floor.
devotionals.question: How does the "future glory" make my "present suffering" look "light and temporary" today?
devotionals.scripture: For I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worth comparing with the glory... — Romans 8:18
devotionals.title: Not Worth Comparing
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me the "Long Perspective." Help me to see my trials in the light of Your eternal Glory.

devotionals.reflection: The "brokenness" of the world (storms, sickness, decay) is not a death rattle; it’s a birth pang. Something new is coming.
devotionals.question: Do I hear the "groaning" of the world as a sign of despair or a sign of "labor pains" for the New Heaven?
devotionals.scripture: For we know that the whole creation has been groaning together... — Romans 8:22
devotionals.title: Groaning in Hope
devotionals.prayer: Lord, help me to groan with hope. I wait for the day You make all things new and right.

devotionals.question: How does the "sample" of peace I have today make me hungry for the "full meal" of eternity?
devotionals.reflection: You have a "down payment" of Heaven inside you right now. The Spirit’s presence is a "sample" of the joy to come.
devotionals.scripture: ...we ourselves, who have the firstfruits of the Spirit, groan inwardly... — Romans 8:23
devotionals.title: Firstfruits of the Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, thank You for being the "Firstfruits." Give me a deeper hunger for the fullness of God.

devotionals.question: Am I being patient with my "aching body," knowing that a "resurrection body" is on the way?
devotionals.reflection: Your soul is saved, but your "suit" (your body) is still waiting for the upgrade. Full adoption includes a physical rescue.
devotionals.scripture: ...waiting eagerly for adoption as sons, the redemption of our bodies. — Romans 8:23
devotionals.title: Waiting for Adoption
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You that my redemption is total—body, soul, and spirit. I wait for the final "reveal."

devotionals.question: What "unseen" promise am I holding onto today with the "hands" of hope?
devotionals.reflection: Hope is specifically for the things we can't see yet. If you could see it, you wouldn't need to hope for it.
devotionals.scripture: Now hope that is seen is not hope. For who hopes for what he sees? — Romans 8:24
devotionals.title: Hope for the Unseen
devotionals.prayer: Lord, strengthen my hope. I don't need to "see" the answer to know that You are faithful.

devotionals.reflection: Patience is the "fruit" of hope. When you are sure of the outcome, you can handle the "wait."
devotionals.question: Is my "waiting" characterized by "anxiety" or by "patience" today?
devotionals.scripture: But if we hope for what we do not see, we wait for it with patience. — Romans 8:25
devotionals.title: Waiting with Patience
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, give me Your patience. I trust the "Not Yet" because I trust the "I Am."

devotionals.question: Where do I feel "weakest" today? Will I invite the Spirit to "help" me in that specific spot?
devotionals.reflection: You don't have to be "strong" to be spiritual. The Spirit’s specific job description is to "help" the weak.
devotionals.scripture: Likewise the Spirit helps us in our weakness. — Romans 8:26
devotionals.title: The Spirit’s Help
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, I admit my weakness. Come and be my Strength in the areas where I am failing.

devotionals.reflection: When you are so overwhelmed you can't even "pray," your "sighs" are heard by God as perfect intercession.
devotionals.question: Am I "silent" because I’m far from God, or because I’m letting the Spirit "groan" for me?
devotionals.scripture: ...but the Spirit himself intercedes for us with groanings too deep for words. — Romans 8:26
devotionals.title: Groans Too Deep
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for hearing my "wordless" prayers. Thank You for the Spirit’s perfect translation.

devotionals.question: Am I comfortable with God "searching my heart" today? Is there anything I’m trying to hide?
devotionals.reflection: God is "searching" your heart right now—not to find "dirt," but to find the "Spirit’s longing."
devotionals.scripture: And he who searches hearts knows what is the mind of the Spirit... — Romans 8:27
devotionals.title: He Who Searches Hearts
devotionals.prayer: Father, search me. Know my thoughts. Let my heart’s desire be aligned with the Spirit’s prayer.

devotionals.reflection: This is the "Safety Net" of the Bible. God takes the "random" threads of our lives and weaves them into a masterpiece.
devotionals.question: Do I really believe that all things—even the mistakes—are being "worked for good" by God?
devotionals.scripture: And we know that for those who love God all things work together for good... — Romans 8:28
devotionals.title: Working for Good
devotionals.prayer: Sovereign God, I trust Your loom. Weave my joys and my sorrows into something beautiful for Your glory.

devotionals.reflection: The "8:28 promise" is for the "lovers" and the "called." Your love for God is the "key" that unlocks the peace.
devotionals.question: Is my "love for God" the primary engine of my life today, or am I just "doing church"?
devotionals.scripture: ...for those who love God and are called according to his purpose. — Romans 8:28
devotionals.title: Those Who Love Him
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I love You. I am called by You. I rest in the purpose You have designed for my life.

devotionals.reflection: You aren't an "afterthought." God "knew" you before time began and "planned" your destination.
devotionals.question: Does it change my "identity" to know that I was "foreknown" by the Creator of the Universe?
devotionals.scripture: For those whom he foreknew he also predestined... — Romans 8:29
devotionals.title: Foreknown and Predestined
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for knowing me. Thank You for choosing me before I ever chose You.

devotionals.reflection: The goal of your life isn't "happiness"; it’s "Jesus-likeness." Every trial is a "sculptor's chisel" for this one goal.
devotionals.question: How is my current "frustration" or "trial" helping me to look a little more like Jesus today?
devotionals.scripture: ...to be conformed to the image of his Son... — Romans 8:29
devotionals.title: Conformed to the Son
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me like Jesus. Whatever it takes, conform my character to His Image.

devotionals.reflection: Jesus is your "Big Brother." He cleared the way so we could follow Him into the Father's house.
devotionals.question: Do I see Jesus as a "distant King" or as my "Firstborn Brother" who loves me?
devotionals.scripture: ...in order that he might be the firstborn among many brothers. — Romans 8:29
devotionals.title: The Firstborn
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for being my Brother. Thank You for leading the family into the Kingdom.

devotionals.reflection: Justified means "Legally Right." God’s "Calling" is the "Summons" that leads to your "Exoneration."
devotionals.question: Do I live today as someone who has been "Justified" (Declared Not Guilty) by the Highest Court?
devotionals.scripture: And those whom he predestined he also called, and those whom he called he also justified... — Romans 8:30
devotionals.title: Called and Justified
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the "Call." Thank You for "Justifying" me by Your Grace.

devotionals.question: Am I "worrying" about a future that God has already "finalized" in His eternal plan?
devotionals.reflection: In God's mind, your "Glorification" is so certain that He speaks of it in the past tense. It’s already "done."
devotionals.scripture: ...and those whom he justified he also glorified. — Romans 8:30
devotionals.title: Justified and Glorified
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You that the "end" is certain. I walk today in the light of my future "Glory."

devotionals.question: Who/What am I allowing to be "against me" in my mind that is smaller than "God for me"?
devotionals.reflection: This is the "Mic-Drop" of the Bible. If the Creator is "For" you, every critic is "irrevelant."
devotionals.scripture: What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us? — Romans 8:31
devotionals.title: If God is For Us
devotionals.prayer: Sovereign God, You are FOR me! I reject fear and I embrace Your overwhelming support.

devotionals.reflection: If God already gave the "Best" (His Son), why would He withhold the "Rest" (peace, provision, help)?
devotionals.question: Is there any "good thing" I think God is "withholding" from me? (He already gave me Jesus!)
devotionals.scripture: He who did not spare his own Son but gave him up for us all... — Romans 8:32
devotionals.title: He Did Not Spare His Son
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the ultimate Gift. I trust You to "graciously give me all things" I truly need.

devotionals.reflection: The "Accuser" (Satan) is a "Prosecutor" with no standing in court. The Judge is on your side.
devotionals.question: Am I listening to "accusations" in my head that the Judge has already dismissed?
devotionals.scripture: Who shall bring any charge against God's elect? It is God who justifies. — Romans 8:33
devotionals.title: Who Shall Bring a Charge?
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for my defense. I refuse to listen to "charges" that You have already cleared.

devotionals.question: Am I "condemning myself" for things that Jesus has already "died for"?
devotionals.reflection: The only Person with the "right" to condemn you is the Person who died to "save" you. He isn't condemning; He’s praying.
devotionals.scripture: Who is to condemn? Christ Jesus is the one who died... who is at the right hand of God... — Romans 8:34
devotionals.title: Who is to Condemn?
devotionals.prayer: Savior, I accept Your pardon. I stop the "self-condemnation" and I look to Your intercession.

devotionals.reflection: Right now, Jesus is "speaking your name" to the Father. You have a 24/7 "Advocate" in the Presence of God.
devotionals.question: How does it change my "boldness" today to know that Jesus is "praying for me" right now?
devotionals.scripture: ...who indeed is interceding for us. — Romans 8:34
devotionals.title: Interceding for Us
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for interceding for me. I go through this day with confidence because You are speaking for me.

devotionals.question: What "circumstance" am I afraid will "separate" me from God's love? (Hint: It can't.)
devotionals.reflection: God’s love isn't a "feeling" that comes and goes; it’s a "bond" that nothing in creation can break.
devotionals.scripture: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution... — Romans 8:35
devotionals.title: Who Shall Separate Us?
devotionals.prayer: Father, I am glued to Your love. Thank You that no distress can ever pull me away from You.

devotionals.question: Am I mistaking my "hard day" for a "lack of God's love"?
devotionals.reflection: Being "Loved by God" doesn't mean life is "Easy." It means life is "Victorious" in the middle of the pain.
devotionals.scripture: As it is written, 'For your sake we are being killed all the day long...' — Romans 8:36
devotionals.title: As It Is Written
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I see the reality of the struggle. But I also see the reality of Your Presence in the struggle.

devotionals.reflection: A "Conqueror" wins the battle. A "More Than Conqueror" takes the enemy’s weapons and uses them for good.
devotionals.question: How can I be "More than a Conqueror" in my current struggle today?
devotionals.scripture: No, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him who loved us. — Romans 8:37
devotionals.title: More Than Conquerors
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for the victory. I don't just "survive" this trial; I "triumph" through Your love.

devotionals.reflection: This is the "Ultimate List." Death can't do it, Life can't do it, Angels and Demons can't do it. You are safe.
devotionals.question: Am I "sure" (convinced) of this today, or am I still looking for "loopholes" in God's love?
devotionals.scripture: For I am sure that neither death nor life... will be able to separate us from the love of God... — Romans 8:38-39
devotionals.title: Neither Death Nor Life
devotionals.prayer: Father, I am convinced! I stand on the "unsinkable" truth of Your eternal love in Christ Jesus.

devotionals.reflection: Paul goes from the "High" of Romans 8 to the "Deep Concern" of Romans 9. Great love for God leads to great sorrow for the lost.
devotionals.question: Who is the "one person" I have "unceasing anguish" for? Am I praying for them today?
devotionals.scripture: I have great sorrow and unceasing anguish in my heart. — Romans 9:2
devotionals.title: Great Sorrow
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me Your heart for the lost. Let my joy in You be balanced by a burden for those who don't know You yet.

devotionals.question: How far am I willing to go (comfort-wise or ego-wise) for the sake of those who are lost?
devotionals.reflection: This is the "Moses-Heart." Paul would trade his own salvation for his people's. That is the true height of spiritual maturity.
devotionals.scripture: For I could wish that I myself were accursed... for the sake of my brothers... — Romans 9:3
devotionals.title: Accursed for Others
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You were accursed for me. Give me a heart that is willing to "lose" so that others can "find."

devotionals.reflection: Heritage is a "gift," but it isn't a "guarantee." Having the "tools" of religion doesn't mean you have the "Truth" of the Spirit.
devotionals.question: Am I relying on my "Christian heritage" (parents, upbringing) or on my "Personal Faith" today?
devotionals.scripture: They are Israelites, and to them belong the adoption, the glory, the covenants... — Romans 9:4
devotionals.title: The Adoption and the Glory
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for my heritage. But I ask for a "Fresh Faith" of my own right now.

devotionals.question: Do I "belong" to the family because I am "doing the ritual" or because I have "the Promise" in my heart?
devotionals.reflection: God’s family is "By Promise," not "By Blood." It’s an internal "Yes" to God, not an external "Birth Certificate."
devotionals.scripture: For not all who are descended from Israel belong to Israel... — Romans 9:6
devotionals.title: Not All are Israel
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me a child of the Promise. I rest in Your Word, not my works.

devotionals.reflection: God’s choice is based on His purpose, not Our performance. This is the ultimate "Ego-Crusher."
devotionals.question: Does God's "sovereign choice" make me "nervous" (pride) or "secure" (grace)?
devotionals.scripture: ...in order that God's purpose of election might continue, not because of works but because of him who calls. — Romans 9:11
devotionals.title: The Purpose of Election
devotionals.prayer: Sovereign Lord, I trust Your choice. Thank You that my salvation depends on Your "Call," not my "Works."

devotionals.reflection: Our idea of "Fairness" is limited. God’s "Justice" is perfect. He owes no one mercy, yet He gives it freely to many.
devotionals.question: Am I trying to "judge the Judge"? Can I trust that His "Fairness" is higher than mine?
devotionals.scripture: What shall we say then? Is there injustice on God's part? By no means! — Romans 9:14
devotionals.title: Is There Injustice?
devotionals.prayer: Holy God, I bow to Your Justice. You are perfect in all Your ways.

devotionals.question: How can I "celebrate" the mercy I’ve been given today, rather than "complaining" about how God handles others?
devotionals.reflection: Mercy is by definition "undeserved." If it were "fair," it wouldn't be "mercy."
devotionals.scripture: I will have mercy on whom I have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I have compassion. — Romans 9:15
devotionals.title: Mercy on Whom I Will
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for Your mercy. I don't deserve it, and that’s what makes it so beautiful.

devotionals.question: Am I "exerting" myself today to be "enough," or am I "resting" in God's mercy?
devotionals.reflection: You can't "Will" your way to Heaven and you can't "Work" your way to Heaven. You "Mercy" your way to Heaven.
devotionals.scripture: So then it depends not on human will or exertion, but on God, who has mercy. — Romans 9:16
devotionals.title: Not on Human Will
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I stop the "exertion." I depend entirely on Your mercy today.

devotionals.question: Am I trusting that God is using the "Pharaohs" (difficult bosses/leaders) in my life for His purposes?
devotionals.reflection: God can use even a "hard heart" to show His power. No one is "outside" of God's utility.
devotionals.scripture: For the Scripture says to Pharaoh, 'For this very purpose I have raised you up...' — Romans 9:17
devotionals.title: For This Very Purpose
devotionals.prayer: Sovereign Lord, show Your power through my situation. I trust that even the "opposition" is in Your hands.

devotionals.reflection: The "Clay" doesn't get to cross-examine the "Potter." Humility is knowing our place in the Universe.
devotionals.question: Am I "answering back" to God about my "shape" or my "life circumstances" today?
devotionals.scripture: But who are you, O man, to answer back to God? — Romans 9:20
devotionals.title: Who Are You, O Man?
devotionals.prayer: Potter, I am the clay. Mold me as You wish. I trust Your hands and Your design.

devotionals.question: What "glory" can I hold today if I just "empty" myself of my own pride?
devotionals.reflection: You are a "Vessel." Your only job is to be "empty" enough to be filled with the "Riches of His Glory."
devotionals.scripture: ...in order to make known the riches of his glory for vessels of mercy, which he has prepared beforehand... — Romans 9:23
devotionals.title: Vessels of Mercy
devotionals.prayer: Father, fill my vessel. I want to carry Your mercy and Your glory into my world today.

devotionals.question: Who is an "Outsider" in my life that I should start viewing as a "Potential Insider" for God?
devotionals.reflection: God loves "surprises." He specializes in taking "Outsiders" and making them "Insiders."
devotionals.scripture: Those who were not my people I will call 'my people'... — Romans 9:25
devotionals.title: Not My People -> My People
devotionals.prayer: Lord, expand my heart. Help me to see the "Not My People" as people You are calling.

devotionals.reflection: Jesus is either your "Cornerstone" (support) or your "Stumbling Stone" (offense). You can't just "ignore" Him.
devotionals.question: Is Jesus the "Stone" I build my life on today, or am I "tripping" over His claims?
devotionals.scripture: ...they have stumbled over the stumbling stone, as it is written, 'Behold, I am laying in Zion a stone of stumbling...' — Romans 9:32-33
devotionals.title: Stumbling Stone
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, be my Cornerstone. I build my life, my marriage, and my career on the solid rock of You.

devotionals.question: Is my "passion" today directed by the "Truth of the Word," or just by my "Feelings"?
devotionals.reflection: You can be "sincerely wrong." Zeal (passion) is dangerous if it isn't anchored in Truth.
devotionals.scripture: For I bear them witness that they have a zeal for God, but not according to knowledge. — Romans 10:2
devotionals.title: Zeal Without Knowledge
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, guide my zeal. Let my passion for You be founded on the "Knowledge of Your Truth."

devotionals.question: Have I "reached the end" of my self-effort today? Have I found my "rest" in Christ?
devotionals.reflection: Jesus is the "Finish Line." He did what the Law couldn't. When you have Jesus, you can stop "running" the Law-race.
devotionals.scripture: For Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to everyone who believes. — Romans 10:4
devotionals.title: Christ is the End
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are my "End." I stop trying to satisfy the Law and I start enjoying Your righteousness.

devotionals.reflection: You don't have to "climb to heaven" or "go to the abyss." The Truth is as close as your next breath.
devotionals.question: How "near" am I letting the Word be today? Is it "in my mouth" and "in my heart"?
devotionals.scripture: The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart... — Romans 10:8
devotionals.title: The Word is Near
devotionals.prayer: Lord, keep Your Word near me. Let it be the "song" in my heart and the "speech" on my lips.

devotionals.question: Have I "confessed" Jesus as Lord of my schedule and my wallet today, as well as my soul?
devotionals.reflection: This is the "Simplicity of the Gospel." Internal Belief + External Confession = Eternal Life.
devotionals.scripture: ...if you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart... you will be saved. — Romans 10:9
devotionals.title: Confess and Believe
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are Lord. I believe it in my heart and I say it with my mouth. You are my Master.

devotionals.reflection: Faith isn't an "intellectual nod"; it’s a "heart-move." You have to "fall in love" with the Truth.
devotionals.question: Is my "belief" today a "mental fact" or a "heart-affection"?
devotionals.scripture: For with the heart one believes and is justified... — Romans 10:10
devotionals.title: Believing with the Heart
devotionals.prayer: Father, I believe with my heart. Let my faith be a "love story" between me and You.

devotionals.question: Am I "creating distinctions" between people that God has already removed at the Cross?
devotionals.reflection: The "Table of Grace" has no reserved seating. Anyone, anywhere, can pull up a chair.
devotionals.scripture: For there is no distinction between Jew and Greek; for the same Lord is Lord of all... — Romans 10:12
devotionals.title: No Distinction
devotionals.prayer: Lord of All, I worship You. Thank You for Your "No Distinction" policy of grace.

devotionals.reflection: Everyone means Everyone. Your past, your race, and your failures are swallowed up by the Name of Jesus.
devotionals.question: Who can I tell today that "Everyone" includes them?
devotionals.scripture: For 'everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.' — Romans 10:13
devotionals.title: Everyone Who Calls
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for Your Name. I call on You today for help, for hope, and for life.

devotionals.question: Who is "waiting to hear" the Gospel today, and am I willing to be the "someone" who tells them?
devotionals.reflection: God uses "human mouthpieces." He has chosen us to be the "how" of the Gospel.
devotionals.scripture: And how are they to hear without someone preaching? — Romans 10:14
devotionals.title: How Will They Hear?
devotionals.prayer: Lord, use my voice. I am the "how" for someone today. Give me the words to speak.

devotionals.reflection: Your "feet" (your walk, your travel, your presence) are "beautiful" to God when they carry His message.
devotionals.question: Where are my "feet" taking me today? Are they carrying "Good News" or just "Busy News"?
devotionals.scripture: How beautiful are the feet of those who preach the good news! — Romans 10:15
devotionals.title: Beautiful Feet
devotionals.prayer: Father, make my feet "beautiful." Let my presence in every room be a delivery of Your Good News.

devotionals.reflection: If you want "more faith," you need "more Word." Faith is a "reaction" to hearing what God has said.
devotionals.question: What am I "listening to" today? Am I "feeding my faith" or "feeding my fears"?
devotionals.scripture: So faith comes from hearing, and hearing through the word of Christ. — Romans 10:17
devotionals.title: Faith by Hearing
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I want more faith. I open Your Word and I "hear" Your voice today.

devotionals.question: Am I afraid God has "rejected" me because of my recent failures? (By no means!)
devotionals.reflection: God doesn't "break up" with His people. His "Yes" is eternal. If He was faithful to Israel, He will be faithful to you.
devotionals.scripture: I ask, then, has God rejected his people? By no means! — Romans 11:1
devotionals.title: Has God Rejected?
devotionals.prayer: Faithful God, thank You for Your "Un-Rejecting" Love. I trust Your commitment to me.

devotionals.question: Who is part of my "remnant" (my core group of believers) that I can connect with today?
devotionals.reflection: You are never "the only one." God always has a "remnant" of faithful people, even in the darkest times.
devotionals.scripture: So too at the present time there is a remnant, chosen by grace. — Romans 11:5
devotionals.title: A Remnant Chosen
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for the "Remnant." Help me to find my strength in the community of the faithful.

devotionals.reflection: You can't "mix" Grace and Works. It’s either "All Gift" or "All Wage." You can't have it both ways.
devotionals.question: Am I trying to "add" a little of my "work" to God's "Grace" today?
devotionals.scripture: But if it is by grace, it is no longer on the basis of works; otherwise grace would no longer be grace. — Romans 11:6
devotionals.title: Not Based on Works
devotionals.prayer: Father, I take it as a "Pure Gift." I stop the "mixing" and I start the "receiving."

devotionals.reflection: God can use a "Tragedy" (Israel’s rejection) to create a "Triumph" (The World’s salvation). He is the Master of the "Plot Twist."
devotionals.question: Can I trust God’s "Plot Twist" in my current "Tragedy" today?
devotionals.scripture: ...through their trespass salvation has come to the Gentiles... — Romans 11:11
devotionals.title: Their Trespass -> Salvation
devotionals.prayer: Sovereign Lord, You use everything! I trust You to turn my "trespass" into someone else’s "salvation."

devotionals.reflection: You were a "Wild Olive" who has been "Grafted" into the "Rich Root." You are drinking from a history that isn't yours.
devotionals.question: Am I being "humble" today, knowing that I am a "grafted-in" guest at God's table?
devotionals.scripture: But if some of the branches were broken off... you were grafted in among them... — Romans 11:17
devotionals.title: Grafted In
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for grafting me in. I humble myself before the "Root" that supports me.

devotionals.question: Is there any "haughtiness" (spiritual superiority) in me today? Will I "fear" and stay humble?
devotionals.reflection: Spiritual pride is a "branch-breaker." Stay "low" (humble) and you will stay "on the tree."
devotionals.scripture: Do not be haughty, but fear. For if God did not spare the natural branches, neither will he spare you. — Romans 11:20-21
devotionals.title: Do Not Be Haughty
devotionals.prayer: Lord, keep me humble. I am only here by Grace. I don't "deserve" my spot on the tree.

devotionals.reflection: God is not a "one-sided" Grandpa. He is Kind to the trusting and Severe to the rebellious. We must respect both.
devotionals.question: Am I "noting" both the "Kindness" and the "Severity" of God in my life today?
devotionals.scripture: Note then the kindness and the severity of God... — Romans 11:22
devotionals.title: Kindness and Severity
devotionals.prayer: Holy God, I worship Your balance. I tremble at Your severity and I rejoice in Your kindness.

devotionals.question: Who have I "given up on" that God is still "Able to graft in again"?
devotionals.reflection: No one is "too far gone." God is "Able" to bring back anyone who turns toward Him.
devotionals.scripture: And even they... will be grafted in, for God is able to graft them in again. — Romans 11:23
devotionals.title: Able to Graft Again
devotionals.prayer: Father, bring them back! I pray for the "broken branches" in my life to be restored to the Tree.

devotionals.question: Am I "patient" with the "hardness" I see in the world, trusting God's "Fullness" schedule?
devotionals.reflection: God has a "Schedule." The "Hardness" of some is creating "Space" for others. Everything is moving toward a "Fullness."
devotionals.scripture: ...a partial hardening has come upon Israel, until the fullness of the Gentiles has come in. — Romans 11:25
devotionals.title: The Partial Hardening
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I trust Your timing. I pray for the "Fullness" to come quickly!

devotionals.reflection: God doesn't "take back" what He gives. His "Call" on your life is a permanent signature.
devotionals.question: How does the "Irrevocable" nature of God's call give me security today?
devotionals.scripture: For the gifts and the calling of God are irrevocable. — Romans 11:29
devotionals.title: The Gifts and the Call
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You that You don't change Your mind about me. Your gifts and Your call are my anchor.

devotionals.reflection: We were all "trapped" in sin so that we could all be "rescued" by Mercy. The "Prison" was the prerequisite for the "Pardon."
devotionals.question: Do I realize that my "failures" were the very thing that qualified me for God's "Mercy"?
devotionals.scripture: For God has consigned all to disobedience, that he may have mercy on all. — Romans 11:32
devotionals.title: Consigned to Disobedience
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for the rescue. I celebrate the "Mercy on All" that You have provided.

devotionals.question: When was the last time I just stood in "Wonder" at the "Depth of God"?
devotionals.reflection: We finally reach the "End of the Argument." Paul just stops and "Worships." Some things are too deep for "logic"—they require "Awe."
devotionals.scripture: Oh, the depth of the riches and wisdom and knowledge of God! — Romans 11:33
devotionals.title: The Depth of Riches
devotionals.prayer: Lord, You are too big for my brain. I stop "analyzing" and I start "adoring" Your deep riches today.

devotionals.reflection: This is the "Full Circle." Everything Starts with Him, is Sustained by Him, and Ends with Him. He is the Point.
devotionals.question: Is my life "To Him" today? Am I living for His glory or my story?
devotionals.scripture: For from him and through him and to him are all things. To him be glory forever. — Romans 11:36
devotionals.title: From Him and To Him
devotionals.prayer: Father, it’s all from You and to You. I give You the glory for everything in my life. Amen.

devotionals.reflection: In the Old Testament, sacrifices were dead. In the New, we are "living" sacrifices—the fire of God consumes our will, not our life.
devotionals.question: What part of my "will" or "agenda" am I refusing to put on the altar today?
devotionals.scripture: I appeal to you... to present your bodies as a living sacrifice... — Romans 12:1
devotionals.title: Living Sacrifice
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I am Yours. I place my body and my plans on Your altar. Burn away the selfish and use the rest.

devotionals.question: Am I letting the "mold" of culture shape my reactions, or is the Spirit "renewing" my perspective?
devotionals.reflection: The world is a "mold" trying to squeeze you into its shape. Transformation starts by changing what you think about.
devotionals.scripture: Do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewal of your mind... — Romans 12:2
devotionals.title: Do Not Be Conformed
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, renew my mind. Help me to see the world through Your eyes, not the media's.

devotionals.question: What "small step of obedience" can I "test" today to see God’s will in action?
devotionals.reflection: You find God's will by "testing" it—trying out obedience and seeing His "good, acceptable, and perfect" results.
devotionals.scripture: ...that by testing you may discern what is the will of God... — Romans 12:2
devotionals.title: Discern the Will
devotionals.prayer: Father, show me Your "perfect" path. I want to walk in what is "good and acceptable" to You.

devotionals.question: Am I "over-estimating" my importance today, or am I resting in my "God-given" measure of faith?
devotionals.reflection: Pride is "drunk thinking." Humility is "sober judgment"—knowing exactly who you are in Christ, nothing more and nothing less.
devotionals.scripture: For... I say to everyone among you not to think of himself more highly than he ought... — Romans 12:3
devotionals.title: Think with Sober Judgment
devotionals.prayer: Lord, give me a "sober" mind. Keep me from pride and help me to see others as more significant than myself.

devotionals.question: Who is a "different part" of the body that I’ve been ignoring or criticizing?
devotionals.reflection: You aren't meant to be "everything." You are a "part" that needs the other parts to function.
devotionals.scripture: For as in one body we have many members... so we, though many, are one body in Christ... — Romans 12:4-5
devotionals.title: One Body, Many Members
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for the Body. Help me to play my part well and value the parts that aren't like me.

devotionals.question: Am I "using" my gift today, or am I "burying" it because I’m comparing it to someone else’s?
devotionals.reflection: Your "Gift" is a "Grace-Deposit." It’s not for your ego; it’s for the church’s health.
devotionals.scripture: Having gifts that differ according to the grace given to us, let us use them... — Romans 12:6
devotionals.title: Gifts that Differ
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the gift You gave me. I commit to using it for Your glory and the good of others.

devotionals.question: Is my "love" for people today "genuine," or am I just "playing the part" while harboring bitterness?
devotionals.reflection: Christian Nice is often fake. "Christian Love" is genuine—it hates evil and clings to good with all its might.
devotionals.scripture: Let love be genuine. Abhor what is evil; hold fast to what is good. — Romans 12:9
devotionals.title: Love Without Hypocrisy
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make my love real. Help me to truly "abhor" the evil in my heart and "hold fast" to Your good.

devotionals.question: Who can I "honor" today in a way that "outdoes" their expectations?
devotionals.reflection: This is the only "competition" allowed in the Kingdom: who can "honor" the other person more.
devotionals.scripture: Love one another with brotherly affection. Outdo one another in showing honor. — Romans 12:10
devotionals.title: Outdo One Another
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, teach me to honor others. Let me be the first to give credit and the last to take it.

devotionals.reflection: Fervent means "boiling." Don't let your spiritual life become "lukewarm" or "slothful." Keep the fire stoked.
devotionals.question: Is my "spiritual temperature" today "boiling" or "barely warm"? How can I "re-stoke" the fire?
devotionals.scripture: Do not be slothful in zeal, be fervent in spirit, serve the Lord. — Romans 12:11
devotionals.title: Fervent in Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, set me on fire again. I refuse to be slothful. I want to serve You with everything I have.

devotionals.reflection: Prayer isn't an "event"; it’s a "state of being." Stay "plugged in" to the Father all day long.
devotionals.question: How can I practice "constant prayer" during my "mundane" tasks today?
devotionals.scripture: Rejoice in hope, be patient in tribulation, be constant in prayer. — Romans 12:12
devotionals.title: Constant in Prayer
devotionals.prayer: Father, I stay in conversation with You. Whether I’m working or resting, I am Yours.

devotionals.question: Who is "persecuting" (annoying/hurting) me today? How can I "bless" them in my heart?
devotionals.reflection: This is the "Supernatural Response." Anyone can "curse" an enemy; only a child of God can "bless" one.
devotionals.scripture: Bless those who persecute you; bless and do not curse them. — Romans 12:14
devotionals.title: Bless Those Who Persecute
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I bless my "enemies." I choose to speak life over them instead of bitterness.

devotionals.question: Whose "weather" do I need to step into today? Who needs me to just "be there"?
devotionals.reflection: Empathy is "stepping into someone else's weather." Don't try to "fix" their feelings; just "be" with them.
devotionals.scripture: Rejoice with those who rejoice, weep with those who weep. — Romans 12:15
devotionals.title: Weep with Those Who Weep
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, give me Your heart of empathy. Help me to feel what others feel and love them through it.

devotionals.reflection: You can't control "them," but you can control "you." Make sure the "peace" isn't broken on your end.
devotionals.question: Is there a "conflict" I’m keeping alive today? What can I do "on my end" to bring peace?
devotionals.scripture: If possible, so far as it depends on you, live peaceably with all. — Romans 12:18
devotionals.title: Live Peaceably
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I am a peacemaker. I take responsibility for my part and I leave the rest to You.

devotionals.question: What "grudge" am I holding onto today? Will I "leave it" in God’s hands instead of my own?
devotionals.reflection: Vengeance is a "Heavy Weight" that only God is strong enough to carry. When you "get even," you push God out of the way.
devotionals.scripture: Beloved, never avenge yourselves, but leave it to the wrath of God... — Romans 12:19
devotionals.title: Never Avenge Yourselves
devotionals.prayer: Father, I release my "right" to get even. I trust You to be the Judge. I choose mercy.

devotionals.question: How can I "overcome" a negative situation today by doing something "unexpectely good"?
devotionals.reflection: You don't "fight" darkness with more darkness; you fight it with "Light." Goodness is the ultimate weapon.
devotionals.scripture: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good. — Romans 12:21
devotionals.title: Overcome Evil with Good
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, show me the "Good" way to respond to evil. Let Your light shine through my actions.

devotionals.reflection: Even when we don't "like" the leader, we "respect" the position. God uses order to prevent chaos.
devotionals.question: Am I "honoring" the leaders in my life (bosses/government) as an act of obedience to God?
devotionals.scripture: Let every person be subject to the governing authorities... — Romans 13:1
devotionals.title: Subject to Authorities
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I submit to the "authorities" You have placed over me. Help me to be a "good citizen" of both Earth and Heaven.

devotionals.reflection: Love is a "Debt" that you can never fully repay. You are "indebted" to every person you meet to show them Christ's love.
devotionals.question: Who do I "owe" a debt of love to today? How can I "make a payment" on that debt?
devotionals.scripture: Owe no one anything, except to love each other... — Romans 13:8
devotionals.title: Owe No One Anything
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the debt of love. Help me to be "generous" with my affection and my service.

devotionals.reflection: If you truly "Love," you won't steal, lie, or kill. Love is the "Shortcut" to keeping all the rules.
devotionals.question: Is my "love" protecting my neighbor today, or am I "justifying" my behavior?
devotionals.scripture: Love does no wrong to a neighbor; therefore love is the fulfilling of the law. — Romans 13:10
devotionals.title: Love Does No Wrong
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, let Your love in me fulfill every requirement of the Law. I want to "do right" because I love well.

devotionals.reflection: Spiritual "drowsiness" is dangerous. The "Dawn" (Christ's return) is closer than we think. Stay awake!
devotionals.question: Am I "sleepwalking" through my spiritual life? What do I need to do to "wake up" today?
devotionals.scripture: Besides this you know the time, that the hour has come for you to wake from sleep. — Romans 13:11
devotionals.title: Wake from Sleep
devotionals.prayer: Lord, wake me up! Give me an "eternal urgency" for the things that really matter.

devotionals.question: What "darkness" do I need to "cast off" this morning? What "Light" am I putting on?
devotionals.reflection: You don't just "stop" doing bad things; you "start" wearing Light. The best "defense" against sin is a "bright" life.
devotionals.scripture: ...let us cast off the works of darkness and put on the armor of light. — Romans 13:12
devotionals.title: Put on the Armor of Light
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are my Light. I put You on today like armor. Protect me from the shadows.

devotionals.reflection: We often "quarrel" over things that don't matter. Welcome the person; leave the "opinion-argument" at the door.
devotionals.question: Am I being "welcoming" to those who disagree with me on "minor" issues?
devotionals.scripture: As for the one who is weak in faith, welcome him, but not to quarrel over opinions. — Romans 13:14:1
devotionals.title: Welcome the Weak
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me a large heart. Help me to "welcome" my brothers and sisters without a "critical" spirit.

devotionals.question: Am I trying to "play Holy Spirit" in someone else's life today? Will I "mind my own business"?
devotionals.reflection: Every Christian has a "Master" (Jesus). It’s not your job to "supervise" another person’s walk; that’s Jesus' job.
devotionals.scripture: Who are you to pass judgment on the servant of another? It is before his own master that he stands... — Romans 14:4
devotionals.title: Who Are You to Pass Judgment?
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I stay in my lane. You are the Master. I trust You to lead Your other servants.

devotionals.reflection: Some things are "gray areas." In those areas, follow your "conscience" before God and let others do the same.
devotionals.question: Am I acting out of "fear of people" or out of a "full conviction" before God?
devotionals.scripture: Each one should be fully convinced in his own mind. — Romans 14:5
devotionals.title: Fully Convinced
devotionals.prayer: Father, help me to live "before Your face" with a clear and convinced mind.

devotionals.question: Who am I "living for" today? Am I trying to "impress" people or "honor" the Lord?
devotionals.reflection: Your "Audience" is a person of One. Whether you are "Eating" or "Fasting," do it for Him.
devotionals.scripture: For if we live, we live to the Lord, and if we die, we die to the Lord. — Romans 14:8
devotionals.title: Living to the Lord
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I live for You. Every breath, every meal, and every task is an offering to You.

devotionals.reflection: We will all "give an account" of our own lives, not our neighbor’s. Focus on your own "Report Card."
devotionals.question: Am I prepared to "give an account" for my actions and my attitudes today?
devotionals.scripture: For we will all stand before the judgment seat of God. — Romans 14:10
devotionals.title: The Judgment Seat of God
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I live with the "Judgment Seat" in view. Keep me faithful to my own call.

devotionals.reflection: Your "Freedom" shouldn't come at the cost of someone else's "Faith." If it "trips them up," it’s not worth doing.
devotionals.question: What "right" or "freedom" am I willing to "give up" today to protect a brother/sister?
devotionals.scripture: ...decide never to put a stumbling block or hindrance in the way of a brother. — Romans 14:13
devotionals.title: Not a Stumbling Block
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I value my family more than my rights. Help me to be a "stepping stone," not a "stumbling block."

devotionals.question: Does my "religion" today look like "Rules" or like "Peace and Joy in the Holy Spirit"?
devotionals.reflection: Don't get "stuck" in the "Rules." The Kingdom is about the "Character" (Righteousness) and the "Atmosphere" (Peace/Joy) of the Spirit.
devotionals.scripture: For the kingdom of God is not a matter of eating and drinking but of righteousness and peace and joy... — Romans 14:17
devotionals.title: Righteousness, Peace, and Joy
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, fill me with Your Kingdom. Let my life overflow with Your righteousness, peace, and joy.

devotionals.reflection: Jesus didn't "please himself"; he served us. We are called to "build up" our neighbors, even when it’s inconvenient.
devotionals.question: Who can I "build up" today by "pleasing" them instead of "insisting" on my own way?
devotionals.scripture: Let each of us please his neighbor for his good, to build him up. — Romans 15:2
devotionals.title: Please Your Neighbor
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me a builder. Help me to "please" others for their "good" and Your glory.

devotionals.reflection: Hope is the "gasoline" of the Christian life. God "fills" us so we can "overflow" into a hopeless world.
devotionals.question: Is my "hope-tank" full today? How can I ask the "God of Hope" for a refill?
devotionals.scripture: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing... that you may abound in hope. — Romans 15:13
devotionals.title: Abound in Hope
devotionals.prayer: God of Hope, fill me! Let me abound in Your joy and peace until it overflows to everyone I meet.

devotionals.reflection: Paul’s final chapter is a "shout-out" to his friends. Ministry is about "People," not just "Projects."
devotionals.question: Who can I "greet" today with a "Holy Kiss" (a warm, sincere word of affection)?
devotionals.scripture: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ greet you. — Romans 16:16
devotionals.title: Greet One Another
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for my friends in the faith. Help me to value them and "greet" them with Your love.

devotionals.question: Am I being "fooled" by "smooth talk" today? Will I ask the Spirit for "discernment"?
devotionals.reflection: Beware of people who "sound good" but "serve themselves." Discernment looks at the "Fruit," not just the "Words."
devotionals.scripture: For such persons do not serve our Lord Christ... and by smooth talk and flattery they deceive... — Romans 16:18
devotionals.title: Smooth Talk and Flattery
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me eyes to see the truth. Keep me from being "deceived" by flattery.

devotionals.question: How does the "future crushing" of the enemy give me "Peace" in my current battle?
devotionals.reflection: The "God of Peace" is the one who "Crushes." Victory is coming, and it will be "Under Your Feet."
devotionals.scripture: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. — Romans 16:20
devotionals.title: Crushing Satan
devotionals.prayer: God of Peace, I trust Your victory. Crush the enemy’s influence in my life and my family today.

devotionals.question: Do I trust God's "ability to strengthen me" today, or am I relying on my own "weak muscles"?
devotionals.reflection: We end Romans with a "Doxology." God is "Able" to keep you strong until the very end.
devotionals.scripture: Now to him who is able to strengthen you according to my gospel... be glory forevermore. — Romans 16:25-27
devotionals.title: Strength to the End
devotionals.prayer: Father, strengthen me! I give You the glory for my past, my present, and my future. Amen.

devotionals.reflection: Welcome to Corinth—the city of "Chaos." Paul starts by reminding them they are "Sanctified" (Set Apart), even in the mess.
devotionals.question: Do I realize that I am a "Saint" (set apart) today, even if my life feels a little "messy"?
devotionals.scripture: To the church of God... to those sanctified in Christ Jesus, called to be saints... — 1 Corinthians 1:2
devotionals.title: Sanctified in Christ
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for sanctifying me. I am Your "Saint," and I walk in that identity today.

devotionals.reflection: You have "Everything You Need" to survive and thrive until Jesus returns. God doesn't "short-change" His children.
devotionals.question: Am I complaining about what I "lack," or am I "using" the gifts God has already given me?
devotionals.scripture: ...so that you are not lacking in any gift, as you wait for the revealing of our Lord... — 1 Corinthians 1:7
devotionals.title: Not Lacking in Any Gift
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for Your provision. I have "no lack" in You. Help me to use what I have.

devotionals.question: Is there a "division" in my heart toward a fellow believer? How can I "repair" the tear?
devotionals.reflection: Divisions "tear" the Body. Unity is a "choice" to have the "Same Mind"—the Mind of Christ.
devotionals.scripture: I appeal to you... that there be no divisions among you, but that you be united in the same mind... — 1 Corinthians 1:10
devotionals.title: No Divisions
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, make us one. Remove the "factions" and the "cliques" from my heart and my church.

devotionals.reflection: To the world, a "dying God" is a joke. To us, it’s the "Dynamite" that saved our souls.
devotionals.question: Am I "embarrassed" by the "foolishness" of the Cross, or do I glory in its Power?
devotionals.scripture: For the word of the cross is folly to those who are perishing, but to us... it is the power of God. — 1 Corinthians 1:18
devotionals.title: Foolishness of the Cross
devotionals.prayer: Father, I glory in the Cross! Thank You for the "Folly" that brought me Life.

devotionals.reflection: God loves "Underdogs." He uses the "unlikely" to prove that the Power is from Him, not us.
devotionals.question: Am I "proud" of my "wisdom," or am I "humble" enough to be used by God as a "fool"?
devotionals.scripture: But God chose what is foolish in the world to shame the wise... — 1 Corinthians 1:27
devotionals.title: God Chose the Foolish
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I am an "underdog." Use my "weakness" to show Your "Strength" to the world today.

devotionals.reflection: You don't "have" wisdom; you "have" Jesus. He is your wisdom, your righteousness, and your life.
devotionals.question: Am I "looking for wisdom" in a book today, or am I "looking for Jesus"?
devotionals.scripture: ...Christ Jesus, who became to us wisdom from God, righteousness and sanctification... — 1 Corinthians 1:30
devotionals.title: Christ Our Wisdom
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are my Wisdom. Lead my thoughts and my decisions today.

devotionals.question: Am I "waiting to feel confident" before I serve God? (Hint: Use your "trembling" hands!)
devotionals.reflection: Even Paul felt "fear" and "trembling" when he spoke. Being "used by God" doesn't mean you have to be "confident" in yourself.
devotionals.scripture: And I was with you in weakness and in fear and much trembling... — 1 Corinthians 2:3
devotionals.title: Weakness and Fear
devotionals.prayer: Father, I come to You in my "weakness." Let Your Power be made perfect in my "trembling."

devotionals.reflection: The Gospel isn't an "argument" to be won; it’s a "Power" to be demonstrated. Let your "Life" be the proof.
devotionals.question: Is my faith based on "clever words" or on a "demonstration" of God's power in my life?
devotionals.scripture: ...my message [was] not in plausible words of wisdom, but in a demonstration of the Spirit and of power. — 1 Corinthians 2:4
devotionals.title: Demonstration of Power
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, demonstrate Your power through me today. Let my life be the "evidence" of Your reality.

devotionals.question: Am I asking the Spirit to "unveil" the "hidden wisdom" of God to me today?
devotionals.reflection: God has "Secrets" for His friends. There is a depth to the Word that only the "spiritually mature" can see.
devotionals.scripture: But we impart a secret and hidden wisdom of God... — 1 Corinthians 2:7
devotionals.title: Secret and Hidden Wisdom
devotionals.prayer: Lord, tell me Your secrets. Show me the "hidden things" in Your Word and Your heart.

devotionals.question: Am I "worrying" about a future that is "better than I can imagine"?
devotionals.reflection: You can't even "imagine" how good the future is. God’s "Surprises" are better than your "Dreams."
devotionals.scripture: What no eye has seen, nor ear heard... what God has prepared for those who love him. — 1 Corinthians 2:9
devotionals.title: Eye Has Not Seen
devotionals.prayer: Father, I trust Your "preparations." Thank You for the "unseen" blessings You have for me.

devotionals.reflection: The "Author" (the Holy Spirit) is the best "Teacher." Ask Him to explain what He wrote.
devotionals.question: Am I reading the Bible with the "Author" present, or am I trying to figure it out alone?
devotionals.scripture: ...interpreting spiritual truths to those who are spiritual. — 1 Corinthians 2:13
devotionals.title: Taught by the Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, teach me. "Interpret" Your truths to my heart as I read today.

devotionals.reflection: You have "Direct Access" to the thoughts of God. You can think about your problems "the Jesus way."
devotionals.question: How can I use the "Mind of Christ" to solve a "problem" I’m facing today?
devotionals.scripture: But we have the mind of Christ. — 1 Corinthians 2:16
devotionals.title: The Mind of Christ
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, give me Your mind. Help me to "think Your thoughts" after You.

devotionals.reflection: It’s okay to be an "infant," but it’s not okay to "stay" one. Growth is the natural result of life.
devotionals.question: Am I "growing up" in my faith, or am I still "crying for milk" (attention/comfort) every day?
devotionals.scripture: But I, brothers, could not address you as spiritual people, but as... infants in Christ. — 1 Corinthians 3:1
devotionals.title: Infants in Christ
devotionals.prayer: Lord, help me to "grow up." I want to move from "milk" to "solid food" in Your Word.

devotionals.reflection: Don't "worship" the gardener; "worship" the One who makes the seeds grow. It’s all about God's Power.
devotionals.question: Am I "comparing" my leaders, or am I looking to "God who gives the growth"?
devotionals.scripture: I planted, Apollos watered, but God gave the growth. — 1 Corinthians 3:6
devotionals.title: God Gave the Growth
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for the "planters" and the "waterers." But I give You the glory for the growth.

devotionals.reflection: You aren't "working for God"; you are "working with God." He is your "Partner" in everything.
devotionals.question: How does it change my "workday" to know that I am a "fellow worker" with the Creator?
devotionals.scripture: For we are God's fellow workers. You are God's field, God's building. — 1 Corinthians 3:9
devotionals.title: Fellow Workers
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, let’s work together today. I am Your "field" and Your "building." Do Your work in me.

devotionals.question: What "foundation" am I building my "happiness" on today? Is it "Jesus" or something else?
devotionals.reflection: If you build on "Money," "Fame," or "Self," the house will fall. Build on "Jesus" and you are "Unshakable."
devotionals.scripture: For no one can lay a foundation other than that which is laid, which is Jesus Christ. — 1 Corinthians 3:11
devotionals.title: One Foundation
devotionals.prayer: Lord, You are my Foundation. I build my life on the solid rock of Your Word.

devotionals.question: Is the "work" I’m doing today "Gold" (love) or "Stubble" (ego)?
devotionals.reflection: It’s not about "How Much" you do; it’s about "What Kind" of work you do. Only "Love-motivated" work survives the fire.
devotionals.scripture: Each one's work will become manifest... for the Day will disclose it, because it will be revealed by fire. — 1 Corinthians 3:13
devotionals.title: Fire Will Test
devotionals.prayer: Father, help me to build with "Gold." Let my motives be pure so my work will endure Your "fire."

devotionals.question: How would I treat my "day" differently if I realized I am a "walking temple" of God?
