devotionals.reflection: You are "Holy Ground." The Spirit "dwells" (lives) in you. Treat your body and your mind like a sanctuary.
devotionals.scripture: Do you not know that you are God's temple and that God's Spirit dwells in you? — 1 Corinthians 3:16
devotionals.title: Temple of God
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, thank You for dwelling in me. Help me to keep Your "temple" clean and honorable today.

devotionals.reflection: To get "God's Wisdom," you have to "unlearn" the world's wisdom. Humility is the prerequisite for insight.
devotionals.question: What "worldly wisdom" (cynicism/selfishness) do I need to "unlearn" to become truly wise?
devotionals.scripture: If anyone among you thinks that he is wise... let him become a fool that he may become wise. — 1 Corinthians 3:18
devotionals.title: Foolish to be Wise
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me a "fool" for Your sake. Give me Your Wisdom and take away my pride.

devotionals.reflection: You are a "joint-heir" with Christ. You don't need to "envy" anyone because you already "own" the Kingdom.
devotionals.question: Am I living like a "spiritual billionaire" today, or am I "grudging" others for what they have?
devotionals.scripture: So let no one boast in men. For all things are yours... — 1 Corinthians 3:21
devotionals.title: All Things are Yours
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You that "all things are mine" in Christ. I rest in Your abundance today.

devotionals.question: Am I being a "faithful manager" of the "mysteries" (truths) God has entrusted to me?
devotionals.reflection: A "Steward" doesn't "own" the stuff; they "manage" it for the Owner. You are a "Manager" of God's Truth.
devotionals.scripture: This is how one should regard us... as stewards of the mysteries of God. — 1 Corinthians 4:1
devotionals.title: Stewards of Mysteries
devotionals.prayer: Lord, make me a "faithful steward." Help me to manage Your truth and Your resources with integrity.

devotionals.reflection: We don't have the "full story" yet. Only the Lord can "disclose the purposes of the heart." Wait for His verdict.
devotionals.question: Am I "judging" someone’s motives today without having all the facts?
devotionals.scripture: Therefore do not pronounce judgment before the time... when the Lord comes. — 1 Corinthians 4:5
devotionals.title: Judge Nothing Before the Time
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I leave the "judgment" to You. I don't know the heart, but You do. I choose to wait for Your light.

devotionals.reflection: Everything you are—your brain, your health, your faith—is a "Gift." Boasting is just "forgetting the Giver."
devotionals.question: What "gift" am I tempted to "boast about" today as if I "earned" it?
devotionals.scripture: What do you have that you did not receive? If then you received it, why do you boast...? — 1 Corinthians 4:7
devotionals.title: What Do You Have?
devotionals.prayer: Father, everything I have is from You. Thank You for Your "unmerited" gifts. I give You all the praise.

devotionals.reflection: Paul was willing to "look like a loser" so that the Gospel could win. Your "reputation" is a small price to pay for the Truth.
devotionals.question: Am I willing to "look foolish" today to stand up for what is Right?
devotionals.scripture: We are fools for Christ's sake, but you are wise in Christ... — 1 Corinthians 4:10
devotionals.title: Fools for Christ's Sake
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, I’m okay with being a "fool" for Your sake. My reputation belongs to You.

devotionals.question: Who do I need to "admonish" (warn) today out of a heart of "beloved affection"?
devotionals.reflection: Correction should come from "Love," not "Shame." We "admonish" (warn) because we "care."
devotionals.scripture: I do not write these things to make you ashamed, but to admonish you as my beloved children. — 1 Corinthians 4:14
devotionals.title: My Beloved Children
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me a "fatherly/motherly" heart. Help me to speak the truth in love without shaming others.

devotionals.question: Is my "faith" today just "talk," or am I experiencing the "Power" of the Kingdom in my actions?
devotionals.reflection: Anyone can "talk" a good game. The Kingdom is seen in the "Power" to change a life, a habit, or a heart.
devotionals.scripture: For the kingdom of God does not consist in talk but in power. — 1 Corinthians 4:20
devotionals.title: Not Talk, but Power
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I don't want just "talk." I want Your "Power" to be evident in my life today.

devotionals.question: Am I approaching "difficult people" today with a "rod" (harshness) or with "gentleness"?
devotionals.reflection: We choose the "tool" of our approach. "Gentleness" is usually the better choice for restoration.
devotionals.scripture: What do you wish? Shall I come to you with a rod, or with love in a spirit of gentleness? — 1 Corinthians 4:21
devotionals.title: A Spirit of Gentleness
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, You are "gentle and lowly in heart." Make me like You as I deal with the "messes" of this day.

devotionals.reflection: A little sin (leaven) affects the whole batch. Integrity isn't about being "mostly good"; it's about removing the "hidden" pride.
devotionals.question: What "small compromise" am I allowing today that might be "leavening" my whole character?
devotionals.scripture: Cleanse out the old leaven that you may be a new lump... — 1 Cor 5:7
devotionals.title: Purge the Leaven
devotionals.prayer: Lord, search my "pantry." Cleanse out the old habits so I can be "unleavened" in Your sight.

devotionals.reflection: The blood is on the doorpost. You are safe from judgment because the Lamb took the hit. Celebrate the "Festival" of Grace!
devotionals.question: Am I living in "Fear" of the plague, or in the "Freedom" of the Passover Lamb?
devotionals.scripture: For Christ, our Passover lamb, has been sacrificed. — 1 Cor 5:7
devotionals.title: Christ Our Passover
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for being my Sacrifice. I feast on Your faithfulness today.

devotionals.question: Am I being "harsh" on non-believers while "excusing" my own brothers and sisters?
devotionals.reflection: We often judge the "world" and ignore the "church." God calls us to hold each other to a higher standard of love.
devotionals.scripture: For what have I to do with judging outsiders? Is it not those inside the church whom you are to judge? — 1 Cor 5:12
devotionals.title: Judging Insiders
devotionals.prayer: Father, give me a heart that protects the holiness of Your family first.

devotionals.question: What "right" am I fighting for today that I might need to "surrender" for the sake of peace?
devotionals.reflection: Sometimes "winning" a legal battle is "losing" a spiritual one. It is better to be "cheated" than to ruin the Gospel's name.
devotionals.scripture: To have lawsuits at all... is already a defeat... Why not rather suffer wrong? — 1 Cor 6:7
devotionals.title: Lawsuits and Loss
devotionals.prayer: Lord, my "rights" are in Your hands. I would rather "suffer wrong" than dishonor Your Name.

devotionals.question: Do I still identify with my "old sins," or do I identify with my "new washing"?
devotionals.reflection: Your "Past" is a "Was." You aren't who you were; you are who God says you are now.
devotionals.scripture: And such were some of you. But you were washed, you were sanctified... — 1 Cor 6:11
devotionals.title: Such Were Some of You
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for the "But." I was lost, BUT I am washed. I was a sinner, BUT I am a saint.

devotionals.question: Is this habit "building me up" or is it just something I’m "allowed" to do?
devotionals.reflection: Just because you "can" do something doesn't mean you "should." Maturity is choosing the "Helpful" over the "Legal."
devotionals.scripture: All things are lawful for me,' but not all things are helpful. — 1 Cor 6:12
devotionals.title: All Things Lawful?
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, guide my choices. I don't want to just be "legal"; I want to be "helpful."

devotionals.reflection: If you "can't quit it," it’s your master. True freedom is the power to say "No" to a neutral thing.
devotionals.question: Is there any "neutral" thing (phone, food, hobby) that is "dominating" me today?
devotionals.scripture: All things are lawful for me,' but I will not be dominated by anything. — 1 Cor 6:12
devotionals.title: Not Dominated
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I refuse to be dominated by anything but You. Break the power of my "lesser masters."

devotionals.question: Am I treating my "physical self" today as a "tool" for God's purposes?
devotionals.reflection: Your "skin and bones" are a "Sacred Space." What you do with your body, you do with the Lord.
devotionals.scripture: The body is not meant for sexual immorality, but for the Lord... — 1 Cor 6:13
devotionals.title: Bodies for the Lord
devotionals.prayer: Father, my body is Yours. Use my eyes, my hands, and my heart for Your glory.

devotionals.question: What situation do I need to "run away from" today to protect my integrity?
devotionals.reflection: You don't "fight" some temptations; you "run" from them. Wisdom knows when to "Flee" instead of "Stand."
devotionals.scripture: Flee from sexual immorality. — 1 Cor 6:18
devotionals.title: Flee Immorality
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, give me "fast feet." Help me to flee from anything that would stain my soul.

devotionals.reflection: You are a "Mobile Sanctuary." God isn't in a "building"; He is in "you." Carry Yourself with dignity.
devotionals.question: How would I treat my "temple" today if I truly believed God was "sitting" in my heart?
devotionals.scripture: Or do you not know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit...? — 1 Cor 6:19
devotionals.title: Temple of the Spirit
devotionals.prayer: Holy Spirit, I honor Your presence in me. Make my life a "holy place" for You to rest.

devotionals.reflection: You are "Paid For." You don't belong to yourself anymore; you belong to the One who bought you.
devotionals.question: Since I am "not my own," how should I handle "my" schedule and "my" money today?
devotionals.scripture: ...you are not your own, for you were bought with a price. — 1 Cor 6:19-20
devotionals.title: Bought with a Price
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I am Your "property." Handle me as You wish. I trust Your ownership.

devotionals.question: How can my "physical actions" (service/rest/kindness) glorify God on this Christmas Day?
devotionals.reflection: On Christmas, God "became a body" (Jesus). Now, He wants to "be glorified" in your body.
devotionals.scripture: So glorify God in your body. — 1 Cor 6:20
devotionals.title: Glorify God in Your Body
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for taking on flesh. Help me to use my "flesh" to reflect Your love today.

devotionals.question: Am I "waiting for a better life" to start serving, or am I "leading the life" God assigned me right now?
devotionals.reflection: You don't have to "change your circumstances" to serve God. He "assigned" you to this moment for a reason.
devotionals.scripture: Only let each person lead the life that the Lord has assigned to him... — 1 Cor 7:17
devotionals.title: Remain as You Are
devotionals.prayer: Father, I accept my "assignment." I will serve You right where I am, in this season and this place.

devotionals.reflection: Your "Social Status" doesn't matter to God. If you are a slave on earth, you are a "Freedman" in Heaven.
devotionals.question: Do I see myself as "Free" in Christ, regardless of my "job title" or "bank account"?
devotionals.scripture: For he who was called in the Lord as a bondservant is a freedman of the Lord. — 1 Cor 7:22
devotionals.title: God's Freedman
devotionals.prayer: Jesus, thank You for my freedom. I am Your servant, and that makes me the freest person on earth.

devotionals.question: What is "dividing" my heart today? What can I "cut out" to give God my "Full Attention"?
devotionals.reflection: Life is full of "Distractions." Paul’s goal (and God’s) is for your heart to be "Undivided."
devotionals.scripture: I say this... to secure your undivided devotion to the Lord. — 1 Cor 7:35
devotionals.title: Undivided Devotion
devotionals.prayer: Lord, I give You my "Undivided Devotion." Take the "pieces" of my heart and make them "One" in You.

devotionals.question: Am I using my "Bible knowledge" today to "win arguments" or to "build people up"?
devotionals.reflection: Being "Right" isn't the goal; being "Loving" is. Knowledge without love is just "spiritual ego."
devotionals.scripture: Knowledge 'puffs up,' but love builds up. — 1 Cor 8:1
devotionals.title: Knowledge Puffs Up
devotionals.prayer: Father, don't let me be "puffed up." Fill me with the Love that "builds up" my brothers and sisters.

devotionals.reflection: It is better to be "Known by God" than to "Know everything." Relationship is the ultimate "Knowledge."
devotionals.question: Does it comfort me to know that the Creator of the Universe "Knows my name" today?
devotionals.scripture: But if anyone loves God, he is known by God. — 1 Cor 8:3
devotionals.title: Known by God
devotionals.prayer: Lord, thank You for "Knowing" me. I love You, and I am secure in Your intimate gaze.

devotionals.question: As I look back on 2028, did I "do it all" for His glory? How can I start 2029 with this one goal?
devotionals.reflection: We end the year with the "Universal Rule." Every meal, every mile, and every moment is an opportunity to say, "God, You are Great."
devotionals.scripture: So, whether you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God. — 1 Cor 10:31
devotionals.title: To the Glory of God
devotionals.prayer: Father, thank You for 2028. As I enter a new year, let my "eating, drinking, and doing" all be for Your Glory. Amen.

heart.Question: I have gone more than three days this week without intentional, unhurried prayer.
heart.Prescription: Set one specific prayer time today — even five minutes. Start with: "God, I don't know how to pray right now." He will meet you there.

heart.Question: I find myself anxious about something I haven't fully brought to God.
heart.Prescription: Write down the specific worry. Spend five minutes talking to God about it — surrendering it rather than just asking Him to fix it.

heart.Question: I have been reaching out to people about my problems more than reaching out to God.
heart.Prescription: Before texting or calling someone this week, spend five minutes praying about it first. Let God be your first call.

heart.Question: I have gone more than three days without reading the Bible.
heart.Prescription: Start with one chapter of Psalms or Proverbs today. Consistency beats volume — five minutes every day beats an hour once a week.

heart.Question: I struggle to understand or apply what I read in Scripture to daily life.
heart.Prescription: After reading one passage, ask two questions: What does this say about God? What does it ask of me? Write one answer down.

heart.Question: I am relying more on my own understanding than on God's Word in a decision I am facing.
heart.Prescription: Before making this decision, search the Scriptures for wisdom. Ask a trusted believer to pray with you about it.

heart.Question: There is someone I have not fully forgiven.
heart.Prescription: Write their name and one thing you want to release. Pray for them by name today — even if it feels impossible.

heart.Question: I know I have hurt someone and I have not made it right.
heart.Prescription: Reach out this week with a sincere apology — no explanations, just ownership of what you did.

heart.Question: I am carrying bitterness or resentment toward someone.
heart.Prescription: Bitterness takes root slowly and poisons deeply. Ask God to replace it with compassion. Consider talking to a pastor.

heart.Question: I haven't shared my faith — or something God has done in my life — with anyone this month.
heart.Prescription: Think of one person in your life who doesn't know Jesus. Pray for them by name this week and look for one natural opening.

heart.Question: I feel ashamed or afraid to identify myself as a follower of Jesus in some contexts.
heart.Prescription: Ask God to give you a love for people that overcomes fear of their opinion. Think of one specific context where you held back.

heart.Question: I have been attending church infrequently or sitting through it passively.
heart.Prescription: Show up this Sunday with one expectation: ask God to speak to you personally. Write down what you hear.

heart.Question: I feel disconnected from Christian community right now.
heart.Prescription: Isolation is the enemy's favorite tool. Reach out to one believer this week — not for advice, just for connection.

heart.Question: I am anxious about finances or material needs instead of trusting God as my provider.
heart.Prescription: Identify one area where you can simplify. Then give something away this week — generosity breaks the grip of financial anxiety.

heart.Question: I have not been giving generously — of time, money, or gifts.
heart.Prescription: Generosity is a spiritual discipline, not just a financial act. Start with one intentional, specific act of giving this week.

mirror.Question: I have been comparing myself to others and feeling either superior or inferior.
mirror.Prescription: Your identity is hidden with Christ. Compare yourself only to who God is calling you to become — not to others.

mirror.Question: I struggle to receive correction or feedback without feeling defensive or wounded.
mirror.Prescription: A defensive spirit blocks growth. Ask God this week for a teachable heart — then actively seek one person's honest input.

mirror.Question: I have been seeking validation from people more than from God.
mirror.Prescription: Spend time in Psalm 139 this week. Let God's view of you be the view that settles the question of your worth.

mirror.Question: There is an area of sin I am aware of but have not surrendered or confessed.
mirror.Prescription: Unconfessed sin loses its power when brought into the light. Tell a trusted pastor or believer today.

mirror.Question: I have been rationalizing or justifying behavior I know is wrong.
mirror.Prescription: Write down the rationalization. Then ask: Would I say this directly to Jesus? Let that clarity guide your next step.

mirror.Question: I know what God is calling me to do in a specific area, but I am delaying.
mirror.Prescription: Delayed obedience is partial disobedience. What one small step can you take today — not next week, today?

mirror.Question: I have been withdrawing from accountability, community, or close relationships.
mirror.Prescription: The isolated sheep is the one in danger. Reach out to your accountability partner or pastor this week — even just to check in.

mirror.Question: There is a broken relationship I have not taken meaningful steps to restore.
mirror.Prescription: You can only control your side of the table. Write down what you would need to say to make peace — even if the other person isn't ready.

mirror.Question: I have been harsh, unfair, or unkind to someone in my home or close circle.
mirror.Prescription: The people closest to us deserve our best, not our worst. Apologize specifically and ask what they need from you.

mirror.Question: My prayer life has been inconsistent, shallow, or effectively absent.
mirror.Prescription: Start with: "Lord, I don't know how to pray right now." He will meet you there. Commit to ten minutes daily this week.

mirror.Question: I have been depending more on information about God than on actual relationship with God.
mirror.Prescription: After reading Scripture this week, spend as many minutes in silence as you did reading. Let God speak.

mirror.Question: I have been living reactively — pulled along by the day — rather than surrendering each morning to God.
mirror.Prescription: Set your phone to Do Not Disturb for the first fifteen minutes of your morning. Give the day to God before the world takes it.

mirror.Question: I have used my words to wound, gossip about, or diminish someone.
mirror.Prescription: Words cannot be unsaid, but they can be followed by something healing. Go back to that person and speak life this week.

mirror.Question: I have been complaining, criticizing, or expressing cynicism more than gratitude.
mirror.Prescription: At the end of each day this week, name three specific things you are grateful for. Write them down. Do not let them be generic.

mirror.Question: I have been dishonest, misleading, or less than fully transparent in any context recently.
mirror.Prescription: Integrity is built in small moments. Identify the specific situation and make it right this week.

theology.categoryIntro: "Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God, the LORD is one." — Deuteronomy 6:4
theology.content: I believe in One Eternal God, who exists in three distinct Persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit (Matthew 28:19; 2 Corinthians 13:14). The Father reveals Himself through the Son (John 1:18) and the Holy Spirit glorifies the Son (John 16:14). In Christ we behold the Fullness of the Godhead in bodily form (Colossians 2:9), and through Him we are reconciled to God (Ephesians 2:13-18). Christ is the Word made Flesh (John 1:14).
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of God — His nature, attributes, and triune being

theology.categoryIntro: "All Scripture is breathed out by God and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness." — 2 Timothy 3:16
theology.content: I believe the Bible, consisting of the sixty-six books of the Old (39) and New Testaments (27), is the inspired, infallible, and inerrant Word of God (2 Timothy 3:16-17; 2 Peter 1:20-21). I believe and affirm that the Scriptures are sufficient and authoritative for all matters of faith and life. The Scripture reveals the glory, will, and redemptive plan of God through Jesus Christ (Luke 24:27; John 5:39). I affirm that its literal, grammatical, and historical method of interpretation is the correct interpretation. Further, I submit to Scripture as the final authority in my life and ministry.
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of Holy Scripture — its inspiration, authority, and sufficiency

theology.content: I believe and testify that Jesus Christ is the Eternal Son of God, fully God and fully Man (John 1:1-14; Colossians 1:15-20). He was born of a virgin (Matthew 1:23), lived a sinless life (Hebrews 4:15), died as a substitutionary sacrifice for our sins (Isaiah 53:4-6; 1 Peter 2:24), rose bodily from the dead (1 Corinthians 15:3-4), and ascended into heaven, where He intercedes for His people (Romans 8:34; Hebrews 7:25). In Him alone we have redemption, reconciliation, and eternal hope (Colossians 1:14-27). Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever (Hebrews 13:8).
theology.categoryIntro: "Jesus said to him, I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me." — John 14:6
theology.categorySubtitle: The person and work of Jesus Christ — fully God and fully man

theology.categoryIntro: "And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Helper, to be with you forever." — John 14:16
theology.content: I believe the Holy Spirit is the third Person of the Trinity, fully Divine and Eternally existent (Acts 5:3-4). He regenerates (Titus 3:4-6) the sinner (John 3:5-8), indwells every believer (Romans 8:9), and empowers the Church to proclaim the Gospel (Acts 1:8). He distributes spiritual gifts for the building up of the Body of Christ (1 Corinthians 12:4-11), and I affirm that He still works today according to His will-always to glorify Christ and never to exalt man (1 Corinthians 14:26-33; John 16:14).
theology.categorySubtitle: The person and work of the Holy Spirit

theology.content:
Salvation is by grace alone, through faith alone, in Jesus Christ alone (Ephesians 2:8-9; Acts 4:12). It involves election (Ephesians 1:4-5), regeneration (Titus 3:5), justification (Romans 5:1), adoption (Galatians 4:4-6), sanctification (1 Thessalonians 4:3), and glorification (Romans 8:30). Saving faith always bears fruit: repentance (Acts 2:38), a new heart (Ezekiel 36:26), and a transformed life (2 Corinthians 5:17; James 2:17). Those in Christ are sealed by the Holy Spirit and kept by the power of God (Romans 8:38-39; Philippians 1:6).I affirm that election is a sovereign act of God in which He chooses, according to His grace and purposes, those who will be saved (Ephesians 1:4-6; Romans 8:29-30). His divine initiative does not diminish human responsibility; in fact, if anything, it would remind us all the more that all (every soul, every beating heart) are called to repent and believe the Gospel (Acts 17:30; Romans 10:9-13). God’s election guarantees the believer’s security, magnifies His mercy, and leads to praise. Grace is not the believer's pride, but rather, a joy from within.He did it for our sake, not for our pride.
“For our sake He made Him to be sin who knew no sin, so that in Him we might become the righteousness of God.” (2 Corinthians 5:21)
theology.categoryIntro: "For by grace you have been saved through faith. And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God." — Ephesians 2:8
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of salvation — grace, faith, and redemption through Christ alone

theology.content: I believe that the body is the temple of the Holy Spirit (1 Corinthians 6:19-20), and that Christians are called to live sober-minded lives (1 Peter 5:8). Addiction to substances such as alcohol, drugs, or other life-controlling habits is destructive and dishonoring to God and The Gospel offers deliverance, healing, and new life to those enslaved by addiction (Titus 2:11-14; Romans 6:12-14). In the same way, I believe we ought to operate with a high degree of compassion toward the brother or sister struggling with addiction.
theology.categoryIntro: "So God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him; male and female he created them." — Genesis 1:27
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of humanity — created in God's image, fallen, and redeemable

theology.categoryIntro: "So God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him; male and female he created them." — Genesis 1:27
theology.content: I believe that God created humanity male and female (Genesis 1:27) and that gender is not a social ideology, but rather, a biological and spiritual reality. Attempts to redefine gender, alter one’s sex, or embrace same-sex relationships are contrary to God's design (Romans 1:24-28). That said, all people-regardless of sexual identity-are loved by God and can find forgiveness and transformation in Jesus Christ (1 Corinthians 6:11). As the Church, we reject both hatred and compromise, choosing instead to love the sinner, hate the sin, and call all people to repentance and faith.
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of humanity — created in God's image, fallen, and redeemable

theology.content: I believe that human life begins at conception and is a sacred gift from God. Every human being-regardless of age, ability, or circumstance-is created in the image of God (Genesis 1:27; Psalm 139:13-16). Abortion is unjust, in the taking of human life and is morally and biblically wrong (Exodus 20:13). As believers, we are called to protect the unborn, care for vulnerable mothers, and support life-affirming alternatives (Proverbs 24:11; James 1:27).
theology.categoryIntro: "So God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him; male and female he created them." — Genesis 1:27
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of humanity — created in God's image, fallen, and redeemable

theology.content: I believe mankind was created in the image of God (Genesis 1:26-27), but through Adam's disobedience, all have inherited a sinful nature (Romans 5:12). All are sinners by nature and choice (Romans 3:23; Ephesians 2:1-3) and are utterly incapable of saving themselves apart from divine grace. This is why we all have a need for a savior, and there is only One Savior for all.
theology.categoryIntro: "So God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him; male and female he created them." — Genesis 1:27
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of humanity — created in God's image, fallen, and redeemable

theology.categoryIntro: "So God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him; male and female he created them." — Genesis 1:27
theology.content: I believe that God designed men and women as equal in value and dignity, but distinct in role and function (1 Corinthians 11:3; 1 Timothy 2:12-14). In the home and in the Church, God has assigned unique leadership roles to men, and complementary roles to women. These roles are not based on cultural norms, but on God’s wise design for human flourishing.
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of humanity — created in God's image, fallen, and redeemable

theology.content: I believe that marriage is a covenant instituted by God between one man and one woman (Genesis 2:24; Matthew 19:4-6). Sexual intimacy is designed by God for the context of this covenant. Any sexual activity outside of biblical marriage-including fornication, adultery, homosexuality, or pornography-is sin (1 Corinthians 6:9-11; Hebrews 13:4). We are called to speak truth in love, offering the grace of the Gospel to all who repent (John 8:11).
theology.categoryIntro: "So God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him; male and female he created them." — Genesis 1:27
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of humanity — created in God's image, fallen, and redeemable

theology.content: I believe that all people are made in the image of God and are of equal value before Him (Acts 17:26; Galatians 3:28). The Gospel transcends race, ethnicity, and culture. By design, the church reflects this unity, displaying love and reconciliation among all people. I reject racism, partiality and any ideology that elevates one group over another (James 2:1-9). Christ must be first in all things. (Colossians 1:18)
theology.categoryIntro: "So God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him; male and female he created them." — Genesis 1:27
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of humanity — created in God's image, fallen, and redeemable

theology.categoryIntro: "And I tell you, you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it." — Matthew 16:18
theology.content: I believe the Church is the Body and Bride of Christ (Ephesians 5:23-27), comprised of all who have trusted in Him from Pentecost to the Rapture. Believers are called to fellowship in local assemblies under the Lordship of Christ and the guidance of biblically qualified elders (1 Peter 5:1-4; 1 Timothy 3; Colossians 1:18). The Church exists to worship Christ, teach the Word, observe the ordinances, make disciples, and proclaim the Gospel (Acts 2:42-47; Matthew 28:18-20). Each member is gifted by the Spirit for the building up of the Body in truth, unity, and love (Romans 12:4-8; 1 Peter 2:9).
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of the Church — her nature, mission, and ordinances

theology.content: I believe that Scripture calls elders and deacons to be men of integrity, faithful in character, and above reproach (1 Timothy 3:1-13; Titus 1:5-9). The phrase “husband of one wife” refers to a man’s present character - his devotion and moral faithfulness - not simply his marital history.While divorce is always the result of sin, it is not always disqualifying.If a divorce occurred on biblical grounds (Matthew 19:9; 1 Corinthians 7:15), and the man now lives a life marked by repentance, restoration, and spiritual maturity, he may serve in leadership. We are called to restore one another in a spirit of gentleness and with recognition that we all suffer from the same condition. Sin. (Galatians 6)Christ redeems brokenness and raises up shepherds from unlikely places, for His glory and the good of His Church. As one who has walked through the valley of divorce and come out clinging more closely to the grace of God, I stand not on my own perfection but on the sufficiency of Christ’s perfection. In other words, I stand on His qualifications, not my own.
theology.categoryIntro: "And I tell you, you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it." — Matthew 16:18
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of the Church — her nature, mission, and ordinances

theology.content: I affirm two ordinances given to the Church: Baptism and Communion. Baptism by immersion is a public testimony of faith and union with Christ (Romans 6:3-11). The Communion is a solemn reminder of Christ's death and a proclamation of His return (1 Corinthians 11:23-26). Neither ordinance imparts grace but both are acts of obedience and worship when practiced in such a way.
theology.categoryIntro: "And I tell you, you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it." — Matthew 16:18
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of the Church — her nature, mission, and ordinances

theology.content: I believe that men and women are created equal in value and dignity, both bearing the image of God (Genesis 1:27), and both essential in the life and mission of the Church. Women are called to serve, teach, and disciple other women and children (Titus 2:3-5), to labor in ministry (Romans 16:1-2), and to use their gifts for the building up of the Body of Christ (1 Corinthians 12:7).However, I also affirm that God has assigned distinct and complementary roles to men and women in the home and in the church. Scripture clearly teaches that the office of elder, overseer, or pastor is reserved for qualified men (1 Timothy 2:11-14; 1 Timothy 3:1-7; Titus 1:5-9). The pastoral role includes the authoritative preaching and teaching of God’s Word and the spiritual oversight of the Church-functions that are explicitly tied to male leadership in the New Testament.This is not a matter of cultural preference nor tradition, but one of biblical design, rooted in the created order (1 Timothy 2:13), not merely in first-century culture. Women serving as pastors, elders, or spiritual authorities over men contradicts the clear pattern of Scripture and the qualifications established by the Holy Spirit.Women are cherished and vital members of Christ’s body, and their contributions to the mission of the Church are absolutely essential. But we must not go beyond what is written (1 Corinthians 4:6). Upholding biblical roles honors the wisdom of God and safeguards the health of the church.
theology.categoryIntro: "And I tell you, you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it." — Matthew 16:18
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of the Church — her nature, mission, and ordinances

theology.categoryIntro: "And I tell you, you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it." — Matthew 16:18
theology.content: I believe that the pulpit is a sacred place reserved for the preaching of the Word of God (2 Timothy 4:2; Nehemiah 8:8). It is not to be used to promote political parties, endorse candidates, or push partisan ideologies. The Church is not a political organization, but the pillar and foundation of the truth (1 Timothy 3:15).While the Word of God speaks to multiple moral issues that are politically charged in American culture such as: the sanctity of life, biblical marriage, human dignity, and religious freedom - our duty is to preach the whole counsel of God (Acts 20:27) with clarity, conviction, and compassion. We do not tailor the message of the Gospel to fit political narratives, nor do we avoid biblical truths out of fear of political offense.I believe that pastors are charged with equipping believers to live as faithful citizens (Romans 13:1-7; 1 Peter 2:13-17), and to engage the public square with truth and grace. Our ultimate allegiance is not to earthly kingdoms, or powers, but to the Lord Jesus Christ, whose Kingdom is not of this world (John 18:36). Though we are in the world, we are not of the world.
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of the Church — her nature, mission, and ordinances

theology.categoryIntro: "For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a cry of command, with the voice of an archangel, and with the sound of the trumpet of God." — 1 Thessalonians 4:16
theology.content: I believe in the personal, bodily, and imminent return of Jesus Christ to rapture His Church before the Tribulation (1 Thessalonians 4:13-18; John 14:1-3). After the Tribulation, Christ will return to establish His Millennial Kingdom on earth (Revelation 20:1-6; Zechariah 14:9). Following the final judgment (Revelation 20:11-15), believers will dwell with God forever in the new Heavens and new Earth (Revelation 21:1-4). The unsaved will face eternal, conscious punishment (Matthew 25:46).
theology.categorySubtitle: The doctrine of last things — Christ's return, judgment, and eternity

theology.content: In a world that redefines love by feelings and self-interest, Christian marriage stands as a countercultural testimony. It is a daily dying to self, a living sacrifice (Romans 12:1), and a bold declaration that real love is not about getting-but giving. Our marriages are not just for our happiness; they are for His glory.
theology.categoryIntro: "This mystery is profound, and I am saying that it refers to Christ and the church." — Ephesians 5:32
theology.categorySubtitle: God's covenant design for the lifelong union of husband and wife

theology.content:
I believe that every Christian marriage is a pulpit. Whether we realize it or not, we are preaching something with our union - either the faithful love of Christ or a distortion of it. My aim, as a husband, is to display the Gospel in how I love my wife, serve my home, and honor the Lord. Who is sufficient for these things? Christ alone.
"Let marriage be held in honor among all…" - Hebrews 13:4

To Him be the glory, in our homes and in the church. Amen.
theology.categoryIntro: "This mystery is profound, and I am saying that it refers to Christ and the church." — Ephesians 5:32
theology.categorySubtitle: God's covenant design for the lifelong union of husband and wife

theology.content: Marriage is the living view of the Gospel. In it, husbands portray the initiating, pursuing, covenant-keeping love of Christ; wives portray the receiving, honoring, and responsive love of the Church. Every act of forgiveness, every moment of reconciliation, every shared burden, and every act of self-denial in marriage is meant to proclaim this truth: Jesus never gives up on His Bride. When a husband loves in spite of flaws, and a wife honors in the midst of suffering, they are preaching the Gospel to a watching world.
theology.categoryIntro: "This mystery is profound, and I am saying that it refers to Christ and the church." — Ephesians 5:32
theology.categorySubtitle: God's covenant design for the lifelong union of husband and wife

theology.categoryIntro: "This mystery is profound, and I am saying that it refers to Christ and the church." — Ephesians 5:32
theology.content: Marriage is not built on convenience, compatibility, or mere emotional connection. It is built on a covenant. It is a sacred promise made before God, to be faithful in all seasons: “for better or worse, in sickness and in health.” This covenant echoes God's unbreakable love for His people: “I will never leave you nor forsake you” (Hebrews 13:5).Divorce grieves the heart of God (Malachi 2:16) because it mars the picture of His unchanging, covenantal love. Yet, even in brokenness, God can redeem, restore, and heal. I believe that His grace reaches the depths of every failure.
theology.categorySubtitle: God's covenant design for the lifelong union of husband and wife

theology.content:
"This mystery is profound, and I am saying that it refers to Christ and the church." - Ephesians 5:32
I believe that marriage is a sacred covenant, established by God in creation (Genesis 2:24), defined as the lifelong union of one man and one woman (Matthew 19:4-6), for the purpose of reflecting the glory of Christ’s love for His Bride-the Church. Marriage is not a cultural “thing” or a social contract; it is a divine photograph, etched into creation itself, of the eternal love between Jesus Christ and His redeemed people.
theology.categoryIntro: "This mystery is profound, and I am saying that it refers to Christ and the church." — Ephesians 5:32
theology.categorySubtitle: God's covenant design for the lifelong union of husband and wife

theology.content: In this covenant, the husband is called to love his wife as Christ loved the Church-sacrificially, faithfully, and unconditionally (Ephesians 5:25). He is not her master but her servant leader, who lays down his life for her spiritual flourishing. The wife is called to submit to her husband as the Church submits to Christ-not out of fear or inferiority, but out of reverence and trust in God’s good design (Ephesians 5:22-24). These roles are not oppressive but redemptive, revealing the beauty of complementarity and the unity of purpose within the Godhead itself (1 Corinthians 11:3).
theology.categoryIntro: "Husbands, love your wives, as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her." — Ephesians 5:25
theology.categorySubtitle: The covenant roles of husband and wife as a picture of Christ and the Church

theology.content: I believe that God has ordained civil government for the punishment of evil and the preservation of order (Romans 13:1-7). The Government rests upon Christ’s shoulders (Isaiah 9:6-8). However, when civil authority contradicts God’s Word, we must obey God rather than man (Acts 5:29). I affirm the believer’s right to freedom of conscience and religious expression, and I oppose any attempt to silence biblical truth through governmental coercion or social pressure.
theology.categoryIntro: "He has told you, O man, what is good; and what does the LORD require of you but to do justice, and to love kindness, and to walk humbly with your God?" — Micah 6:8
theology.categorySubtitle: Loving our neighbor and reflecting the justice of God in the world

theology.content: I believe that true religion includes caring for the poor, the orphaned, the widowed, and the oppressed (James 1:27; Isaiah 1:17). The Church must be active in mercy ministry while holding fast to the Gospel, remembering that Christ came to set the captives free and bind up the brokenhearted (Luke 4:18; Galatians 6:10).
theology.sectionTitle: 🤲 Care for the Poor and Oppressed
theology.categoryIntro: "He has told you, O man, what is good; and what does the LORD require of you but to do justice, and to love kindness, and to walk humbly with your God?" — Micah 6:8
theology.categorySubtitle: Loving our neighbor and reflecting the justice of God in the world

theology.categoryIntro: "Do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewal of your mind, that by testing you may discern what is the will of God." — Romans 12:2
theology.content: I believe that Christians are to walk in holiness, including how they use technology, entertainment, and media (Philippians 4:8; Ephesians 5:3-12). We are called to flee from pornography, deceitful content, and worldliness, and instead fill our minds with what honors Christ. Technology is a tool, not a master. Social media and television are two of the primary methods by which pornographic and deceitful content is shared at rapid pace.
theology.categorySubtitle: Walking in holiness and wisdom in every area of everyday life

quiz.question: On which day of creation did God create humanity?

Psalm 119, the longest chapter in the Bible, is an acrostic poem primarily focused on what theme?
quiz.optionC: The Word of God / The Law

quiz.question: According to Proverbs 1:7, what is the 'beginning of knowledge'?

According to Micah 5:2, where was the Messiah prophesied to be born?
What was the specific physical sign of the covenant God made with Abraham?

quiz.optionA: No one comes to the Father except through me.
quiz.question: In the Gospel of John, Jesus says 'I am the way, and the truth, and the life.' What follows this statement?
quiz.optionD: The kingdom of heaven is near.
quiz.optionC: Believe in me and be saved.

quiz.question: Which apostle betrayed Jesus for thirty pieces of silver?

quiz.optionD: Turning water into wine at Cana

quiz.question: Which son of Jacob was sold into slavery by his brothers and later became a ruler in Egypt?
To whom did Jesus say, 'You must be born again'?
In Acts 2, the Holy Spirit descends upon the disciples on the day of what Jewish festival?
Who was the first Christian martyr, stoned to death after preaching to the Sanhedrin?
On the road to which city was Saul (later Paul) converted after seeing a blinding vision of the resurrected Christ?

quiz.optionD: Who would replace Judas as the 12th Apostle
quiz.optionC: Which books belonged in the New Testament
quiz.optionB: Whether Gentile converts needed to be circumcised and follow the Law of Moses

quiz.question: According to conservative evangelical scholarship, who is the primary author of the book of Acts?
In Romans 3:23, Paul writes that 'all have sinned and...' what?
quiz.optionB: fall short of the glory of God.

quiz.question: According to Ephesians 2:8-9, by what are we saved?

quiz.optionD: That believers could live in unrestrained sin (antinomianism)
quiz.optionC: That faith must be supplemented by circumcision and works of the law
quiz.optionB: That there is no bodily resurrection

quiz.question: Which New Testament epistle contains the 'Hall of Faith' highlighting Old Testament saints?

quiz.question: In 1 Corinthians 15, Paul states that if Christ has not been raised, our faith is what?
