Who authored the book of Revelation while exiled on the island of Patmos?
In Revelation 20, a period of 1,000 years is mentioned where Christ reigns. What is this period called?
What theological term describes God's unmerited favor toward sinners?
Which biblical attribute of God signifies that He is all-knowing?
According to Protestant evangelical theology (Sola Scriptura), what is the ultimate, final, and infallible authority for faith and practice?
What is the theological doctrine that there is one God who eternally exists in three distinct persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit?
Which Old Testament book serves primarily as a manual for the Levitical priesthood regarding sacrifices and holiness?

quiz.optionA: Making a sinner completely sinless in this life
quiz.question: In Reformed theology, the doctrine of 'Justification' refers to what specific act of God?
quiz.optionC: Giving a sinner the ability to earn salvation
quiz.optionB: Declaring a sinner righteous based on the imputed righteousness of Christ

quiz.question: Who succeeded Moses as the leader of the Israelites and led them into the Promised Land?
During the period of the Judges, which leader defeated the Midianites with an army of only 300 men?
Who was the first king of the united kingdom of Israel?
Which prophet confronted King David regarding his sin with Bathsheba?

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the origin of sin, the fallen nature of humanity, and why redemption through Christ is necessary.
apologetics.answerContent: Sin is any failure to conform to God's holy character and law in thought, word, or deed.
apologetics.quoteText: Everyone who makes a practice of sinning also practices lawlessness; sin is lawlessness.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the origin of sin, the fallen nature of humanity, and why redemption through Christ is necessary.
apologetics.answerContent: Scripture teaches that humanity is fallen and corrupted by sin, unable to save itself.
apologetics.quoteText: None is righteous, no, not one.

apologetics.answerContent: Through Adam's disobedience, sin entered the world and brought death to all humanity.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the origin of sin, the fallen nature of humanity, and why redemption through Christ is necessary.
apologetics.quoteText: Therefore, just as sin came into the world through one man, and death through sin.

apologetics.answerContent: Human nature is corrupted by sin in every aspect, leaving people spiritually unable to seek God apart from grace.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the origin of sin, the fallen nature of humanity, and why redemption through Christ is necessary.
apologetics.quoteText: No one understands; no one seeks for God.

apologetics.answerContent: Sin flows from the human heart, which is naturally inclined toward rebellion against God.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the origin of sin, the fallen nature of humanity, and why redemption through Christ is necessary.
apologetics.quoteText: The heart is deceitful above all things, and desperately sick.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the origin of sin, the fallen nature of humanity, and why redemption through Christ is necessary.
apologetics.answerContent: The just consequence of sin is death—both spiritual separation and eternal judgment.
apologetics.questionTitle: 61. What is the penalty for sin?
apologetics.quoteText: For the wages of sin is death.

apologetics.questionTitle: 62. Can people fix their own sin problem?
apologetics.answerContent: No amount of moral effort or religious activity can remove guilt before a holy God.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the origin of sin, the fallen nature of humanity, and why redemption through Christ is necessary.
apologetics.quoteText: By works of the law no human being will be justified in his sight.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the origin of sin, the fallen nature of humanity, and why redemption through Christ is necessary.
apologetics.questionTitle: 63. Why does sin separate us from God?
apologetics.answerContent: God's holiness cannot coexist with sin, so rebellion creates separation between humanity and God.
apologetics.quoteText: Your iniquities have made a separation between you and your God.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the origin of sin, the fallen nature of humanity, and why redemption through Christ is necessary.
apologetics.answerContent: Temptation is the enticement to sin that arises from our desires and the influence of the world and Satan.
apologetics.quoteText: Each person is tempted when he is lured and enticed by his own desire.

apologetics.answerContent: Yes. Through Jesus Christ, sinners can be forgiven, justified, and restored to God.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the origin of sin, the fallen nature of humanity, and why redemption through Christ is necessary.
apologetics.quoteText: But where sin increased, grace abounded all the more.

apologetics.answerContent: The church exists to glorify God by making disciples of all nations through the preaching of the gospel.
apologetics.questionTitle: 76. What is the mission of the church?
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the purpose of the church, the mission of believers in the world, and the call to proclaim the gospel to all nations.
apologetics.quoteText: Go therefore and make disciples of all nations.

apologetics.answerContent: Evangelism is the proclamation of the good news of Jesus Christ, calling people to repentance and faith.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the purpose of the church, the mission of believers in the world, and the call to proclaim the gospel to all nations.
apologetics.quoteText: How are they to believe in him of whom they have never heard?

apologetics.answerContent: Discipleship is the process of helping believers grow in obedience, knowledge, and Christlikeness.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the purpose of the church, the mission of believers in the world, and the call to proclaim the gospel to all nations.
apologetics.quoteText: Teaching them to observe all that I have commanded you.

apologetics.answerContent: God appoints elders and leaders to shepherd the church through teaching, oversight, and care.
apologetics.questionTitle: 79. Why does the church have leaders?
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the purpose of the church, the mission of believers in the world, and the call to proclaim the gospel to all nations.
apologetics.quoteText: Shepherd the flock of God that is among you.

apologetics.answerContent: Spiritual gifts are abilities given by the Holy Spirit to build up the church and serve others.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the purpose of the church, the mission of believers in the world, and the call to proclaim the gospel to all nations.
apologetics.quoteText: To each is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the common good.

apologetics.answerContent: Believers serve others because Christ first served us and calls us to love sacrificially.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the purpose of the church, the mission of believers in the world, and the call to proclaim the gospel to all nations.
apologetics.quoteText: The Son of Man came not to be served but to serve.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the purpose of the church, the mission of believers in the world, and the call to proclaim the gospel to all nations.
apologetics.answerContent: Church discipline lovingly corrects sin in order to restore believers and protect the church's purity.
apologetics.quoteText: If your brother sins against you, go and tell him his fault.

apologetics.answerContent: Giving supports the work of the gospel and expresses gratitude for God's provision.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the purpose of the church, the mission of believers in the world, and the call to proclaim the gospel to all nations.
apologetics.quoteText: Each one must give as he has decided in his heart.

apologetics.answerContent: Fellowship is the shared life of believers who encourage, strengthen, and care for one another in Christ.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the purpose of the church, the mission of believers in the world, and the call to proclaim the gospel to all nations.
apologetics.quoteText: They devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and the fellowship.

apologetics.answerContent: Missions exists because many people have not yet heard the gospel of Jesus Christ.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the purpose of the church, the mission of believers in the world, and the call to proclaim the gospel to all nations.
apologetics.quoteText: How beautiful are the feet of those who preach the good news.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Addressing the exclusive claims of Jesus Christ, the nature of grace, and the requirements for eternal salvation.
apologetics.answerContent: Yes, Jesus physically rose from the dead, conquering sin and death.
apologetics.quoteText: For by grace you have been saved through faith. And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God, not a result of works, so that no one may boast.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Addressing the exclusive claims of Jesus Christ, the nature of grace, and the requirements for eternal salvation.
apologetics.answerContent: Christ died as a substitute, bearing the penalty for sin so that sinners might be reconciled to God.
apologetics.questionTitle: 31. Why did Jesus have to die?
apologetics.quoteText: He himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we might die to sin and live to righteousness. By his wounds you have been healed.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Addressing the exclusive claims of Jesus Christ, the nature of grace, and the requirements for eternal salvation.
apologetics.answerContent: Grace is God's unearned favor shown to sinners who deserve judgment.
apologetics.quoteText: For from his fullness we have all received, grace upon grace.

apologetics.questionTitle: 33. What does it mean to repent?
apologetics.answerContent: Repentance is a Spirit-wrought turning from sin toward God in humility and faith.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Addressing the exclusive claims of Jesus Christ, the nature of grace, and the requirements for eternal salvation.
apologetics.quoteText: Repent therefore, and turn back, that your sins may be blotted out.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Addressing the exclusive claims of Jesus Christ, the nature of grace, and the requirements for eternal salvation.
apologetics.answerContent: Justification is God's legal declaration that a sinner is righteous because of Christ's righteousness credited to them.
apologetics.quoteText: Therefore, since we have been justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Addressing the exclusive claims of Jesus Christ, the nature of grace, and the requirements for eternal salvation.
apologetics.answerContent: Regeneration is the supernatural work of the Holy Spirit giving new spiritual life to a sinner.
apologetics.quoteText: Jesus answered him, “Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born again he cannot see the kingdom of God.”

apologetics.categoryIntro: Addressing the exclusive claims of Jesus Christ, the nature of grace, and the requirements for eternal salvation.
apologetics.answerContent: Only He is the God-man who could pay an infinite debt to an infinite God.
apologetics.questionTitle: 6. Why is Jesus the only way?
apologetics.quoteText: Jesus said to him, “I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.”

apologetics.categoryIntro: Addressing the exclusive claims of Jesus Christ, the nature of grace, and the requirements for eternal salvation.
apologetics.questionTitle: 7. Can I be saved by works?
apologetics.answerContent: No. Salvation is a gift of grace received through faith alone.
apologetics.quoteText: For by grace you have been saved through faith. And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God, not a result of works, so that no one may boast.

apologetics.answerContent: Yes. He is the eternal Word made flesh, fully God and fully man.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Addressing the exclusive claims of Jesus Christ, the nature of grace, and the requirements for eternal salvation.
apologetics.quoteText: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Addressing the exclusive claims of Jesus Christ, the nature of grace, and the requirements for eternal salvation.
apologetics.answerContent: Repentance toward God and faith in our Lord Jesus Christ.
apologetics.quoteText: because, if you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

apologetics.answerContent: God will judge every person according to His righteousness, revealing both justice and mercy.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the final destiny of humanity, including judgment, heaven, hell, resurrection, and the restoration of creation.
apologetics.quoteText: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the final destiny of humanity, including judgment, heaven, hell, resurrection, and the restoration of creation.
apologetics.answerContent: Heaven is the eternal dwelling place of God where believers experience perfect joy and fellowship with Him.
apologetics.quoteText: In my Father's house are many rooms.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the final destiny of humanity, including judgment, heaven, hell, resurrection, and the restoration of creation.
apologetics.answerContent: Hell is the place of eternal punishment where God's justice is revealed against unrepentant sin.
apologetics.quoteText: These will go away into eternal punishment.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the final destiny of humanity, including judgment, heaven, hell, resurrection, and the restoration of creation.
apologetics.answerContent: The resurrection is the future event when all people will be raised bodily to eternal life or judgment.
apologetics.quoteText: The hour is coming when all who are in the tombs will hear his voice.

apologetics.answerContent: The Book of Life contains the names of those who belong to Christ and will inherit eternal life.
apologetics.questionTitle: 90. What is the Book of Life?
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the final destiny of humanity, including judgment, heaven, hell, resurrection, and the restoration of creation.
apologetics.quoteText: Anyone whose name was not found written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the final destiny of humanity, including judgment, heaven, hell, resurrection, and the restoration of creation.
apologetics.answerContent: God graciously rewards the faithful service of believers in His kingdom.
apologetics.quoteText: Each will receive his wages according to his labor.

apologetics.answerContent: God will renew creation so that righteousness dwells in a restored and perfected world.
apologetics.questionTitle: 92. What is the new heaven and new earth?
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the final destiny of humanity, including judgment, heaven, hell, resurrection, and the restoration of creation.
apologetics.quoteText: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth.

apologetics.answerContent: In the new creation God will remove every sorrow, pain, and death forever.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the final destiny of humanity, including judgment, heaven, hell, resurrection, and the restoration of creation.
apologetics.quoteText: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the final destiny of humanity, including judgment, heaven, hell, resurrection, and the restoration of creation.
apologetics.answerContent: Believers will enjoy God's presence forever in worship, joy, and service.
apologetics.quoteText: His servants will worship him.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the final destiny of humanity, including judgment, heaven, hell, resurrection, and the restoration of creation.
apologetics.questionTitle: 95. What is the ultimate hope of Christians?
apologetics.answerContent: The ultimate hope of believers is the return of Jesus Christ and the full restoration of all things.
apologetics.quoteText: Waiting for our blessed hope, the appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior Jesus Christ.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how believers live in response to the gospel through obedience, perseverance, and daily transformation.
apologetics.answerContent: Believers manage their time, resources, and gifts faithfully as servants of God.
apologetics.quoteText: It is required of stewards that they be found faithful.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how believers live in response to the gospel through obedience, perseverance, and daily transformation.
apologetics.answerContent: Christians are called to treat others with the same mercy and kindness they desire.
apologetics.quoteText: Whatever you wish that others would do to you, do also to them.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how believers live in response to the gospel through obedience, perseverance, and daily transformation.
apologetics.answerContent: Believers forgive because God has forgiven them through Christ.
apologetics.quoteText: Forgiving one another, as God in Christ forgave you.

apologetics.answerContent: Humility is recognizing God's authority and valuing others above ourselves.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how believers live in response to the gospel through obedience, perseverance, and daily transformation.
apologetics.quoteText: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will exalt you.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how believers live in response to the gospel through obedience, perseverance, and daily transformation.
apologetics.answerContent: Believers seek righteousness because they desire to reflect God's character.
apologetics.quoteText: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how believers live in response to the gospel through obedience, perseverance, and daily transformation.
apologetics.answerContent: The love of Christ compels believers to live for Him rather than themselves.
apologetics.quoteText: The love of Christ controls us.

apologetics.answerContent: Biblical wisdom is the ability to live rightly before God by applying His truth to life.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the virtues that shape the life of a believer as they grow into the likeness of Christ.
apologetics.quoteText: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom.

apologetics.answerContent: Patience is enduring hardship or delay without bitterness or complaint.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the virtues that shape the life of a believer as they grow into the likeness of Christ.
apologetics.quoteText: Be patient, therefore, brothers, until the coming of the Lord.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the virtues that shape the life of a believer as they grow into the likeness of Christ.
apologetics.answerContent: Self-control is the Spirit-enabled ability to restrain sinful desires.
apologetics.quoteText: A person without self-control is like a city broken into and left without walls.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the virtues that shape the life of a believer as they grow into the likeness of Christ.
apologetics.answerContent: Contentment is trusting God’s provision regardless of circumstances.
apologetics.quoteText: I have learned in whatever situation I am to be content.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the virtues that shape the life of a believer as they grow into the likeness of Christ.
apologetics.answerContent: Courage is steadfast confidence in God even in the face of fear or danger.
apologetics.quoteText: Be strong and courageous. Do not be frightened.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the virtues that shape the life of a believer as they grow into the likeness of Christ.
apologetics.answerContent: Gentleness reflects Christ’s humble strength in dealing with others.
apologetics.quoteText: A soft answer turns away wrath.

apologetics.answerContent: Kindness is actively seeking the good of others through compassion and generosity.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the virtues that shape the life of a believer as they grow into the likeness of Christ.
apologetics.quoteText: Be kind to one another.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the virtues that shape the life of a believer as they grow into the likeness of Christ.
apologetics.answerContent: Faithfulness means remaining loyal to God and steadfast in obedience.
apologetics.quoteText: Well done, good and faithful servant.

apologetics.answerContent: Integrity is living with honesty and consistency before God and people.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the virtues that shape the life of a believer as they grow into the likeness of Christ.
apologetics.quoteText: Whoever walks in integrity walks securely.

apologetics.answerContent: Gratitude is giving thanks to God in all circumstances.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the virtues that shape the life of a believer as they grow into the likeness of Christ.
apologetics.quoteText: Give thanks in all circumstances.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how believers live in response to the gospel through obedience, perseverance, and daily transformation.
apologetics.answerContent: Obedience is the loving response of believers to God's grace and commands.
apologetics.quoteText: If you love me, you will keep my commandments.

apologetics.answerContent: True believers continue in faith because God preserves them through trials and struggles.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how believers live in response to the gospel through obedience, perseverance, and daily transformation.
apologetics.quoteText: The one who endures to the end will be saved.

apologetics.answerContent: Suffering refines faith and helps believers share in Christ's sufferings.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how believers live in response to the gospel through obedience, perseverance, and daily transformation.
apologetics.quoteText: Rejoice insofar as you share Christ's sufferings.

apologetics.answerContent: Spiritual growth is the ongoing transformation of believers as they mature in faith and godliness.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how believers live in response to the gospel through obedience, perseverance, and daily transformation.
apologetics.quoteText: Grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the nature of God, His sovereign rule over creation, the problem of evil, and divine election.
apologetics.answerContent: God is not the author of evil, but He ordains its existence for His ultimate glory and the display of His justice and mercy.
apologetics.questionTitle: 1. If God is good, why is there evil?
apologetics.quoteText: As for you, you meant evil against me, but God meant it for good...

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the nature of God, His sovereign rule over creation, the problem of evil, and divine election.
apologetics.questionTitle: 2. How can a loving God send someone to Hell?
apologetics.answerContent: God’s love does not negate His holiness. He is a perfectly just Judge who must punish rebellion against His infinite glory.
apologetics.quoteText: They will suffer the punishment of eternal destruction, away from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of his might.

apologetics.answerContent: God sovereignly governs all things according to His eternal purpose while creatures remain morally responsible.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the nature of God, His sovereign rule over creation, the problem of evil, and divine election.
apologetics.quoteText: For by grace you have been saved through faith. And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God, not a result of works, so that no one may boast.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the nature of God, His sovereign rule over creation, the problem of evil, and divine election.
apologetics.answerContent: God uses suffering to refine faith, expose idols, and conform believers into the image of Christ.
apologetics.quoteText: For this light momentary affliction is preparing for us an eternal weight of glory beyond all comparison.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the nature of God, His sovereign rule over creation, the problem of evil, and divine election.
apologetics.answerContent: God does not change in His nature or eternal decree, though He interacts with humanity in real history.
apologetics.quoteText: For I the Lord do not change; therefore you, O children of Jacob, are not consumed.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the nature of God, His sovereign rule over creation, the problem of evil, and divine election.
apologetics.answerContent: God perfectly knows all things—past, present, and future—because He ordains history according to His will.
apologetics.quoteText: I'm still learning and can't help with that. Do you need help with anything else?

apologetics.questionTitle: 3. Is God the author of sin?
apologetics.answerContent: No. Sin arises from the creature's heart. God is light and in Him is no darkness.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the nature of God, His sovereign rule over creation, the problem of evil, and divine election.
apologetics.quoteText: Let no one say when he is tempted, “I am being tempted by God,” for God cannot be tempted with evil, and he himself tempts no one.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the nature of God, His sovereign rule over creation, the problem of evil, and divine election.
apologetics.answerContent: God's holiness requires that evil be judged; justice is the necessary expression of His righteous character.
apologetics.quoteText: The Rock, his work is perfect, for all his ways are justice. A God of faithfulness and without iniquity, just and upright is he.

apologetics.answerContent: Yes, election is the sovereign act of God choosing individuals for salvation before the foundation of the world.
apologetics.questionTitle: 4. Does God choose who is saved?
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the nature of God, His sovereign rule over creation, the problem of evil, and divine election.
apologetics.quoteText: even as he chose us in him before the foundation of the world, that we should be holy and blameless before him. In love.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring the nature of God, His sovereign rule over creation, the problem of evil, and divine election.
apologetics.questionTitle: 5. Why did God create the tree in Eden?
apologetics.answerContent: To provide a test of obedience and to demonstrate the beauty of holiness through the contrast of human choice.
apologetics.quoteText: And the LORD God commanded the man, saying, "You may surely eat of every tree of the garden, but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat, for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die."

apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.
apologetics.answerContent: Yes, it is God-breathed and the final authority on all it addresses.
apologetics.quoteText: All Scripture is breathed out by God and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.
apologetics.answerContent: In its original manuscripts, the Bible is inerrant and infallible.
apologetics.quoteText: The words of the LORD are pure words, like silver refined in a furnace on the ground, purified seven times.

apologetics.answerContent: The Old points forward to Christ; the New reveals Christ and His fulfillment of the law.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.
apologetics.quoteText: Do not think that I have come to abolish the Law or the Prophets; I have not come to abolish them but to fulfill them.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.
apologetics.answerContent: Yes, it contains everything necessary for life and godliness.
apologetics.quoteText: His divine power has granted to us all things that pertain to life and godliness, through the knowledge of him who called us to his own glory and excellence.

apologetics.answerContent: Literally, historically, and grammatically, letting Scripture interpret Scripture.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.

apologetics.answerContent: The Bible was written by human authors under the inspiration of the Holy Spirit, so that their words are truly God's Word.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.
apologetics.quoteText: For no prophecy was ever produced by the will of man, but men spoke from God as they were carried along by the Holy Spirit.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.
apologetics.answerContent: Inspiration means that Scripture originates from God Himself, though written through human authors.
apologetics.quoteText: All Scripture is breathed out by God and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness,

apologetics.answerContent: The Bible demonstrates divine authority through fulfilled prophecy, historical reliability, and the testimony of Christ.
apologetics.questionTitle: 38. Why should we trust the Bible?
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.
apologetics.quoteText: The sum of your word is truth, and every one of your righteous rules endures forever.

apologetics.answerContent: The canon was recognized by the early church as those writings that were apostolic, consistent with truth, and widely received.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.
apologetics.quoteText: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me.

apologetics.answerContent: Faithful translations accurately convey the meaning of the original Hebrew, Aramaic, and Greek texts.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.
apologetics.quoteText: The grass withers, the flower fades, but the word of our God will stand forever.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.
apologetics.answerContent: Scripture renews the mind, reveals God's character, and equips believers for righteous living.
apologetics.quoteText: Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.
apologetics.answerContent: The Bible stands as the final and supreme authority in matters of faith, doctrine, and life.
apologetics.quoteText: but the word of the Lord remains forever. And this word is the good news that was preached to you.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.
apologetics.answerContent: Because God authored the whole Bible, its clearer passages help interpret more difficult ones.
apologetics.quoteText: knowing this first of all, that no prophecy of Scripture comes from someone's own interpretation.

apologetics.answerContent: Hiding God's Word in the heart guards believers against sin and strengthens faith.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.
apologetics.quoteText: I have stored up your word in my heart, that I might not sin against you.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Understanding the inspiration, infallibility, sufficiency, and correct interpretation of the biblical texts.
apologetics.answerContent: The Word of God is living and active, transforming hearts through the work of the Spirit.
apologetics.quoteText: For the word of God is living and active, sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing to the division of soul and of spirit, of joints and of marrow, and discerning the thoughts and intentions of the heart.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how God rescues sinners through the work of Christ, including redemption, reconciliation, and the transforming work of the Holy Spirit.
apologetics.answerContent: Redemption is Christ purchasing sinners out of slavery to sin through the payment of His own blood.
apologetics.quoteText: In him we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of our trespasses.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how God rescues sinners through the work of Christ, including redemption, reconciliation, and the transforming work of the Holy Spirit.
apologetics.answerContent: Through Christ's sacrifice, enemies of God are restored into a relationship of peace with Him.
apologetics.quoteText: While we were enemies we were reconciled to God by the death of his Son.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how God rescues sinners through the work of Christ, including redemption, reconciliation, and the transforming work of the Holy Spirit.
apologetics.answerContent: Believers are graciously brought into God's family and given the full rights of sons and daughters.
apologetics.quoteText: You have received the Spirit of adoption as sons, by whom we cry, “Abba! Father!”

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how God rescues sinners through the work of Christ, including redemption, reconciliation, and the transforming work of the Holy Spirit.
apologetics.answerContent: The perfect righteousness of Christ is credited to believers so they stand justified before God.
apologetics.quoteText: For our sake he made him to be sin who knew no sin.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how God rescues sinners through the work of Christ, including redemption, reconciliation, and the transforming work of the Holy Spirit.
apologetics.answerContent: God promises a new relationship where sins are forgiven and hearts are transformed by His Spirit.
apologetics.quoteText: I will forgive their iniquity, and I will remember their sin no more.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how God rescues sinners through the work of Christ, including redemption, reconciliation, and the transforming work of the Holy Spirit.
apologetics.answerContent: The Holy Spirit is the third Person of the Trinity who applies the work of salvation to believers.
apologetics.quoteText: The Helper, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how God rescues sinners through the work of Christ, including redemption, reconciliation, and the transforming work of the Holy Spirit.
apologetics.answerContent: The Spirit convicts of sin, regenerates the heart, indwells believers, and produces spiritual fruit.
apologetics.questionTitle: 72. What does the Holy Spirit do?
apologetics.quoteText: He will convict the world concerning sin and righteousness and judgment.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how God rescues sinners through the work of Christ, including redemption, reconciliation, and the transforming work of the Holy Spirit.
apologetics.answerContent: Believers are spiritually united to Christ so that His life, righteousness, and inheritance become theirs.
apologetics.quoteText: I have been crucified with Christ. It is no longer I who live, but Christ who lives in me.

apologetics.answerContent: The Holy Spirit produces Christlike character in believers such as love, joy, peace, and self-control.
apologetics.questionTitle: 74. What are the fruits of the Spirit?
apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how God rescues sinners through the work of Christ, including redemption, reconciliation, and the transforming work of the Holy Spirit.
apologetics.quoteText: The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Exploring how God rescues sinners through the work of Christ, including redemption, reconciliation, and the transforming work of the Holy Spirit.
apologetics.answerContent: The final stage of salvation when believers are fully transformed and freed from sin forever.
apologetics.quoteText: Those whom he justified he also glorified.

apologetics.answerContent: No. True believers are kept by the power of God for eternity.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.quoteText: I give them eternal life, and they will never perish, and no one will snatch them out of my hand.

apologetics.answerContent: No. Faith is objective trust based on the truth of God's Word.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.quoteText: Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the conviction of things not seen.

apologetics.answerContent: All are guilty through creation and conscience; none are innocent seekers of God.
apologetics.questionTitle: 18. What about those who never hear?
apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.quoteText: For his invisible attributes, namely, his eternal power and divine nature, have been clearly perceived, ever since the creation of the world, in the things that have been made. So they are without excuse.

apologetics.answerContent: The visible church contains both "wheat and tares"—true and false believers.
apologetics.questionTitle: 19. Why is there hypocrisy in church?
apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.quoteText: Let both grow together until the harvest, and at harvest time I will tell the reapers, "Gather the weeds first and bind them in bundles to be burned, but gather the wheat into my barn."

apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.answerContent: God is one in essence, eternally existing in three Persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.
apologetics.quoteText: Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.

apologetics.answerContent: Final and decisive rejection of the Holy Spirit’s testimony of Christ.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.quoteText: Truly, I say to you, all sins will be forgiven the children of man, and whatever blasphemies they utter, but whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin.

apologetics.answerContent: God can do anything, but the specific apostolic sign-gifts have ceased.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.quoteText: The signs of a true apostle were performed among you with utmost patience, with signs and wonders and mighty works.

apologetics.answerContent: The event where Christ gathers His church to meet Him in the air.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.quoteText: Then we who are alive, who are left, will be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air, and so we will always be with the Lord.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.answerContent: Believers are present with the Lord; unbelievers await judgment in torment.
apologetics.quoteText: We are of good courage, and we would rather be away from the body and at home with the Lord.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.answerContent: Yes, Christ will return in glory to judge the world and establish His kingdom.
apologetics.quoteText: He who testifies to these things says, “Surely I am coming soon.” Amen. Come, Lord Jesus!

apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.answerContent: Process
apologetics.quoteText: For this is the will of God, your sanctification.

apologetics.answerContent: Prayer is communion with God where believers express dependence, worship, confession, and petition.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.quoteText: pray without ceasing,

apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.answerContent: The church is the gathered body of believers united to Christ and one another for worship, teaching, fellowship, and mission.
apologetics.quoteText: And he is the head of the body, the church. He is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, that in everything he might be preeminent.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.answerContent: God calls believers to assemble regularly for encouragement, worship, and instruction in truth.
apologetics.quoteText: not neglecting to meet together, as is the habit of some, but encouraging one another, and all the more as you see the Day drawing near.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.answerContent: Baptism is the public sign of union with Christ in His death and resurrection.
apologetics.quoteText: We were buried therefore with him by baptism into death.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.answerContent: The Lord's Supper is a memorial meal where believers remember Christ's sacrifice and proclaim His death until He comes.
apologetics.quoteText: Do this in remembrance of me.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.answerContent: Believers face real spiritual opposition and must stand firm through faith, truth, and the armor of God.
apologetics.quoteText: Put on the whole armor of God.

apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.answerContent: Believers confess sin, repent, and rely on God's grace for forgiveness and transformation.
apologetics.questionTitle: 53. How should Christians deal with sin?
apologetics.quoteText: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us.

apologetics.answerContent: Biblical love seeks the good of others sacrificially, reflecting the love of Christ.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.quoteText: By this all people will know that you are my disciples, if you have love for one another.

apologetics.answerContent: God calls believers to holiness because they belong to Him and reflect His character.
apologetics.categoryIntro: Practical questions regarding eternal security, the nature of faith, church dynamics, and end-times events.
apologetics.quoteText: Be holy, for I am holy.

missionsRegistry.owSummary: Stability in Africa’s most failed state remains depressingly elusive. Somalia now has a parliament and recognized national leaders after 20 years without an effective central government. But the militant Islamist group Al-Shabaab (linked to Al-Qaeda) still controls much of southern and central Somalia, and continues to hold influence in rural areas. Although it no longer contests control of Mogadishu, it continues murderous bombing attacks there, and in neighboring countries. It even battles against the Islamic State for “terrorist primacy” in the Puntland area. Pray that Somalia’s rulers might learn from the past, govern for the good of the people, and respect human rights and religious freedom. Pray that those deceived by the enemy into committing terrorism might encounter Jesus who loves them and died on their behalf.
owPrayerChallenges: False notions of Christianity. Pray that the association of Christianity with Western decadence, European colonialism and Ethiopian interference would be broken.

Practical demonstrations of Christ’s love through aid and mercy ministry must be done in the right manner. Pray that refugees and recipients of help from Christians would be touched and would respond.

Nomadic pastoralism and the tightly bound clan structures of Somali society are perceived to be incompatible with Christianity; 60-70% of Somalis are at least semi-nomadic and 95% are tied into clans.

Pray that those deceived by the enemy into committing terrorism might encounter Jesus who loves them and died on their behalf.
A negative prejudice against Christianity must be overcome if the community of faith is ever to make a greater breakthrough. These issues include:
missionsRegistry.bibleShortageNeed: Bible needs estimate is less than ten thousand

missionsRegistry.owSummary: The re-conquest of Afghanistan by the Taliban in 2021 came as a shock to many. These events led to the suspension of humanitarian aid to the needy people of Afghanistan, the reintroduction of much harsher Islamic laws, the flight of millions of Afghans from Taliban rule, the intensified persecution (and even manhunt) of Christians, and the emboldening of Islamist movements elsewhere against what they perceive as a weak and failing Western world. The destruction and aftermath of the 2023 earthquake, the gripping poverty and hunger, and the heavy hand of this Islamist regime also intensify the suffering. There is so much to pray for; above all, pray that God preserve the lives of Afghans so that they might hear the good news of Jesus.
owPrayerAnswers: The re-conquest of Afghanistan by the Taliban in 2021 came as a shock to many. These events led to the suspension of humanitarian aid to the needy people of Afghanistan, the reintroduction of much harsher Islamic laws, the flight of millions of Afghans from Taliban rule, the intensified persecution (and even manhunt) of Christians, and the emboldening of Islamist movements elsewhere against what they perceive as a weak and failing Western world. The destruction and aftermath of the 2023 earthquake, the gripping poverty and hunger, and the heavy hand of this Islamist regime also intensify the suffering. There is so much to pray for; above all, pray that God preserve the lives of Afghans so that they might hear the good news of Jesus.

The status of women deserves special prayer. Virtually all progress made from 2001-2021 has been undone by the Taliban since September 2021. Women are once again all but banned from participating in public life. Numerous women die in childbirth in Afghanistan because many cannot receive healthcare from male doctors. Women’s voices are forbidden to be heard in public, and they cannot travel without a male guardian present. One-third of women suffer from physical violence. Widows face very difficult circumstances, and suicide is common. Special radio programmes share with women from the Bible, about the love and value God holds for them. Female literacy had improved from 11% in 1979 to over 55% in 2018; perhaps this will enable more Afghan women to read material that will lead them to Jesus. Pray for justice and freedom from gender-based oppression, and especially that women might find the true freedom that comes only through Christ.
missionsRegistry.bibleShortageNeed: Bible needs estimate is less than ten thousand

missionsRegistry.owSummary: Amidst the terrible suffering, Yemenis are finding life in Christ through radio, Bible distribution, careful witness, and dreams and visions from the Lord! Believers meet secretly and only in small groups. They often face dangerous opposition. Praise God for these followers of Jesus. Pray for them as they learn how to honour their culture and family while faithfully serving the Lord. There may be more Yemeni followers of Christ than all the other indigenous groups in the Arabian Peninsula combined!
owPrayerAnswers: Amidst the terrible suffering, Yemenis are finding life in Christ through radio, Bible distribution, careful witness, and dreams and visions from the Lord! Believers meet secretly and only in small groups. They often face dangerous opposition. Praise God for these followers of Jesus. Pray for them as they learn how to honour their culture and family while faithfully serving the Lord. There may be more Yemeni followers of Christ than all the other indigenous groups in the Arabian Peninsula combined!

Yemen has suffered in recent decades from three civil wars, tribal conflicts, and war in nearby countries. Currently Yemen is divided between Shi’a Houthi rebels who control much of the country and Sunni forces supporting the ousted President. Radical Islamist groups like Al Qaeda and ISIS have seized on the chaos to take control of other areas. Neighbouring countries such as Saudi Arabia, U.A.E. and Iran have used Yemen as a theatre for their own proxy wars. Western governments, including the USA and UK, continue to sell arms that are used by their allies to indiscriminately take Yemeni lives. Many civilians have died in bombings, including people in schools, hospitals, and prisons. As a result of all this upheaval, much of the population has faced food shortages and even starvation in recent years. Few children can regularly go to school and fewer adults have a reliable income. May the God of peace and justice bring both to this land of great suffering.
missionsRegistry.bibleShortageNeed: Bible needs estimate is less than ten thousand

missionsRegistry.owSummary: North Korea today is like a nightmare. The state creates a cult around the young “Supreme Leader” (Kim Jong-un) and his dead grandfather (Kim Il-Sung), and does not allow the people to interact with the outside world. More than 3 million people have starved to death since 1994. Foreign charities can bring food, farming technology, training, and start business ventures, but are restricted in their activites and closely watched. Pray that help will reach the desperate, hungry people. Pray that in God’s timing a change would come to completely free and transform this land.
missionsRegistry.bibleShortageRange: Between fifty and one hundred thousand
owPrayerAnswers: North Korea today is like a nightmare. The state creates a cult around the young “Supreme Leader” (Kim Jong-un) and his dead grandfather (Kim Il-Sung), and does not allow the people to interact with the outside world. More than 3 million people have starved to death since 1994. Foreign charities can bring food, farming technology, training, and start business ventures, but are restricted in their activites and closely watched. Pray that help will reach the desperate, hungry people. Pray that in God’s timing a change would come to completely free and transform this land.

The Korean revival (1907) began in the Church in North Korea! People in those days called Pyongyang the “Jerusalem of the East”. But most Christians fled to the South during the Korean War, or died as martyrs. Now if you even say the name “Jesus” aloud you may die for it. We do not know much about the underground Church, but we know it survived and even grows. The government holds up to 100,000 Christians in labour camps. Pray for North Korean believers to persevere in probably the most difficult country for Christians.

missionsRegistry.owSummary: Islam has been entrenched for 1,000 years with little challenge. Mauritanians see their nation as the most Islamic country in Africa. Many are the barriers to change – laws hindering proclamation of the gospel, powerful social resistance to change, an historic reluctance to engage with the outside world, geographical isolation, low literacy and minimal exposure to Christians and Christian media. The arrest of alleged converts to Christianity in 2023 highlights the hostility to even the notion of Mauritanians leaving Islam. Pray for greater spiritual openness and hunger for God. World Vision, Caritas, the Lutherans and others are working in development as well as with issues such as disease awareness and prevention, human rights and environmental protection.

Many are the barriers to change – laws hindering proclamation of the gospel, powerful social resistance to change, an historic reluctance to engage with the outside world, geographical isolation, low literacy and minimal exposure to Christians and Christian media. missionsRegistry.owSummary: Eritrea is located in a region fraught with conflict and crises. Many face extreme poverty with no relief ahead as drought and food shortages are serious challenges in this one-party state. Mandatory military service limits the size of the workforce. Many people flee the country to escape the military, and the money sent home by those who live abroad now provides a vital source of income for the country. Pray for the government to focus on caring and providing for its own people – it has a terrible record on human rights and press freedoms, accompanied by an obsession with military power and control.
missionsRegistry.bibleShortageRange: Between one hundred and two hundred fifty thousand
owPrayerAnswers: Eritrea is located in a region fraught with conflict and crises. Many face extreme poverty with no relief ahead as drought and food shortages are serious challenges in this one-party state. Mandatory military service limits the size of the workforce. Many people flee the country to escape the military, and the money sent home by those who live abroad now provides a vital source of income for the country. Pray for the government to focus on caring and providing for its own people – it has a terrible record on human rights and press freedoms, accompanied by an obsession with military power and control.

Religious freedom remains a major issue. A 2002 government ruling banned all religious groups from meeting together and practicing their faith without official recognition, and it granted recognition only to Sunni Islam, Eritrean Orthodox, Roman Catholic and Evangelical Lutheran groups. The effects of this on all Eritreans are significant, especially so on Christians within non-approved groups. Some posit this as a reaction against the evangelical growth within the Orthodox Church and during the long war with Ethiopia. Pray for government acceptance of religious groups and for restoration of basic human rights and religious freedom to all Eritreans.

missionsRegistry.owSummary: Qaddafi’s fall in 2011 led to nearly a decade of civil war between different factions. A permanent ceasefire was signed in 2020, but political wrangling between the factions have prevented elections. Libyans desire to see progress in their nation, but those in power – and foreign interests as well – continue to prefer conflict over reconciliation. Pray for a resolution to the issue of governance, for the rebuilding of infrastructure that enables communities to thrive, and most of all, for an opening of these lands and people to the gospel.
owPrayerAnswers: Qaddafi’s fall in 2011 led to nearly a decade of civil war between different factions. A permanent ceasefire was signed in 2020, but political wrangling between the factions have prevented elections. Libyans desire to see progress in their nation, but those in power – and foreign interests as well – continue to prefer conflict over reconciliation. Pray for a resolution to the issue of governance, for the rebuilding of infrastructure that enables communities to thrive, and most of all, for an opening of these lands and people to the gospel.

Large numbers of migrants come into Libya. Most come from Sub-Saharan Africa, but some come from North Africa and even parts of Asia. A few find work in Libya, but more cross the dangerous deserts and seas in search of a new life in Europe. In the last decade, around 1 million people have made the dangerous crossing to Italy. The current chaos in Libya gives freedom for traffickers to exploit these vulnerable people. Pray that these tens or even hundreds of thousands would find salvation, and not just earthly gain. Some of these migrants are believers; many others find Jesus in the midst of their turbulent lives. Pray that they might have a powerful spiritual impact on Libyans and on fellow migrants.
missionsRegistry.bibleShortageNeed: Bible needs estimate is less than ten thousand

missionsRegistry.owSummary: The growth of the Algerian Church over the past decade is an answer to prayer! Many years of hard work by missionaries and praying people produced beautiful fruit. Most believers come from a Kabyle Berber background, but faith grows among Arabs and most other people groups as well. The Christian community enjoys a spirit of unity, which stands out from the long history of conflict among ethnic groups. Some received supernatural visions of Jesus, but most came to Him through personal evangelism. New fellowships began throughout Algeria, partly because Berber believers moved into unreached Arab areas with the Gospel.
owPrayerAnswers: The growth of the Algerian Church over the past decade is an answer to prayer! Many years of hard work by missionaries and praying people produced beautiful fruit. Most believers come from a Kabyle Berber background, but faith grows among Arabs and most other people groups as well. The Christian community enjoys a spirit of unity, which stands out from the long history of conflict among ethnic groups. Some received supernatural visions of Jesus, but most came to Him through personal evangelism. New fellowships began throughout Algeria, partly because Berber believers moved into unreached Arab areas with the Gospel.

The Church has a very indigenous, truly Algerian way of expressing faith. Scriptures and study materials, worship styles, and even training and leadership reflect Algerian culture well. Algeria needs more workers to help strengthen the local church and to bring the gospel to the unreached millions, but access for missionaries is a real challenge. Several agencies reach out to Algerians through radio, literature, satellite TV, portable media, and Bible correspondence courses. Praise God for the increase of these resources, and pray for wider distribution! The pressure on churches has been increasing for a few years now, so it is very important to have Christian resources in languages and formats that will allow the persecuted church to continue to grow and spread.
missionsRegistry.bibleShortageNeed: Bible needs estimate is less than ten thousand

missionsRegistry.owSummary: Massive numbers of Iranians have come to Jesus in recent years! From only 500 Muslim-background believers in 1979, some estimates suggest the number is even greater than 1 million just in Iran alone. Large numbers of Persian people have also encountered the risen Christ outside of Iran. While hundreds of thousands of Iranians have indicated that they are Christian, only a fraction actively participate in church life. Even so, the Church in Persia has not grown this fast since the 7th century. In Iran, a person can receive a death sentence for apostasy (abandoning religious faith). Despite this, 50,000 mosques have closed in recent years as Iranians are disillusioned with both the regime and with Islam. This growth is a remarkable move of the Holy Spirit, with many signs and wonders, dreams and visions.
owPrayerChallenges: The Zoroastrians (Parsees) follow an ancient Persian religion founded 1,000, years before Christ.

The Baha’i religion started in Iran, but the government seeks to drive its followers out. Very little Christian love and witness to them exists among either the 300,000 in Iran, or the 5-7 million worldwide.
The nomadic and semi-nomadic Luri, Bakhtiari, and Qashqai live in the Zagros Mountains. Only a few dozen known believers exist from these groups. Persian Christians have begun to reach out to them.
The Turkic Azeri and Turkmen in the north have had almost no positive contact with Christianity. Azeris form the largest minority group within Iran.
The Gypsy communities have no Christian workers committed to outreach among them.

The Persian-speaking Jews descend from those exiled to Babylon 2,700 years ago. Their numbers decline as more and more move away to escape harassment, but a number have become active, witnessing Christians!

Despite this, 50,000 mosques have closed in recent years as Iranians are disillusioned with both the regime and with Islam. This growth is a remarkable move of the Holy Spirit, with many signs and wonders, dreams and visions.

The 1979 Islamic Revolution promised peace and prosperity, but more than 40 years later, we may be witnessing the collapse of the regime. Widespread protests in 2022 and 2026 demonstrated that the younger generation is fed up with the legacy of oppression, bloodshed, cruel ‘justice’, corruption, economic hardship, and cultural isolation from most of the world. Despite external religiosity, drug addiction, and prostitution are widespread. Iran is an ancient, noble, and proud civilization. But in the modern era, these struggles have made many people very open to the gospel. Many others are turning to atheism or to Iran’s ancient religion, Zoroastrianism. Pray that Iranians’ desires for greatness, prosperity, freedom, and even for righteousness might ultimately be met through worship of Jesus.

Iran contains some of the largest unreached, unengaged peoples in the world. Missions are not free to minister in Iran, but some tentmaking opportunities exist. Pray for the door to Iran to open in God’s perfect timing.

missionsRegistry.owSummary: Hostility against almost any foreign Christian activity or even presence has persisted for over 30 years. Almost every foreign Christian has been expelled. Several national pastors have been exiled, beaten, heavily fined or imprisoned. The twenty or so registered congregations continue to be intimidated and forbidden to meet. Registration is a difficult, near-impossible process, and when it does occur, it only subjects the church to greater surveillance. Pray for a softening of the attitude of the authorities, for courage for Christians in Turkmenistan to stand firm and for Christians outside of the country to pray and speak up against these hostile actions.

missionsRegistry.owSummary: Catastrophe is not too strong a word to describe Sudan’s current status. Massive protests in 2018 eventually led to the arrest of the President and the end of his rule, 30 years after he seized power in a military coup. A transitional military-civilian government was formed in 2019, but this brief hope for progress was undone by subsequent military coups. Now, civil war rages between the Sudan Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces which was formed from government-supported paramilitaries. The millions of Sudanese who want neither of these forces to rule them have been subjected to atrocities by both sides. Over 10 million Sudanese are displaced, and famine – especially in Darfur – reaches out to claim more lives. Pray that the legacy of oppressive, militaristic rule in Sudan might be ended in Jesus’ name. Pray for a government that will oversee a flourishing of the country – most importantly through the rapid spread of the gospel.
missionsRegistry.bibleShortageRange: Between two hundred fifty and five hundred thousand

The gospel spread through these many years of upheaval. Although the conflicts created terrible suffering, they made the Church more mature. The wars scattered Christian refugees throughout the country, so even through suffering the good news spread. Churches formed in places and among peoples that previously had no Christians! Praise God that this growth occurs across many denominations. Pray for them to find unity through Christ. The Islamic government bombed churches and other Christian buildings in the South, and specifically targeted Christian areas for attack. But many believers kept their faith, and even took the gospel to other ethnic groups during these hard times!

missionsRegistry.owSummary: Tourists see the Maldives as an island paradise, but a darker reality hides below the surface. Powerful social and official forces limit freedom of expression and belief. The Maldives has one of the highest divorce rates in the world. Crime rates and gang activity both continue to rise. Child abuse and teenage drug abuse (among up to 70% of teens) indicate deep problems. Islam is the only recognized religion, and the government forbids open practice of all other religions. But beyond Islamic beliefs, many people follow occult practices called fanditha. Pray for the light of the gospel to shine among Maldivians. Pray against strongholds of pride, fear, and selfish pursuit of physical pleasures.

missionsRegistry.owSummary: The vast majority of the population are Muslim. They were almost completely unevangelized before 1973. Islamic fundamentalism is on the rise. However, most are involved in occult practices through witchcraft, curses and spirit possession. Many young people – disillusioned with life in this society that offers so little hope – attempt to find solace in drugs, sex or the opportunity to leave the islands. Pray that they might have opportunities to hear the gospel of life that offers hope to all.

missionsRegistry.owSummary: The Arab Spring began in Tunisia (December 2010). The country had one of the most progressive and open societies in the Arab world, but Tunisians felt frustrated by high unemployment, corruption, political oppression, and poor living conditions. Protests led to government changes, and inspired similar protests across the whole Arab world. However, continued economic decline and unemployment, the increasing presence of Islamic fundamentalism, and a heavy-handed President (who rewrote the constitution to give himself more power) all serve to rob Tunisians of confidence in their country’s future. Pray that the travails of Tunisia might somehow draw people towards Christ.

missionsRegistry.owSummary: Although Islam is the religion of 94% of the population, only a small fraction practice “pure” Islam. Most are more influenced by folk superstitions and Zoroastrian beliefs. Mosques sprouted up everywhere in the years following independence, but now the government places severe restrictions on mosque building. Tajikistan’s proximity to Iran and Afghanistan makes it vulnerable to Islamism. Pray for extremism to be restrained, and that Muslims might have unprecedented opportunities to discover Christ.
